docs.title: Hardware Requirements
docs.text:
Component Minimum Recommended Large Networks (10k+ hosts) CPU Quad-core 2 GHz Quad-core 2 GHz+ Octa-core 2 GHz+ RAM 4 GB 8 GB 16 GB Disk 40 GB 80 GB 120 GB+ Network Stable connection Gigabit Ethernet Gigabit Ethernet

Disk space

The 40 GB minimum includes space for the PostgreSQL database, which grows with the number of vulnerabilities, hosts, and services. Large environments with millions of vulnerability records may require 80 GB or more for PostgreSQL data alone. Docker images add approximately 500 MB per Faraday image.

docs.title: Operating System
docs.text:
OS Supported Versions Notes Ubuntu 22.04 LTS, 24.04 LTS Recommended Debian 12 (Bookworm)+ Kali Linux Latest rolling RHEL 8, 9 CentOS Stream 8, 9 Rocky Linux 8, 9 AlmaLinux 8, 9

Architecture: 64-bit (amd64/x86_64) only.

End-of-life operating systems

Ubuntu 18.04, CentOS 7, and RHEL 7 are end-of-life and unsupported. Their default Python and PostgreSQL versions are below the Faraday minimum requirements.

docs.title: Required
docs.text:
Component Minimum Version Recommended Notes PostgreSQL 12 14+ Primary data store. Docker reference: postgres:12.7-alpine Redis 6.0 7.x Celery message broker. Docker reference: redis:8.4-alpine Python 3.9 3.11 Bare-metal installs only. Docker uses Python 3.11

End-of-life software

PostgreSQL 9.6–11, Python 3.7–3.8, and Redis 5.x are end-of-life. Faraday's dependencies (e.g., cryptography>=40.0.1, celery==5.4.0) may not install or function correctly on these versions.

docs.title: Docker installs
docs.text:
Component Minimum Version Docker Engine 20.10+ Docker Compose v2 (the docker compose CLI plugin)

Note

Docker installs bundle their own Python, PostgreSQL, and Redis — you do not need to install these separately.

docs.title: Optional
docs.text: Component Version Purpose Elasticsearch 7.16+ Advanced search capabilities (configured via faraday-manage settings) NGINX Any current Reverse proxy with SSL/TLS termination (Advanced setup) beautifulsoup4 4.6+ Extended report parsing selenium 3.9+ Web automation for certain plugins

docs.title: Ports
docs.text: Port Service Direction Notes 5985 Faraday Server Inbound HTTP API and Web UI 5432 PostgreSQL Internal Database (expose only to Faraday) 6379 Redis Internal Message broker (expose only to Faraday) 443 NGINX (optional) Inbound HTTPS via reverse proxy

docs.title: Firewall
docs.text:
• Allow inbound TCP port 5985 (or 443 if using NGINX reverse proxy)
  • PostgreSQL (5432) and Redis (6379) should only be accessible from the Faraday Server host
  • Outbound internet access is required for license validation (Pro/Corp) and plugin updates

docs.title: Supported Browsers
docs.text:
Faraday's Web UI is a React.js application. Use a current version of:

  • Google Chrome / Chromium
  • Mozilla Firefox
  • Microsoft Edge

docs.title: Key Filesystem Paths
docs.text:
Path Purpose ~/.faraday/ Faraday configuration root ~/.faraday/config/server.ini Server configuration file ~/.faraday/logs/ Application logs ~/.faraday/session/ Session storage ~/.faraday/storage/ Report and evidence storage /home/faraday/.faraday/doc/ License files (Pro/Corp)

The FARADAY_HOME environment variable can override the default configuration root. It is set to /home/faraday in the Docker image.

docs.title: Configuration Defaults by Deployment Type
docs.text:
Bare-metal and Docker deployments use different default values for some settings:

Setting Bare-Metal Docker Notes bind_address localhost 0.0.0.0 Docker binds to all interfaces for container networking api_token_expiration 43200 sec (12 hr) 604800 sec (7 days) API token lifetime session_timeout 12 hours 24 hours Web session timeout delete_report_after_process false true Remove uploaded report files after processing

These are set in ~/.faraday/config/server.ini. Adjust them to match your security and usability requirements.

docs.title: Activities API
docs.text:
API Version: v3 Spec Version: Faraday 5.10.0 Base URL: https://localhost:5985 Authentication: Cookie-based session or API token (Authorization: Token <token>) Content-Type: application/json

Activities represent an enriched view of command executions within a workspace. Each activity record combines the core command metadata (tool, command string, timestamp) with aggregated counts of assets created during the command's run -- including totals for hosts, services, and vulnerabilities broken down by severity. The Activities API is the primary data source for the Faraday dashboard activity feed.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• ActivityFeed Schema
  • Endpoints
  • List Activities
  • Create Activity
  • Get Activity
  • Update Activity (Full)
  • Update Activity (Partial)
  • Delete Activity
  • Count Activities
  • Error Responses
  • See Also

docs.title: ActivityFeed Schema
docs.text:
Property Type Required Read-Only Description _id integer -- yes Internal document ID command string yes -- The full command string that was executed ip string -- -- IP address from which the command was executed hostname string -- -- Hostname of the machine that executed the command params string -- -- Additional parameters or notes user string -- -- Username of the person or process that ran the command workspace string -- yes Name of the workspace this activity belongs to tool string yes -- Tool identifier (e.g., "nmap", "burp", "nessus") import_source string -- -- Source of the import (e.g., "report", "shell", "api") itime datetime yes -- Command start time in ISO 8601 format sum_created_vulnerabilities integer -- yes Total number of vulnerabilities created by this command sum_created_hosts integer -- yes Total number of hosts created by this command sum_created_services integer -- yes Total number of services created by this command sum_created_vulnerability_critical integer -- yes Count of critical-severity vulnerabilities created sum_created_vulnerability_high integer -- yes Count of high-severity vulnerabilities created sum_created_vulnerability_medium integer -- yes Count of medium-severity vulnerabilities created sum_created_vulnerability_low integer -- yes Count of low-severity vulnerabilities created sum_created_vulnerability_info integer -- yes Count of informational-severity vulnerabilities created sum_created_vulnerability_unclassified integer -- yes Count of unclassified vulnerabilities created creator string -- yes Username of the Faraday user who created this record

Required fields for creation: command, itime, tool

docs.title: Example ActivityFeed Object
docs.text:
{
  "_id": 8,
  "command": "nmap -sV -O 192.168.1.0/24",
  "ip": "10.0.0.5",
  "hostname": "scanner.internal",
  "params": "-sV -O",
  "user": "admin",
  "workspace": "pentest_q1",
  "tool": "nmap",
  "import_source": "shell",
  "itime": "2026-02-27T10:00:00+00:00",
  "sum_created_vulnerabilities": 156,
  "sum_created_hosts": 24,
  "sum_created_services": 87,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_critical": 3,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_high": 12,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_medium": 45,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_low": 61,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_info": 35,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_unclassified": 0,
  "creator": "admin"
}

docs.title: Endpoints
docs.text:
All activity endpoints are workspace-scoped under:

/_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/activities
# Method Path Summary 1 GET /_api/v3/ws/{ws}/activities List activities 2 POST /_api/v3/ws/{ws}/activities Create activity 3 GET /_api/v3/ws/{ws}/activities/{object_id} Get activity 4 PUT /_api/v3/ws/{ws}/activities/{object_id} Full update 5 PATCH /_api/v3/ws/{ws}/activities/{object_id} Partial update 6 DELETE /_api/v3/ws/{ws}/activities/{object_id} Delete activity 7 GET /_api/v3/ws/{ws}/activities/count Count activities

docs.title: 1. List Activities
docs.text:
Retrieve a paginated list of all activities in a workspace. Activities are returned in reverse chronological order by default, providing a timeline of tool executions and their impact.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/activities Auth Required

Query Parameters:

Parameter Type Default Description page integer 0 Page number (zero-indexed) page_size integer 20 Number of results per page sort string -- Field to sort by sort_dir string asc Sort direction (asc or desc)

Response: 200 OK

{
  "count": 15,
  "data": [
    {
      "_id": 8,
      "command": "nmap -sV -O 192.168.1.0/24",
      "tool": "nmap",
      "user": "admin",
      "itime": "2026-02-27T10:00:00+00:00",
      "sum_created_hosts": 24,
      "sum_created_services": 87,
      "sum_created_vulnerabilities": 156,
      "sum_created_vulnerability_critical": 3,
      "sum_created_vulnerability_high": 12,
      "sum_created_vulnerability_medium": 45,
      "sum_created_vulnerability_low": 61,
      "sum_created_vulnerability_info": 35,
      "sum_created_vulnerability_unclassified": 0,
      "creator": "admin"
    },
    {
      "_id": 7,
      "command": "nikto -h https://web.example.com",
      "tool": "nikto",
      "user": "admin",
      "itime": "2026-02-26T16:30:00+00:00",
      "sum_created_hosts": 1,
      "sum_created_services": 2,
      "sum_created_vulnerabilities": 23,
      "sum_created_vulnerability_critical": 0,
      "sum_created_vulnerability_high": 4,
      "sum_created_vulnerability_medium": 8,
      "sum_created_vulnerability_low": 7,
      "sum_created_vulnerability_info": 4,
      "sum_created_vulnerability_unclassified": 0,
      "creator": "admin"
    }
  ]
}

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/activities?page=0&page_size=10&sort=itime&sort_dir=desc" \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication 403 User lacks permission for this workspace 404 Workspace not found

docs.title: 2. Create Activity
docs.text:
Create a new activity record in a workspace. This is typically performed internally by Faraday when a command execution completes, but can also be called by external integrations to record tool runs.

Method POST Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/activities Auth Required Content-Type application/json

Request Body:

Field Type Required Description command string yes Full command string tool string yes Tool identifier itime datetime yes Start time (ISO 8601) ip string no Source IP address hostname string no Source hostname params string no Additional parameters user string no User who ran the command import_source string no Import source identifier

Note: The sum_created_* fields are computed server-side based on the assets linked to the underlying command. They do not need to be provided in the request body.

Response: 201 Created

Returns the newly created activity object.

Example:

curl -s -X POST \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/activities" \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{
    "command": "nessus_scan --policy full --target 10.0.0.0/24",
    "tool": "nessus",
    "itime": "2026-02-27T14:00:00",
    "ip": "10.0.0.5",
    "hostname": "scanner.internal",
    "user": "admin",
    "import_source": "report"
  }'

Success (201):

{
  "_id": 9,
  "command": "nessus_scan --policy full --target 10.0.0.0/24",
  "tool": "nessus",
  "itime": "2026-02-27T14:00:00+00:00",
  "ip": "10.0.0.5",
  "hostname": "scanner.internal",
  "user": "admin",
  "workspace": "pentest_q1",
  "import_source": "report",
  "sum_created_hosts": 0,
  "sum_created_services": 0,
  "sum_created_vulnerabilities": 0,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_critical": 0,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_high": 0,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_medium": 0,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_low": 0,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_info": 0,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_unclassified": 0,
  "creator": "admin"
}

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Validation error (missing command, tool, or itime) 401 Missing or invalid authentication 403 User lacks permission for this workspace 404 Workspace not found 409 Duplicated key found

docs.title: 3. Get Activity
docs.text:
Retrieve a single activity by its ID.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/activities/{object_id} Auth Required

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Description workspace_name string Workspace slug object_id integer Activity ID

Response: 200 OK

Returns the full activity object.

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/activities/8" \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -k

Success (200):

{
  "_id": 8,
  "command": "nmap -sV -O 192.168.1.0/24",
  "tool": "nmap",
  "user": "admin",
  "itime": "2026-02-27T10:00:00+00:00",
  "ip": "10.0.0.5",
  "hostname": "scanner.internal",
  "params": "-sV -O",
  "workspace": "pentest_q1",
  "import_source": "shell",
  "sum_created_hosts": 24,
  "sum_created_services": 87,
  "sum_created_vulnerabilities": 156,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_critical": 3,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_high": 12,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_medium": 45,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_low": 61,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_info": 35,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_unclassified": 0,
  "creator": "admin"
}

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication 403 User lacks permission for this workspace 404 Activity or workspace not found

docs.title: 4. Update Activity (Full)
docs.text:
Replace all mutable fields of an activity. Fields not included in the request body are reset to their defaults.

Method PUT Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/activities/{object_id} Auth Required Content-Type application/json

Request Body:

Full activity object with all required fields (command, tool, itime).

Response: 200 OK

Returns the updated activity object.

Example:

curl -s -X PUT \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/activities/8" \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{
    "command": "nmap -sV -O -A 192.168.1.0/24",
    "tool": "nmap",
    "itime": "2026-02-27T10:00:00",
    "ip": "10.0.0.5",
    "hostname": "scanner.internal",
    "params": "-sV -O -A",
    "user": "admin",
    "import_source": "shell"
  }'

Success (200):

{
  "_id": 8,
  "command": "nmap -sV -O -A 192.168.1.0/24",
  "tool": "nmap",
  "itime": "2026-02-27T10:00:00+00:00",
  "ip": "10.0.0.5",
  "hostname": "scanner.internal",
  "params": "-sV -O -A",
  "user": "admin",
  "workspace": "pentest_q1",
  "import_source": "shell",
  "sum_created_hosts": 24,
  "sum_created_services": 87,
  "sum_created_vulnerabilities": 156,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_critical": 3,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_high": 12,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_medium": 45,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_low": 61,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_info": 35,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_unclassified": 0,
  "creator": "admin"
}

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Validation error (missing required fields) 401 Missing or invalid authentication 403 User lacks permission for this workspace 404 Activity or workspace not found 409 Duplicated key found

docs.title: 5. Update Activity (Partial)
docs.text:
Update only the specified fields of an activity. Omitted fields remain unchanged.

Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/activities/{object_id} Auth Required Content-Type application/json

Request Body:

Only the fields to be updated.

Response: 200 OK

Returns the updated activity object.

Example:

curl -s -X PATCH \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/activities/8" \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{"user": "security_team", "params": "-sV -O --script vuln"}'

Success (200):

{
  "_id": 8,
  "command": "nmap -sV -O 192.168.1.0/24",
  "tool": "nmap",
  "user": "security_team",
  "itime": "2026-02-27T10:00:00+00:00",
  "ip": "10.0.0.5",
  "hostname": "scanner.internal",
  "params": "-sV -O --script vuln",
  "workspace": "pentest_q1",
  "import_source": "shell",
  "sum_created_hosts": 24,
  "sum_created_services": 87,
  "sum_created_vulnerabilities": 156,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_critical": 3,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_high": 12,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_medium": 45,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_low": 61,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_info": 35,
  "sum_created_vulnerability_unclassified": 0,
  "creator": "admin"
}

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Validation error 401 Missing or invalid authentication 403 User lacks permission for this workspace 404 Activity or workspace not found 409 Duplicated key found

docs.title: 6. Delete Activity
docs.text:
Delete a single activity by ID.

Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/activities/{object_id} Auth Required

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Description workspace_name string Workspace slug object_id integer Activity ID

Response: 204 No Content

No response body.

Example:

curl -s -X DELETE \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/activities/8" \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -k
# Returns HTTP 204 with empty body

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication 403 User lacks permission for this workspace 404 Activity or workspace not found

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: • Deleting an activity removes the activity feed record only. It does not delete the associated command, hosts, services, or vulnerabilities.

docs.title: 7. Count Activities
docs.text:
Return the total number of activities in a workspace, optionally grouped by a field.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/activities/count Auth Required

Query Parameters:

Parameter Type Default Description group_by string (none) Field to group counts by (e.g., tool, user)

Response: 200 OK

Without group_by:

{
  "count": 15
}

With group_by=tool:

{
  "groups": [
    {"count": 6, "tool": "nmap"},
    {"count": 4, "tool": "nessus"},
    {"count": 3, "tool": "burp"},
    {"count": 2, "tool": "nikto"}
  ]
}

Example:

# Total count
curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/activities/count" \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -k

# Grouped by tool
curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/activities/count?group_by=tool" \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication 403 User lacks permission for this workspace 404 Workspace not found or group_by parameter is not specified

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text:
All endpoints return errors in the standard Faraday format:

{
  "messages": ["Error description here"]
}
Code Meaning 200 Success (GET, PUT, PATCH) 201 Created (POST) 204 Deleted (DELETE) 400 Bad request -- validation error or malformed JSON 401 Unauthorized -- authentication required or token expired 403 Forbidden -- insufficient workspace permissions 404 Not found -- workspace, activity, or endpoint does not exist 500 Internal server error -- check server logs

docs.title: Relationship to Commands
docs.text:
The Activities API and the [[commands]] API are closely related but serve different purposes:

Aspect Commands API Activities API Purpose Raw command execution records Enriched activity feed with asset counts Asset counts Not included in response sum_created_* fields with host/service/vuln totals Global access Yes (/_api/v3/global_commands) No (workspace-scoped only) Primary consumer CLI, automation, audit trail Web UI dashboard, activity timeline

When a command is executed and assets are imported, the server creates both a command record and a corresponding activity record. The activity record is augmented with the sum_created_* aggregate fields that summarize the command's impact on the workspace.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• [[README]] -- API endpoint index and common patterns
  • [[commands]] -- Commands API (raw command records, global access)
  • [[hosts]] -- Hosts API (assets created by activities/commands)
  • [[vulnerabilities]] -- Vulnerabilities API (findings discovered by tool runs)
  • [[services]] -- Services API (services discovered by tool runs)

docs.title: Agent Executions API
docs.text:
Faraday Security REST API v3 -- Version 5.17.0

This document covers the Agent Executions API, which provides read-only access to the history of agent executor runs. Agent executions are created automatically when an agent executor is triggered (manually, via schedule, or via pipeline). The only write operation available is bulk deletion.

This is a new resource group added in version 5.17.0 and is not present in the 5.10.0 published OpenAPI specification.

All endpoints require authentication via API token (Authorization: Token <token>) or session cookie.

Base URL: https://localhost:5985

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• AgentExecution Schema
  • Endpoints
  • List Agent Executions
  • Get Agent Execution
  • Bulk Delete Agent Executions
  • Common Error Codes

docs.title: AgentExecution Schema
docs.text:
All properties are read-only. No fields are required in request bodies because this resource does not support individual creation or update operations.

Property Type Description id integer Primary key. Unique identifier for the execution record. agent_name string Display name of the agent that performed the execution. agent_id integer Foreign key referencing the parent agent. tool string Name of the tool or plugin that produced the results. create_date datetime (ISO 8601) Timestamp when the execution was created. type string Execution type identifier (e.g., "Agent", "Executor"). running boolean Whether the execution is currently in progress. successful boolean Whether the execution completed successfully. null if still running. category string Categorization label for the execution (e.g., "vulnerability_scan", "recon"). parameters_data object Key-value map of parameters that were passed to the executor at launch time. triggered_by string Identifier of the user, schedule, or pipeline that initiated the execution. executor string Name of the specific executor within the agent that was invoked. update_date datetime (ISO 8601) Timestamp of the last status update for this execution.

docs.title: Example AgentExecution Object
docs.text:
{
  "id": 1,
  "agent_name": "nmap-scanner",
  "agent_id": 5,
  "tool": "nmap",
  "create_date": "2026-02-27T14:30:00+00:00",
  "type": "Agent",
  "running": false,
  "successful": true,
  "category": "recon",
  "parameters_data": {
    "target": "192.168.1.0/24",
    "ports": "1-1024",
    "flags": "-sV -sC"
  },
  "triggered_by": "admin",
  "executor": "nmap_executor",
  "update_date": "2026-02-27T14:35:42+00:00"
}

docs.title: Endpoints
docs.text:
All agent execution endpoints are non-workspaced (global) and use the path prefix /_api/v3/agent_executions. No {workspace_name} segment is required.

This resource is read-only except for bulk delete. There are no individual create, update (PUT), or partial update (PATCH) endpoints.

docs.title: List Agent Executions
docs.text:
Retrieve a paginated list of all agent executions across all workspaces.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/agent_executions Auth Required

Query Parameters:

Parameter Type Default Description page integer 0 Page number (zero-indexed). page_size integer 20 Number of results per page. sort string create_date Field to sort by. sort_dir string desc Sort direction (asc or desc).

Response: 200 OK

{
  "rows": [
    {
      "update_date": "2026-04-05T04:02:32.892798+00:00",
      "running": false,
      "successful": true,
      "type": "Local Agent",
      "category": null,
      "triggered_by": "Monthly Scan *.site.com",
      "id": 748,
      "create_date": "2026-04-05T04:00:48.595969+00:00",
      "agent_name": "agent_firstscan",
      "tool": null,
      "executor": 6,
      "parameters_data": {
        "ignore_info": false,
        "resolve_hostname": true,
        "executor_data": {
          "args": {
            "DOMAIN": "site.com"
          },
          "executor": "discovery"
        },
        "workspaces_commands": [
          {
            "workspace_name": "my_workspace",
            "command_id": 6056
          }
        ],
        "min_severity": null,
        "max_severity": null
      },
      "agent_id": 5
    }
    [...]

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/agent_executions?page=0&page_size=10" \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission to view agent executions.

docs.title: Get Agent Execution
docs.text:
Retrieve a single agent execution by its ID.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/agent_executions/{object_id} Auth Required

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Description object_id integer Agent execution ID.

Response: 200 OK

Returns the full AgentExecution object.

{
  "rows": [
    {
      "update_date": "2026-04-05T04:02:32.892798+00:00",
      "running": false,
      "successful": true,
      "type": "Local Agent",
      "category": null,
      "triggered_by": "Monthly Scan *.site.com",
      "id": 748,
      "create_date": "2026-04-05T04:00:48.595969+00:00",
      "agent_name": "agent_firstscan",
      "tool": null,
      "executor": 6,
      "parameters_data": {
        "ignore_info": false,
        "resolve_hostname": true,
        "executor_data": {
          "args": {
            "DOMAIN": "site.com"
          },
          "executor": "discovery"
        },
        "workspaces_commands": [
          {
            "workspace_name": "my_workspace",
            "command_id": 6056
          }
        ],
        "min_severity": null,
        "max_severity": null
      },
      "agent_id": 5
    }
    [...]

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/agent_executions/1" \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission to view agent executions. 404 Agent execution not found.

docs.title: Bulk Delete Agent Executions
docs.text:
Delete multiple agent execution records in a single request. This is the only write operation available on this resource.

Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/agent_executions Auth Required

Request Headers:

Header Value Content-Type application/json

Request Body:

{
  "ids": [1, 2, 3]
}
Field Type Required Description ids array of integers Yes List of agent execution IDs to delete.

Response: 204 No Content

No response body is returned on success.

Example:

curl -s -X DELETE \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/agent_executions" \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{"ids": [1, 2, 3]}'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Invalid request body or empty ids array. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission to delete agent executions. 404 One or more execution IDs not found.

docs.title: Common Error Codes
docs.text: HTTP Status Meaning 200 Success. Response body contains the requested data. 204 No Content. Records were deleted successfully (bulk delete). 400 Bad Request. Malformed JSON, empty ids array, or invalid filter expression. 401 Unauthorized. Authentication token is missing, expired, or invalid. 403 Forbidden. Authenticated user does not have permission for the requested operation. 404 Not Found. The agent execution ID does not exist. 500 Internal Server Error. Unexpected server-side failure.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
All requests must include one of the following:

API Token (recommended):

Authorization: Token <your_api_token>

Obtain a token from the Faraday web UI under Settings > API Tokens, or via:

curl -s -X POST \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/login" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -c cookies.txt \
  -k \
  -d '{"email": "faraday@example.com", "password": "changeme"}'

TOKEN=$(curl -s -X GET "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/token" \
  -b cookies.txt -k | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['token'])")

Session Cookie:

Authenticate via the login endpoint and pass the session cookie with subsequent requests.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• [[README]] -- API endpoints index
  • [[agents]] -- Agent management endpoints
  • [[agent-schedules]] -- Agent schedule management endpoints
  • [[authentication]] -- Authentication and token management

docs.title: Agent Schedules API
docs.text:
API Version: v3 (Faraday 5.10.0+) Base URL: https://localhost:5985/_api Authentication: Cookie-based session or API token (Authorization: Token <token>) Content-Type: application/json

Agent Schedules allow you to automate recurring agent executions by defining cron-like timing, agent references, executor parameters, and workspace targets. Schedules run in the background and trigger the specified agent executor on the configured cadence.

All endpoints are non-workspaced and operate under the /_api/v3/agents_schedule path prefix.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• AgentsSchedule Schema
  • Endpoints
  • List Schedules
  • Create Schedule
  • Get Schedule
  • Update Schedule (Full)
  • Update Schedule (Partial)
  • Delete Schedule
  • Run Schedule
  • Common Error Codes
  • Authentication
  • Usage Notes

docs.title: AgentsSchedule Schema
docs.text:
Property Type Required Description id integer read-only Primary key. Assigned by the server on creation. description string yes Human-readable description of the schedule (e.g., "Daily nmap scan"). agent_id integer no ID of the agent to execute. executor string no Name of the executor to run (e.g., "nmap", "nikto"). crontab string no Cron expression defining the schedule timing (e.g., "0 0 * * *" for daily at midnight). workspace_name string no Target workspace for the execution results. parameters object no Key-value pairs passed to the executor (e.g., {"target": "192.168.1.0/24"}). active boolean no Whether the schedule is currently enabled.

Required fields for creation: description

Note: The OpenAPI specification defines a minimal schema with only id and description. In practice, schedules are richer objects that include agent references, executor configuration, cron timing, and workspace targeting. The additional properties listed above are derived from the runtime behavior of the scheduling system.

docs.title: Example AgentsSchedule Object
docs.text:
{
  "id": 1,
  "description": "Daily nmap scan",
  "agent_id": 5,
  "executor": "nmap",
  "crontab": "0 0 * * *",
  "workspace_name": "pentest",
  "parameters": {
    "target": "192.168.1.0/24"
  },
  "active": true
}

docs.title: Endpoints
docs.text: All endpoints operate under the non-workspaced path prefix /_api/v3/agents_schedule. No {workspace_name} path segment is required (the workspace is specified within the schedule object itself).

docs.title: 1. List Schedules
docs.text:
Retrieve a paginated list of all agent schedules.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/agents_schedule Auth Session cookie or Authorization: Token <token>

docs.title: Query Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Default Description page integer 0 Page number (0-indexed). page_size integer 20 Number of results per page. sort string -- Field to sort by (e.g., id, description). sort_dir string asc Sort direction: asc or desc.

docs.title: Response: 200 OK
docs.text:
{
  "rows": [
    {
      "id": 49,
      "key": 49,
      "value": {
        "description": "Monthly Scan *.site.com.ar",
        "cloud_agent_id": null,
        "service_tag": [],
        "executor": {
          "parameters_metadata": {
            "DOMAIN": {
              "base": "string",
              "type": "string",
              "mandatory": true
            }
          }

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/agents_schedule?page=0&page_size=10" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text: Code Condition 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission to view schedules.

docs.title: 2. Create Schedule
docs.text:
Create a new agent schedule.

Method POST Path /_api/v3/agents_schedule Auth Session cookie or Authorization: Token <token>

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: Field Type Required Description description string Yes Human-readable description of the schedule. agent_id integer No ID of the agent to execute. executor string No Name of the executor to invoke. crontab string No Cron expression for timing (e.g., "0 0 * * *"). workspace_name string No Target workspace for execution results. parameters object No Key-value pairs to pass to the executor.

docs.title: Request Example
docs.text:
{
  "description": "Daily nmap scan",
  "agent_id": 1,
  "executor": "nmap",
  "crontab": "0 0 * * *",
  "workspace_name": "pentest",
  "parameters": {
    "target": "192.168.1.0/24"
  }
}

docs.title: Response: 201 Created
docs.text:
Returns the newly created schedule object with server-assigned fields.

{
  "id": 4,
  "description": "Daily nmap scan",
  "agent_id": 1,
  "executor": "nmap",
  "crontab": "0 0 * * *",
  "workspace_name": "pentest",
  "parameters": {
    "target": "192.168.1.0/24"
  },
  "active": true
}

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -X POST \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/agents_schedule" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{
    "description": "Daily nmap scan",
    "agent_id": 1,
    "executor": "nmap",
    "crontab": "0 0 * * *",
    "workspace_name": "pentest",
    "parameters": {"target": "192.168.1.0/24"}
  }'

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text: Code Condition 400 Validation error (e.g., missing description, invalid crontab expression). 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission to create schedules.

docs.title: 3. Get Schedule
docs.text:
Retrieve a single schedule by its ID.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/agents_schedule/{object_id} Auth Session cookie or Authorization: Token <token>

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description object_id integer Schedule ID.

docs.title: Response: 200 OK
docs.text:
Returns the full schedule object.

{
  "id": 1,
  "description": "Daily nmap scan",
  "agent_id": 5,
  "executor": "nmap",
  "crontab": "0 0 * * *",
  "workspace_name": "pentest",
  "parameters": {
    "target": "192.168.1.0/24"
  },
  "active": true
}

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/agents_schedule/1" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text: Code Condition 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission to view this schedule. 404 Schedule not found.

docs.title: 4. Update Schedule (Full)
docs.text:
Replace all mutable fields of a schedule. Fields not included in the request body are reset to their defaults.

Method PUT Path /_api/v3/agents_schedule/{object_id} Auth Session cookie or Authorization: Token <token>

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description object_id integer Schedule ID.

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: Same schema as Create Schedule. The description field is required.

docs.title: Request Example
docs.text:
{
  "description": "Nightly nmap full-port scan",
  "agent_id": 1,
  "executor": "nmap",
  "crontab": "0 1 * * *",
  "workspace_name": "pentest",
  "parameters": {
    "target": "192.168.1.0/24",
    "extra_args": "-p-"
  }
}

docs.title: Response: 200 OK
docs.text:
Returns the updated schedule object.

{
  "id": 1,
  "description": "Nightly nmap full-port scan",
  "agent_id": 1,
  "executor": "nmap",
  "crontab": "0 1 * * *",
  "workspace_name": "pentest",
  "parameters": {
    "target": "192.168.1.0/24",
    "extra_args": "-p-"
  },
  "active": true
}

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -X PUT \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/agents_schedule/1" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{
    "description": "Nightly nmap full-port scan",
    "agent_id": 1,
    "executor": "nmap",
    "crontab": "0 1 * * *",
    "workspace_name": "pentest",
    "parameters": {"target": "192.168.1.0/24", "extra_args": "-p-"}
  }'

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text: Code Condition 400 Validation error (e.g., missing description). 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission to update this schedule. 404 Schedule not found.

docs.title: 5. Update Schedule (Partial)
docs.text:
Update only the specified fields of a schedule. Omitted fields remain unchanged.

Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/agents_schedule/{object_id} Auth Session cookie or Authorization: Token <token>

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description object_id integer Schedule ID.

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: Only include the fields you want to change.

docs.title: Request Example
docs.text:
{
  "crontab": "0 3 * * *",
  "description": "Updated: run at 3 AM"
}

docs.title: Response: 200 OK
docs.text: Returns the updated schedule object.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -X PATCH \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/agents_schedule/1" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{"crontab": "0 3 * * *", "description": "Updated: run at 3 AM"}'

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text: Code Condition 400 Invalid field values. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission to update this schedule. 404 Schedule not found.

docs.title: 6. Delete Schedule
docs.text:
Delete a single schedule by its ID.

Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/agents_schedule/{object_id} Auth Session cookie or Authorization: Token <token>

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description object_id integer Schedule ID.

docs.title: Response: 204 No Content
docs.text: Empty response body on success.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -X DELETE \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/agents_schedule/1" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text: Code Condition 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission to delete this schedule. 404 Schedule not found.

docs.title: 7. Run Schedule
docs.text:
Manually trigger an immediate execution of a scheduled agent run, bypassing the cron timing.

Method POST Path /_api/v3/agents_schedule/{schedule_id}/run Auth Session cookie or Authorization: Token <token>

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description schedule_id integer Schedule ID to trigger.

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: No request body is required. Send an empty body or {}.

docs.title: Response: 201 Created
docs.text:
Returns confirmation that the scheduled run has been triggered.

{
  "message": "Schedule triggered successfully"
}

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -X POST \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/agents_schedule/1/run" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{}'

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text: Code Condition 400 Schedule is misconfigured (e.g., missing agent or executor). 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission to trigger this schedule. 404 Schedule not found.

docs.title: Common Error Codes
docs.text:
All endpoints may return the following standard error responses:

HTTP Status Meaning 200 Success. Response body contains the requested data or confirmation. 201 Created. New schedule was successfully created. 204 No Content. Schedule was deleted successfully. 400 Bad Request. Malformed JSON, missing required fields, invalid crontab expression, or invalid filter. 401 Unauthorized. Authentication token is missing, expired, or invalid. 403 Forbidden. Authenticated user does not have permission for the requested operation. 404 Not Found. The schedule does not exist. 500 Internal Server Error. Unexpected server-side failure.

docs.title: Error Response Format
docs.text:
{
  "messages": ["Validation error: description is required"]
}

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
All requests must include one of the following:

API Token (recommended):

Authorization: Token <your_api_token>

Obtain a token from the Faraday web UI under Settings > API Tokens, or via:

curl -s -X POST \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/login" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -c cookies.txt \
  -k \
  -d '{"email": "faraday@example.com", "password": "changeme"}'

TOKEN=$(curl -s -X GET "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/token" \
  -b cookies.txt -k | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['token'])")

Session Cookie:

Authenticate via the login endpoint and pass the session cookie with subsequent requests.

docs.title: Non-Workspaced Endpoints
docs.text: Unlike most Faraday API resources (hosts, services, vulnerabilities), agent schedules are not scoped to a workspace path segment. All schedule endpoints live under /_api/v3/agents_schedule. The target workspace for each schedule is specified within the schedule object's workspace_name field.

docs.title: Cron Expression Format
docs.text:
The crontab field uses standard five-field cron syntax:

* * * * *
| | | | |
| | | | +--- Day of week (0-6, Sun=0)
| | | +----- Month (1-12)
| | +------- Day of month (1-31)
| +--------- Hour (0-23)
+----------- Minute (0-59)

Common examples:

Expression Meaning 0 0 * * * Every day at midnight. 0 */6 * * * Every 6 hours. 30 2 * * 1 Every Monday at 2:30 AM. 0 0 * * 0 Every Sunday at midnight. */15 * * * * Every 15 minutes.

docs.title: Relationship to Agents and Executors
docs.text:
• A schedule references an agent by agent_id and an executor by name (executor). The agent must be registered and active for the schedule to execute successfully.
  • The parameters object is passed directly to the executor at runtime. Valid keys depend on the specific executor (e.g., target, extra_args).
  • Use the Agents API to list available agents and their executors before creating schedules.

docs.title: Manual Triggers
docs.text: The /run endpoint allows you to test a schedule immediately without waiting for its cron timing. This is useful for verifying that the agent, executor, and parameters are configured correctly before relying on automated execution.

docs.title: Schedule Lifecycle
docs.text:
1. Create a schedule with POST /_api/v3/agents_schedule.
  2. Verify the configuration by triggering a manual run with POST /_api/v3/agents_schedule/{id}/run.
  3. Monitor execution results via the Agent Executions API.
  4. Adjust timing or parameters with PATCH /_api/v3/agents_schedule/{id}.
  5. Remove schedules that are no longer needed with DELETE /_api/v3/agents_schedule/{id}.

docs.title: Agents API
docs.text:
Faraday Security REST API v3 -- Version 5.10.0

The Agents API manages Faraday Agents: lightweight dispatcher processes that run security tools (executors) against target infrastructure and feed results back into Faraday workspaces. Agents are non-workspaced resources -- they exist at the server level and can target any workspace when executed.

All endpoints require authentication via API token (Authorization: Token <token>) or session cookie unless stated otherwise.

Base URL: https://localhost:5985

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• Agent Lifecycle
  • Agent Schema
  • Executor Schema
  • AgentAuthToken Schema
  • Standard CRUD Endpoints
  • List Agents
  • Create Agent
  • Get Agent
  • Update Agent (Full)
  • Update Agent (Partial)
  • Delete Agent
  • Special Action Endpoints
  • Run Agent
  • List Active Agents
  • Get Executor Manifests
  • Agent Token Endpoint
  • Get Agent Registration Token
  • Public Agent Self-Registration
  • Register Agent (Public)
  • Common Error Codes

docs.title: Agent Lifecycle
docs.text:
Agents follow a defined lifecycle from registration through execution:

1. REGISTRATION
   Admin obtains agent token   -->  GET /_api/v3/agent_token
   Dispatcher registers itself -->  POST /v3/agents (public, agent-token auth)
   Server returns agent object with registration token

2. ONLINE
   Dispatcher connects via WebSocket using the registration token
   Agent status changes to is_online: true
   Executor manifests become available

3. RUN
   User triggers a run          -->  POST /_api/v3/agents/{agent_id}/run
   Server dispatches executor command over WebSocket
   Executor runs the security tool and streams results back

4. OFFLINE
   Dispatcher disconnects or times out
   Agent status changes to is_online: false

Note: Agents can be scheduled for periodic execution using the [[agent-schedules]] API. Execution history is tracked via the [[agent-executions]] API.

docs.title: Agent Schema
docs.text:
Property Type Required Read-Only Description id integer -- yes Primary key. Auto-assigned on creation. name string yes no Unique display name for the agent. Minimum 1 character. description string no yes Free-text description. Populated by the dispatcher on registration. (Note: not in published OpenAPI spec but may exist at runtime) status string no yes Current agent status string. active boolean no no Whether the agent is enabled. Nullable. create_date datetime -- yes ISO 8601 timestamp of agent creation. update_date datetime -- yes ISO 8601 timestamp of last update. creator string -- yes Username that registered the agent. is_online boolean -- yes true if the dispatcher is currently connected via WebSocket. executors array of Executor -- yes List of executor definitions reported by the dispatcher. last_run datetime -- yes ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent execution.

Required fields for creation: name

docs.title: Example Agent Object
docs.text:
{
  "id": 7,
  "name": "nmap-scanner",
  "description": "Runs nmap network scans",
  "status": "online",
  "active": true,
  "create_date": "2026-02-15T14:30:00+00:00",
  "update_date": "2026-02-27T09:12:00+00:00",
  "creator": "admin",
  "is_online": true,
  "executors": [
    {
      "id": 12,
      "name": "nmap",
      "agent_id": 7,
      "last_run": "2026-02-27T09:10:00+00:00",
      "parameters_metadata": {
        "target": {
          "type": "string",
          "mandatory": true
        },
        "port_list": {
          "type": "string",
          "mandatory": false
        }
      },
      "schedules": []
    }
  ],
  "last_run": "2026-02-27T09:10:00+00:00"
}

docs.title: Executor Schema
docs.text:
Executors are the individual security tools (e.g., nmap, nuclei, nikto) that an agent's dispatcher can invoke. Each agent may expose one or more executors.

Property Type Read-Only Description id integer yes Primary key. name string yes Executor name as reported by the dispatcher manifest. agent_id integer yes Foreign key to the parent agent. last_run datetime yes ISO 8601 timestamp of the last execution of this executor. parameters_metadata object yes Schema describing accepted parameters (names, types, mandatory flags). schedules array of AgentsSchedule yes Schedules attached to this executor. See [[agent-schedules]].

docs.title: Example Executor Object
docs.text:
{
  "id": 12,
  "name": "nmap",
  "agent_id": 7,
  "last_run": "2026-02-27T09:10:00+00:00",
  "parameters_metadata": {
    "target": {
      "type": "string",
      "mandatory": true
    },
    "port_list": {
      "type": "string",
      "mandatory": false
    },
    "extra_args": {
      "type": "string",
      "mandatory": false
    }
  },
  "schedules": []
}

docs.title: AgentAuthToken Schema
docs.text:
The registration token used by Faraday Agent Dispatchers to authenticate during self-registration. This is a time-based one-time-password (TOTP) token that rotates periodically.

Property Type Required Description token string yes The current TOTP agent registration token. expires_in number yes Seconds remaining before the token expires. total_duration number yes Total lifetime of the token in seconds.

docs.title: Example AgentAuthToken Object
docs.text:
{
  "token": "983241",
  "expires_in": 22,
  "total_duration": 30
}

docs.title: Standard CRUD Endpoints
docs.text: All CRUD endpoints are non-workspaced and live under /_api/v3/agents.

docs.title: List Agents
docs.text:
Retrieve a paginated list of all registered agents and their executors.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/agents Auth Required

Query Parameters:

Parameter Type Default Description page integer 0 Page number (zero-indexed). page_size integer 20 Number of results per page. sort string -- Field to sort by. sort_dir string asc Sort direction (asc or desc).

Response: 200 OK

{
  "count": 3,
  "data": [
    {
      "id": 7,
      "name": "nmap-scanner",
      "description": "Runs nmap network scans",
      "status": "online",
      "active": true,
      "is_online": true,
      "executors": [ "..." ],
      "last_run": "2026-02-27T09:10:00+00:00",
      "create_date": "2026-02-15T14:30:00+00:00",
      "update_date": "2026-02-27T09:12:00+00:00",
      "creator": "admin"
    }
  ]
}

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/agents?page=0&page_size=10" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 Insufficient permissions.

docs.title: Create Agent
docs.text:
Register a new agent via the server API. This is the admin-side registration path -- agents typically self-register via the public endpoint.

Method POST Path /_api/v3/agents Auth Required Content-Type application/json

Note: The POST /_api/v3/agents endpoint is not listed in the published OpenAPI spec (only GET is declared at this path), but it exists in the server codebase as part of the standard CRUD mixin.

Request Body:

Field Type Required Description name string yes Unique agent name. Minimum 1 character. active boolean no Whether the agent starts enabled. Defaults to true. 
{
  "name": "nmap-scanner",
  "description": "Runs nmap scans"
}

Response: 201 Created

Returns the created Agent object. When created via the internal registration flow, the response includes a token field used by the dispatcher to establish the WebSocket connection.

{
  "id": 7,
  "name": "nmap-scanner",
  "description": "Runs nmap scans",
  "status": "",
  "active": true,
  "create_date": "2026-02-27T14:30:00+00:00",
  "update_date": "2026-02-27T14:30:00+00:00",
  "creator": "admin",
  "is_online": false,
  "executors": [],
  "last_run": null,
  "token": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIs..."
}

Example:

curl -s -X POST \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/agents" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{
    "name": "nmap-scanner",
    "description": "Runs nmap scans"
  }'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Validation error (e.g., missing name field). 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 409 Agent with this name already exists.

docs.title: Get Agent
docs.text:
Retrieve a single agent by its ID.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/agents/{object_id} Auth Required

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description object_id integer yes Agent primary key.

Response: 200 OK

Returns the full Agent object including nested executors.

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/agents/7" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 404 Agent not found.

docs.title: Update Agent (Full)
docs.text:
Replace all mutable fields of an agent.

Method PUT Path /_api/v3/agents/{object_id} Auth Required Content-Type application/json

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description object_id integer yes Agent primary key.

Request Body:

Full Agent object with all mutable fields. Read-only fields are ignored.

Field Type Required Description name string yes Agent name. Minimum 1 character. active boolean no Whether the agent is enabled. 
{
  "name": "nmap-scanner-v2",
  "active": true
}

Response: 200 OK

Returns the updated Agent object.

Example:

curl -s -X PUT \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/agents/7" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{
    "name": "nmap-scanner-v2",
    "active": true
  }'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Validation error. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 404 Agent not found. 409 Duplicated key found (name conflict).

docs.title: Update Agent (Partial)
docs.text:
Update one or more mutable fields of an agent without replacing the full object.

Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/agents/{object_id} Auth Required Content-Type application/json

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description object_id integer yes Agent primary key.

Request Body:

Only the fields to update. Omitted fields remain unchanged.

{
  "active": false
}

Response: 200 OK

Returns the updated Agent object.

Example:

# Disable an agent
curl -s -X PATCH \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/agents/7" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{"active": false}'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Validation error. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 404 Agent not found. 409 Duplicated key found (name conflict).

docs.title: Delete Agent
docs.text:
Permanently delete an agent and all its executor definitions.

Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/agents/{object_id} Auth Required

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description object_id integer yes Agent primary key.

Response: 204 No Content

No response body.

Example:

curl -s -X DELETE \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/agents/7" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 404 Agent not found.

docs.title: Run Agent
docs.text:
Trigger an agent executor to run against a target workspace. The server sends the execution command to the connected dispatcher over the WebSocket channel.

Method POST Path /_api/v3/agents/{agent_id}/run Auth Required Content-Type application/json

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description agent_id integer yes Agent primary key.

Request Body:

Field Type Required Description executor string yes Name of the executor to invoke (must match one of the agent's executor names). args object yes Key-value pairs of parameters to pass to the executor. Keys must match parameters_metadata. workspace_name string yes Target workspace where results will be stored. 
{
  "executor": "nmap",
  "args": {
    "target": "192.168.1.0/24",
    "port_list": "22,80,443,8080"
  },
  "workspace_name": "pentest_q1"
}

Response: 201 Created

Returns the Agent object. The execution is tracked internally by the server.

{
  "id": 7,
  "name": "nmap-scanner",
  "is_online": true,
  "executors": [ "..." ],
  "last_run": "2026-02-27T10:00:00+00:00"
}

Example:

curl -s -X POST \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/agents/7/run" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{
    "executor": "nmap",
    "args": {
      "target": "192.168.1.0/24"
    },
    "workspace_name": "pentest_q1"
  }'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Bad request. Agent is offline, executor not found, invalid arguments, or workspace does not exist. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 404 Agent not found.

Tip: Verify the agent is online (is_online: true) before triggering a run. Attempting to run an offline agent will return 400.

docs.title: List Active Agents
docs.text:
Retrieve a list of currently online (connected) agents.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/agents/active_agents Auth Required

Query Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description agent_version string no Filter by agent dispatcher version. Use "latest" for the most recent.

Response: 200 OK

Returns an array of Agent objects that are currently connected.

[
  {
    "id": 7,
    "name": "nmap-scanner",
    "is_online": true,
    "executors": [ "..." ],
    "last_run": "2026-02-27T09:10:00+00:00"
  }
]

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/agents/active_agents" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication.

docs.title: Get Executor Manifests
docs.text:
Retrieve the manifests (available executors and their parameter schemas) for all registered agents. This endpoint is useful for building UIs that present executor options to the user.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/agents/get_manifests Auth Required

Query Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description agent_version string no Filter manifests by agent dispatcher version.

Response: 200 OK

Returns an object keyed by agent name, with each value containing the executor manifests and their parameter metadata.

{
  "nmap-scanner": {
    "executors": {
      "nmap": {
        "parameters_metadata": {
          "target": {"type": "string", "mandatory": true},
          "port_list": {"type": "string", "mandatory": false}
        },
        "tool": "nmap",
        "category": ["network", "scanner"]
      }
    }
  }
}

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/agents/get_manifests" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication.

docs.title: Get Agent Registration Token
docs.text:
Retrieve the current time-based one-time-password (TOTP) token used to authenticate agent dispatcher self-registration. This token rotates automatically; the expires_in field indicates remaining validity.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/agent_token Auth Required (admin or user with agent management permissions)

Response: 200 OK

Returns an AgentAuthToken object.

{
  "token": "983241",
  "expires_in": 22,
  "total_duration": 30
}

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/agent_token" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 Insufficient permissions (agent token access may be restricted to admins).

Important: Treat the agent registration token as a secret. Anyone with this token can register new agents on your Faraday server.

docs.title: Register Agent (Public)
docs.text:
Public endpoint used by Faraday Agent Dispatchers to register themselves with the server. This endpoint does not use the /_api prefix and authenticates via the Agent authorization scheme instead of user credentials.

Method POST Path /v3/agents Auth Agent token (Authorization: Agent <agent-token>) Content-Type application/json Rate-Limited Yes

Note: This endpoint is not declared in the published OpenAPI spec (faraday_swagger.json) but exists in the server codebase (agent.py). It is the primary registration path used by the faraday-agent-dispatcher.

Request Headers:

Header Value Description Authorization Agent <token> The TOTP agent registration token obtained from GET /_api/v3/agent_token. Content-Type application/json Required.

Request Body:

Field Type Required Description name string yes Agent name. Must be unique across the server. description string no Description of the agent. 
{
  "name": "nmap-scanner",
  "description": "Nmap network scanner agent"
}

Response: 201 Created

Returns the Agent object with a token field. The dispatcher uses this token to establish its WebSocket connection to the server.

{
  "id": 7,
  "name": "nmap-scanner",
  "description": "Nmap network scanner agent",
  "status": "",
  "active": true,
  "create_date": "2026-02-27T14:30:00+00:00",
  "update_date": "2026-02-27T14:30:00+00:00",
  "creator": "",
  "is_online": false,
  "executors": [],
  "last_run": null,
  "token": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIs..."
}

Example:

# Step 1: Obtain the registration token (requires admin auth)
AGENT_TOKEN=$(curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/agent_token" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k | jq -r '.token')

# Step 2: Register the agent using the TOTP token
curl -s -X POST \
  "https://localhost:5985/v3/agents" \
  -H "Authorization: Agent $AGENT_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{
    "name": "nmap-scanner",
    "description": "Nmap network scanner agent"
  }'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Validation error (e.g., missing name). 401 Invalid or expired agent registration token. 409 Agent with this name already exists. 429 Rate limit exceeded.

Tip: The agent token is time-based and rotates every ~30 seconds. If registration fails with 401, fetch a fresh token from GET /_api/v3/agent_token and retry.

docs.title: Common Error Codes
docs.text:
These error codes apply across all Agents API endpoints.

Code Meaning Typical Cause 200 Success GET, PUT, PATCH succeeded. 201 Created POST succeeded (new agent or run triggered). 204 No Content DELETE succeeded. 400 Bad Request Validation error, missing required fields, agent offline during run, or invalid filter expression. 401 Unauthorized Missing or invalid authentication token/session. 403 Forbidden Insufficient permissions for the requested operation. 404 Not Found Agent or executor ID does not exist. 409 Conflict Duplicate agent name. 429 Too Many Requests Rate limit exceeded (public registration endpoint only). 500 Internal Server Error Unexpected server-side failure.

docs.title: Standard Error Response Format
docs.text:
{
  "messages": ["Error description here"]
}

docs.title: Endpoint Summary Table
docs.text: # Method Path Description 1 GET /_api/v3/agents List all agents 2 POST /_api/v3/agents Create/register agent 3 GET /_api/v3/agents/{object_id} Get agent by ID 4 PUT /_api/v3/agents/{object_id} Full update agent 5 PATCH /_api/v3/agents/{object_id} Partial update agent 6 DELETE /_api/v3/agents/{object_id} Delete agent 7 POST /_api/v3/agents/{agent_id}/run Run an agent executor 8 GET /_api/v3/agents/active_agents List online agents 9 GET /_api/v3/agents/get_manifests Get executor manifests 10 GET /_api/v3/agent_token Get agent registration token -- POST /v3/agents Public agent self-registration
