GET /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/comment
Parameter In Type Required Description workspace_name path string yes Target workspace name page query integer no Page number (0-indexed, default: 0) page_size query integer no Items per page (default: 20) sort query string no Field to sort by sort_dir query string no Sort direction: asc or desc

Authentication: Required (session cookie or API token)

Response

Code Description 200 Success. Returns paginated list of Comment objects 401 Unauthorized -- not authenticated 403 Forbidden -- insufficient permissions 404 Workspace not found

Example

curl -s -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/comment?page=0&page_size=50"

[
  {
    "id": 1,
    "text": "Found during initial pentest reconnaissance",
    "object_type": "vulnerability",
    "comment_type": "",
    "object_id": 42,
    "creator": "faraday",
    "create_date": "2026-02-27T10:00:00+00:00",
    "update_date": "2026-02-27T10:00:00+00:00",
    "metadata": {}
  }
]

docs.title: 2. Create Comment
docs.text:
Create a new comment attached to a host, service, vulnerability, or another comment.

Request

POST /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/comment
Parameter In Type Required Description workspace_name path string yes Target workspace name

Request Body (application/json)

Field Type Required Description text string yes Comment body text object_type string yes Target object type: host, service, vulnerability, or comment object_id integer yes ID of the target object

Authentication: Required

Response

Code Description 201 Created. Returns the new Comment object 400 Bad request -- validation error (missing required fields) 401 Unauthorized 403 Forbidden 404 Workspace or target object not found 409 Conflict -- duplicate comment

Example

curl -s -X POST -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/comment" \
  -d '{
    "text": "Found during pentest",
    "object_type": "vulnerability",
    "object_id": 42
  }'

{
  "id": 3,
  "text": "Found during pentest",
  "object_type": "vulnerability",
  "comment_type": "",
  "object_id": 42,
  "creator": "faraday",
  "create_date": "2026-02-27T14:00:00+00:00",
  "update_date": "2026-02-27T14:00:00+00:00",
  "metadata": {}
}

docs.title: 3. Get Comment by ID
docs.text:
Retrieve a single comment by its numeric ID.

Request

GET /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/comment/{object_id}
Parameter In Type Required Description workspace_name path string yes Target workspace name object_id path integer yes Comment ID

Authentication: Required

Response

Code Description 200 Success. Returns the Comment object 401 Unauthorized 403 Forbidden 404 Comment or workspace not found

Example

curl -s -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/comment/3"

{
  "id": 3,
  "text": "Found during pentest",
  "object_type": "vulnerability",
  "comment_type": "",
  "object_id": 42,
  "creator": "faraday",
  "create_date": "2026-02-27T14:00:00+00:00",
  "update_date": "2026-02-27T14:00:00+00:00",
  "metadata": {}
}

docs.title: 4. Full Update Comment
docs.text:
Replace all fields of a comment. All required fields must be included in the request body.

Request

PUT /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/comment/{object_id}
Parameter In Type Required Description workspace_name path string yes Target workspace name object_id path integer yes Comment ID

Request Body (application/json)

Full Comment object with all required fields (text, object_type, object_id).

Authentication: Required

Response

Code Description 200 Success. Returns the updated Comment object 400 Bad request -- validation error 401 Unauthorized 403 Forbidden 404 Comment or workspace not found 409 Conflict -- duplicate comment

Example

curl -s -X PUT -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/comment/3" \
  -d '{
    "text": "Updated: Confirmed critical finding during retest",
    "object_type": "vulnerability",
    "object_id": 42
  }'

{
  "id": 3,
  "text": "Updated: Confirmed critical finding during retest",
  "object_type": "vulnerability",
  "comment_type": "",
  "object_id": 42,
  "creator": "faraday",
  "create_date": "2026-02-27T14:00:00+00:00",
  "update_date": "2026-02-27T15:00:00+00:00",
  "metadata": {}
}

docs.title: 5. Partial Update Comment
docs.text:
Update one or more fields of a comment without replacing the entire object.

Request

PATCH /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/comment/{object_id}
Parameter In Type Required Description workspace_name path string yes Target workspace name object_id path integer yes Comment ID

Request Body (application/json)

Only the fields to be updated.

Authentication: Required

Response

Code Description 200 Success. Returns the updated Comment object 400 Bad request -- validation error 401 Unauthorized 403 Forbidden 404 Comment or workspace not found 409 Conflict -- duplicate comment

Example

curl -s -X PATCH -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/comment/3" \
  -d '{"text": "Patched: Added remediation steps"}'

{
  "id": 3,
  "text": "Patched: Added remediation steps",
  "object_type": "vulnerability",
  "comment_type": "",
  "object_id": 42,
  "creator": "faraday",
  "create_date": "2026-02-27T14:00:00+00:00",
  "update_date": "2026-02-27T16:00:00+00:00",
  "metadata": {}
}

docs.title: 6. Delete Comment
docs.text:
Delete a single comment by ID.

Request

DELETE /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/comment/{object_id}
Parameter In Type Required Description workspace_name path string yes Target workspace name object_id path integer yes Comment ID

Authentication: Required

Response

Code Description 204 The resource was deleted successfully (no body) 401 Unauthorized 403 Forbidden 404 Comment or workspace not found

Example

curl -s -X DELETE -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/comment/3"
# Returns HTTP 204 with empty body

docs.title: 7. Bulk Delete Comments
docs.text:
Delete multiple comments in a single request by providing a list of IDs.

Request

DELETE /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/comment
Parameter In Type Required Description workspace_name path string yes Target workspace name

Request Body (application/json)

{
  "ids": [1, 2, 3]
}

Authentication: Required

Response

Code Description 204 Resources deleted successfully (no body) 400 Bad request -- invalid or missing ids 401 Unauthorized 403 Forbidden 404 Workspace not found

Example

curl -s -X DELETE -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/comment" \
  -d '{"ids": [4, 5, 6]}'
# Returns HTTP 204 with empty body

docs.title: 8. Count / Group Comments
docs.text:
Return a count of comments in the workspace, optionally grouped by a field.

Request

GET /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/comment/count
Parameter In Type Required Description workspace_name path string yes Target workspace name group_by query string no Field to group results by (e.g., object_type)

Authentication: Required

Response

Code Description 200 Success. Returns count or grouped results 401 Unauthorized 403 Forbidden 404 group_by field is not specified or workspace not found

Example

# Total count
curl -s -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/comment/count"

# Grouped by object_type
curl -s -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/comment/count?group_by=object_type"

{
  "total_count": 128
}

docs.title: 9. Create Deduplicated Comment
docs.text:
Create a comment only if an identical comment (same text, object_type, and object_id) does not already exist. This endpoint is served by the UniqueCommentView and is useful for automated pipelines that may re-process the same data.

Note: This endpoint exists in the Faraday codebase (comment_unique) but is not included in the published OpenAPI specification. It is documented here for completeness.

Request

POST /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/comment_unique
Parameter In Type Required Description workspace_name path string yes Target workspace name

Request Body (application/json)

Field Type Required Description text string yes Comment body text object_type string yes Target object type: host, service, vulnerability, or comment object_id integer yes ID of the target object

Authentication: Required

Response

Code Description 201 Created. Returns the new Comment object (comment did not exist) 409 Conflict -- an identical comment already exists. Returns the existing Comment object 400 Bad request -- validation error 401 Unauthorized 403 Forbidden 404 Workspace or target object not found

Example

# First call creates the comment
curl -s -X POST -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/comment_unique" \
  -d '{
    "text": "Automated scan finding: open SSH port",
    "object_type": "host",
    "object_id": 10
  }'
# Returns HTTP 201

# Second identical call returns 409 (duplicate)
curl -s -X POST -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/comment_unique" \
  -d '{
    "text": "Automated scan finding: open SSH port",
    "object_type": "host",
    "object_id": 10
  }'
# Returns HTTP 409 with existing comment object

{
  "id": 10,
  "text": "Automated scan finding: open SSH port",
  "object_type": "host",
  "comment_type": "",
  "object_id": 10,
  "creator": "faraday",
  "create_date": "2026-02-27T18:00:00+00:00",
  "update_date": "2026-02-27T18:00:00+00:00",
  "metadata": {}
}

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text:
All endpoints return errors in the standard Faraday format:

{
  "messages": ["Error description here"]
}
Code Meaning 400 Bad request -- validation error or malformed input 401 Unauthorized -- authentication required 403 Forbidden -- insufficient workspace permissions 404 Not found -- workspace, comment, or target object does not exist 409 Conflict -- duplicate comment 500 Internal server error

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• [[README]] -- API endpoint index and common patterns
  • [[vulnerabilities]] -- Vulnerabilities API (primary target for comments)
  • [[hosts]] -- Hosts API (comments can be attached to hosts)
  • [[services]] -- Services API (comments can be attached to services)

docs.title: Credentials API
docs.text:
API Version: v3 Spec Version: Faraday 5.10.0 Base URL: https://localhost:5985 Authentication: Cookie-based session or API token (Authorization: Token <token>) Content-Type: application/json (unless noted otherwise)

Credentials represent authentication data (username/password pairs) associated with hosts or services discovered during a penetration test. Each credential is scoped to a workspace and linked to a parent object (host or service).

docs.title: Credential Schema
docs.text:
Property Type Required Read-Only Description id integer -- -- Unique identifier _id integer -- yes Internal document ID _rev string -- yes Document revision identifier username string yes -- Username / login name (min 1 character) password string -- -- Password or secret name string -- -- Credential display name (nullable) description string -- -- Free-text description parent -- yes -- Parent object ID (host or service) parent_type -- yes -- Type of parent (Host or Service) owner string -- yes Username of the creator owned boolean -- yes Whether this credential has been validated couchdbid string -- -- Legacy CouchDB document ID metadata -- -- -- Additional metadata host_ip string -- yes IP of the associated host service_name string -- yes Name of the associated service target string -- yes Target identifier string

Required fields for creation: parent, parent_type, username

docs.title: Example Object
docs.text:
{
  "id": 1,
  "_id": 1,
  "_rev": "",
  "parent": 5,
  "username": "admin",
  "description": "SSH credentials",
  "name": "admin-ssh",
  "password": "changeme",
  "owner": "faraday",
  "owned": true,
  "couchdbid": "",
  "parent_type": "Host",
  "metadata": {},
  "host_ip": "192.168.1.1",
  "service_name": "ssh",
  "target": "192.168.1.1"
}

docs.title: Endpoints
docs.text:
All credential endpoints are workspace-scoped under:

/_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/credential
# Method Path Summary 1 GET /_api/v3/ws/{ws}/credential List credentials 2 POST /_api/v3/ws/{ws}/credential Create credential 3 GET /_api/v3/ws/{ws}/credential/{object_id} Get credential by ID 4 PUT /_api/v3/ws/{ws}/credential/{object_id} Full update credential 5 PATCH /_api/v3/ws/{ws}/credential/{object_id} Partial update credential 6 DELETE /_api/v3/ws/{ws}/credential/{object_id} Delete credential 7 DELETE /_api/v3/ws/{ws}/credential Bulk delete credentials 8 PATCH /_api/v3/ws/{ws}/credential Bulk update credentials 9 GET /_api/v3/ws/{ws}/credential/count Count / group credentials

docs.title: 1. List Credentials
docs.text:
Retrieve a paginated list of all credentials in a workspace.

Request

GET /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/credential
Parameter In Type Required Description workspace_name path string yes Target workspace name

Response

Code Description 200 Success. Returns list of Credential objects

Example

curl -s -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest/credential"

docs.title: 2. Create Credential
docs.text:
Create a new credential in a workspace.

Request

POST /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/credential

Request Body (application/json)

Field Type Required Description username string yes Username / login parent integer yes Parent host or service ID parent_type string yes "Host" or "Service" password string no Password or secret name string no Display name description string no Free-text description

Response

Code Description 201 Created. Returns the new Credential object 409 Conflict -- duplicated key found

Example

curl -s -X POST -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest/credential" \
  -d '{
    "username": "admin",
    "password": "changeme",
    "parent": 5,
    "parent_type": "Host"
  }'

docs.title: 3. Get Credential by ID
docs.text:
Retrieve a single credential by its numeric ID.

Request

GET /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/credential/{object_id}
Parameter In Type Required Description workspace_name path string yes Target workspace name object_id path integer yes Credential ID

Response

Code Description 200 Success. Returns the Credential object

Example

curl -s -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest/credential/1"

docs.title: 4. Full Update Credential
docs.text:
Replace all fields of a credential.

Request

PUT /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/credential/{object_id}
Parameter In Type Required Description workspace_name path string yes Target workspace name object_id path integer yes Credential ID

Request Body (application/json)

Full Credential object with all required fields (username, parent, parent_type).

Response

Code Description 200 Success. Returns the updated Credential object 409 Conflict -- duplicated key found

Example

curl -s -X PUT -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest/credential/1" \
  -d '{
    "username": "administrator",
    "password": "newpass",
    "parent": 5,
    "parent_type": "Host"
  }'

docs.title: 5. Partial Update Credential
docs.text:
Update one or more fields of a credential without replacing the entire object.

Request

PATCH /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/credential/{object_id}
Parameter In Type Required Description workspace_name path string yes Target workspace name object_id path integer yes Credential ID

Request Body (application/json)

Only the fields to be updated.

Response

Code Description 200 Success. Returns the updated Credential object 409 Conflict -- duplicated key found

Example

curl -s -X PATCH -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest/credential/1" \
  -d '{"password": "updated_pass"}'

docs.title: 6. Delete Credential
docs.text:
Delete a single credential by ID.

Request

DELETE /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/credential/{object_id}
Parameter In Type Required Description workspace_name path string yes Target workspace name object_id path integer yes Credential ID

Response

Code Description 204 The resource was deleted successfully

Example

curl -s -X DELETE -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest/credential/1"

docs.title: 7. Bulk Delete Credentials
docs.text:
Delete multiple credentials by providing a list of IDs.

Request

DELETE /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/credential

Request Body (application/json)

{
  "ids": [1, 2, 3]
}

Response

Code Description 204 Resources deleted successfully

Example

curl -s -X DELETE -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest/credential" \
  -d '{"ids": [3, 4, 5]}'

docs.title: 8. Bulk Update Credentials
docs.text:
Update one or more fields on multiple credentials simultaneously.

Request

PATCH /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/credential

Request Body (application/json)

{
  "ids": [1, 2, 3],
  "update": {
    "description": "Updated in bulk"
  }
}

Response

Code Description 204 Updated successfully

Example

curl -s -X PATCH -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest/credential" \
  -d '{"ids": [1, 2], "update": {"description": "bulk updated"}}'

docs.title: 9. Count / Group Credentials
docs.text:
Return a count of credentials in the workspace, optionally grouped by a field.

Request

GET /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/credential/count
Parameter In Type Required Description workspace_name path string yes Target workspace name group_by query string no Field to group results by

Response

Code Description 200 Success. Returns count or grouped results 404 group_by is not specified

Example

curl -s -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest/credential/count"

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• [[README]] -- API endpoint index
  • [[hosts]] -- Hosts API
  • [[services]] -- Services API

docs.title: Custom Fields API
docs.text:
Base URL: https://localhost:5985 API Version: v3 Authentication: Cookie-based session or API token (Authorization: Token <token>) Content-Type: application/json

The Custom Fields API manages user-defined field schemas that extend the default vulnerability data model. Custom fields allow organizations to capture additional metadata on vulnerabilities beyond the built-in fields. Once a custom field schema is defined, the field becomes available on all vulnerabilities and vulnerability templates through the customfields object.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• CustomFieldsSchema
  • Endpoints
  • List Custom Fields
  • Create Custom Field
  • Get Custom Field
  • Full Update Custom Field
  • Partial Update Custom Field
  • Delete Custom Field
  • Bulk Delete Custom Fields
  • Common Error Codes

docs.title: CustomFieldsSchema
docs.text:
Property Type Required Description id integer read-only Primary key. field_name string yes Internal field name (used as the key in customfields objects). Must be unique. No spaces; use underscores. field_display_name string yes Human-readable display label shown in the UI. field_type string yes Data type of the field. One of: "str" (string), "int" (integer), "list" (list of strings), "choice" (single selection from predefined options). field_metadata string no Additional metadata. For "choice" type fields, this contains the JSON-encoded array of options (e.g., "[\"Option A\", \"Option B\"]"). field_order integer yes Display order of this field in the UI. Lower numbers appear first. table_name string yes The resource type this field applies to. Currently only "vulnerability" is supported.

Required fields for creation: field_name, field_display_name, field_type, field_order, table_name

docs.title: Example CustomFieldsSchema Object
docs.text:
{
  "id": 1,
  "field_name": "risk_accepted",
  "field_display_name": "Risk Accepted",
  "field_type": "choice",
  "field_metadata": "[\"Yes\", \"No\", \"Pending\"]",
  "field_order": 1,
  "table_name": "vulnerability"
}

docs.title: Field Types
docs.text: Type Description field_metadata Usage str Free-text string value. Not used. int Integer value. Not used. list Array of string values. Not used. choice Single selection from predefined options. JSON-encoded array of option strings (e.g., "[\"High\", \"Medium\", \"Low\"]").

docs.title: Endpoints
docs.text: All endpoints operate under the path prefix /_api/v3/custom_fields_schema. Custom field schemas are global resources and do not require a workspace context.

docs.title: 1. List Custom Fields
docs.text:
Retrieve a list of all defined custom field schemas.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/custom_fields_schema Auth Required

Query Parameters:

Parameter Type Default Description page integer 0 Page number (zero-indexed). page_size integer 20 Number of results per page. sort string field_order Field to sort by. sort_dir string asc Sort direction (asc or desc).

Response: 200 OK

{
  "count": 3,
  "data": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "field_name": "risk_accepted",
      "field_display_name": "Risk Accepted",
      "field_type": "choice",
      "field_metadata": "[\"Yes\", \"No\", \"Pending\"]",
      "field_order": 1,
      "table_name": "vulnerability"
    },
    {
      "id": 2,
      "field_name": "business_unit",
      "field_display_name": "Business Unit",
      "field_type": "str",
      "field_metadata": "",
      "field_order": 2,
      "table_name": "vulnerability"
    }
  ]
}

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/custom_fields_schema" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication.

docs.title: 2. Create Custom Field
docs.text:
Define a new custom field schema.

Method POST Path /_api/v3/custom_fields_schema Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "field_name": "compliance_status",
  "field_display_name": "Compliance Status",
  "field_type": "choice",
  "field_metadata": "[\"Compliant\", \"Non-Compliant\", \"Remediation In Progress\"]",
  "field_order": 3,
  "table_name": "vulnerability"
}

Required fields: field_name, field_display_name, field_type, field_order, table_name

Response: 201 Created

Returns the newly created custom field schema object.

Example:

curl -s -X POST \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/custom_fields_schema" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{
    "field_name": "compliance_status",
    "field_display_name": "Compliance Status",
    "field_type": "choice",
    "field_metadata": "[\"Compliant\", \"Non-Compliant\", \"Remediation In Progress\"]",
    "field_order": 3,
    "table_name": "vulnerability"
  }'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Validation error (e.g., missing required fields, invalid field_type). 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 409 A custom field with this field_name already exists.

docs.title: 3. Get Custom Field
docs.text:
Retrieve a single custom field schema by its ID.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/custom_fields_schema/{object_id} Auth Required

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Description field_id integer Custom field schema ID.

Response: 200 OK

Returns the full custom field schema object.

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/custom_fields_schema/1" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 404 Custom field schema not found.

docs.title: 4. Full Update Custom Field
docs.text:
Replace all mutable fields of a custom field schema. Fields not included in the request body are reset to their defaults.

Method PUT Path /_api/v3/custom_fields_schema/{object_id} Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "field_name": "compliance_status",
  "field_display_name": "Compliance Status (Updated)",
  "field_type": "choice",
  "field_metadata": "[\"Compliant\", \"Non-Compliant\", \"Remediation In Progress\", \"Exception Granted\"]",
  "field_order": 3,
  "table_name": "vulnerability"
}

Response: 200 OK

Returns the updated custom field schema object.

Example:

curl -s -X PUT \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/custom_fields_schema/1" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{
    "field_name": "compliance_status",
    "field_display_name": "Compliance Status (Updated)",
    "field_type": "choice",
    "field_metadata": "[\"Compliant\", \"Non-Compliant\", \"Exception Granted\"]",
    "field_order": 3,
    "table_name": "vulnerability"
  }'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Validation error. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 404 Custom field schema not found. 409 field_name conflict with another custom field.

docs.title: 5. Partial Update Custom Field
docs.text:
Update only the specified fields of a custom field schema. Omitted fields remain unchanged.

Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/custom_fields_schema/{object_id} Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "field_display_name": "Compliance Check Status",
  "field_order": 5
}

Response: 200 OK

Returns the updated custom field schema object.

Example:

curl -s -X PATCH \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/custom_fields_schema/1" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{"field_display_name": "Compliance Check Status", "field_order": 5}'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Validation error. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 404 Custom field schema not found.

docs.title: 6. Delete Custom Field
docs.text:
Delete a single custom field schema.

Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/custom_fields_schema/{object_id} Auth Required

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Description field_id integer Custom field schema ID.

Response: 204 No Content

No response body.

Example:

curl -s -X DELETE \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/custom_fields_schema/1" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 404 Custom field schema not found.

docs.title: 7. Bulk Delete Custom Fields
docs.text:
Delete multiple custom field schemas in a single request.

Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/custom_fields_schema Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "ids": [1, 2, 3]
}

Response: 204 No Content

No response body.

Example:

curl -s -X DELETE \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/custom_fields_schema" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{"ids": [1, 2, 3]}'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Invalid request body or empty ids array. 401 Missing or invalid authentication.

docs.title: Common Error Codes
docs.text: HTTP Status Meaning 200 Success. Response body contains the requested data. 201 Created. New custom field schema was successfully created. 204 No Content. Custom field schema was deleted successfully. 400 Bad Request. Malformed JSON, missing required fields, or invalid field_type. 401 Unauthorized. Authentication token is missing, expired, or invalid. 404 Not Found. The custom field schema or endpoint does not exist. 409 Conflict. A custom field with the same field_name already exists. 500 Internal Server Error. Unexpected server-side failure.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• All curl examples include -k to skip TLS certificate verification for local development. Remove this flag in production environments with valid certificates.
  • Custom field schemas are global and apply to all vulnerabilities across all workspaces.
  • The field_name must be unique and cannot be changed after creation without deleting and recreating the schema.
  • When a custom field schema is deleted, the corresponding data stored in existing vulnerability customfields objects is also removed.
  • The table_name field currently only supports "vulnerability". Future versions may extend custom fields to other resource types.
  • Custom field values on vulnerabilities are stored in the customfields object as key-value pairs where the key is the field_name.
  • For "choice" type fields, the field_metadata must be a JSON-encoded array of strings representing the allowed options.

docs.title: Faraday API Endpoint Reference
docs.text: API Version: v3 Spec Version: Faraday 5.10.0 Base URL: https://<faraday-server>/_api Authentication: Cookie-based session or API token (see [[authentication]]) Content-Type: application/json (unless noted otherwise)

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
All API requests require authentication. Obtain a session token first:

# Login (cookie-based session)
curl -s -c cookies.txt -X POST https://localhost:5985/_api/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email": "faraday", "password": "changeme"}'

# Or use an API token
TOKEN=$(curl -s -b cookies.txt https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/token | jq -r '.token')

# Subsequent requests with token
curl -s -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws

docs.title: API Overview
docs.text:
# Endpoint Group Base Path Endpoints Doc Page 1 [[authentication]] /_api/login, /_api/session, /v3/token 7 Authentication & Session 2 [[workspaces]] /v3/ws 12 Workspaces 3 [[hosts]] /v3/hosts, /v3/ws/{ws}/hosts 25 Hosts 4 [[services]] /v3/services, /v3/ws/{ws}/services 16 Services 5 [[vulnerabilities]] /v3/vulns, /v3/ws/{ws}/vulns 32 Vulnerabilities 6 [[credentials]] /v3/ws/{ws}/credential 11 Credentials 7 [[comments]] /v3/ws/{ws}/comment 9 Comments 8 [[commands]] /v3/ws/{ws}/commands, /v3/global_commands 11 Commands 9 [[agents]] /v3/agents, /v3/agent_token 12 Agents 11 [[agent-schedules]] /v3/agents_schedule 9 Agent Schedules 12 [[pipelines-jobs-tasks]] /v3/pipelines, /v3/jobs, /v3/tasks 33 Pipelines, Jobs & Tasks 13 [[vulnerability-templates]] /v3/vulnerability_template 10 Vulnerability Templates 14 [[custom-fields]] /v3/custom_fields_schema 7 Custom Fields 15 [[search-filters]] /v3/searchfilter 8 Search Filters 16 [[settings]] /v3/settings/{module} 8 Settings 17 [[licenses]] /v3/licenses 6 Licenses 18 [[bulk-operations]] /v3/ws/{ws}/bulk_create, bulk delete/update 5+ Bulk Operations 19 [[export-upload]] /v3/ws/{ws}/export_data, /v3/ws/{ws}/upload_report 4 Export & Upload 20 [[system-info]] /v3/info, /_api/config, /v3/preferences 4 System Info 21 [[activities]] /v3/ws/{ws}/activities 7 Activities

Total: 232 endpoint definitions across 136 unique paths (27 resource tags)

docs.title: Authentication Headers
docs.text:
All endpoints (except /_api/login, /_api/config, and agent self-registration) require one of:

Method Header/Mechanism Session cookie Cookie: session=<value> (set by /_api/login) API token Authorization: Token <base64-token> Agent token Authorization: Agent <agent-token> (agent endpoints only)

docs.title: Pagination
docs.text:
List endpoints support pagination via query parameters:

Parameter Type Default Description page integer 0 Page number (0-indexed) page_size integer 20 Items per page sort string — Field to sort by sort_dir string asc Sort direction (asc or desc)

docs.title: Filtering
docs.text: Resources with filter support expose GET .../filter endpoints that accept complex filter expressions via query parameters. See Filters documentation for the full filter query syntax.

docs.title: Bulk Operations
docs.text:
Most resources support bulk operations:

Operation Method URL Body Bulk delete DELETE /{resource} Resource list URL {"ids": [1, 2, 3]} Bulk update PATCH /{resource} Resource list URL {"ids": [1, 2], "update": {"field": "value"}}

docs.title: Standard Response Codes
docs.text: Code Meaning 200 Success (GET, PUT, PATCH) 201 Created (POST) 204 Deleted (DELETE) 400 Bad request / validation error 401 Unauthorized (not logged in) 403 Forbidden (insufficient permissions) 404 Resource not found 409 Conflict (duplicate resource) 500 Server error

docs.title: Standard Error Response
docs.text:
{
  "messages": ["Error description here"]
}

docs.title: Version History
docs.text:
Spec Version Changes 5.10.0 Added Agent Executions, global hosts/services/vulns, settings modules, credential filter/CSV import, workspace activate/deactivate 5.10.0 Previous published version — missing 39+ paths added in 5.11–5.17

Note: The published Swagger UI at docs.faradaysec.com/api-swagger/ should be updated from 5.10.0 to 5.10.0. The api.faradaysec.com domain is defunct (NXDOMAIN).

docs.title: Export & Upload API
docs.text:
Base URL: https://localhost:5985 API Version: v3 Authentication: Cookie-based session or API token (Authorization: Token <token>) Content-Type: application/json (unless noted otherwise)

The Export & Upload API provides endpoints for exporting workspace data and importing tool reports. These endpoints enable integration with external tools and workflows, allowing data to be extracted for offline analysis or compliance reporting, and scan results to be ingested from 120+ supported security tools.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• Endpoints
  • Export Workspace as XML
  • Upload Tool Report
  • Export Vulnerabilities as CSV (Global)
  • Export Vulnerabilities as CSV (Workspaced)
  • Common Error Codes

docs.title: 1. Export Workspace as XML
docs.text:
Export the entire contents of a workspace (hosts, services, vulnerabilities, commands) as a Faraday XML file. This is useful for creating backups, migrating data between Faraday instances, or offline analysis.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/export_data Auth Required Content-Type Response: application/xml

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Description workspace_name string Workspace slug to export.

Response: 200 OK

The response body is an XML file download containing all workspace data. The Content-Disposition header is set to trigger a file download.

Response Headers:

Header Value Content-Type application/xml Content-Disposition attachment; filename="{workspace_name}.xml"

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/export_data" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k \
  -o pentest_q1.xml

Example XML Structure:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<faraday_export workspace="pentest_q1">
  <hosts>
    <host>
      <id>42</id>
      <ip>192.168.1.1</ip>
      <description>Web server</description>
      <os>Linux 5.15</os>
      <services>
        <service>
          <port>80</port>
          <protocol>tcp</protocol>
          <name>http</name>
          <status>open</status>
        </service>
      </services>
      <vulnerabilities>
        <vulnerability>
          <name>SQL Injection</name>
          <severity>critical</severity>
          <status>open</status>
        </vulnerability>
      </vulnerabilities>
    </host>
  </hosts>
  <commands>
    <command>
      <tool>nmap</tool>
      <command_string>nmap -sV 192.168.1.0/24</command_string>
    </command>
  </commands>
</faraday_export>

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission for this workspace. 404 Workspace not found.

docs.title: 2. Upload Tool Report
docs.text:
Upload a security tool report file for parsing and import into a workspace. Faraday's plugin engine automatically detects the tool format and extracts hosts, services, and vulnerabilities from the report. This endpoint supports 120+ plugin formats including Nmap, Nessus, Burp Suite, Qualys, OpenVAS, OWASP ZAP, Nuclei, and many more.

Method POST Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/upload_report Auth Required Content-Type multipart/form-data

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Description workspace_name string Target workspace slug for the imported data.

Form Fields:

Field Type Required Description file file yes The report file to upload. Supported formats include XML, JSON, CSV, HTML, and tool-specific formats.

Response: 201 Created

{
  "command_id": 25
}

The command_id identifies the Command record created for this import. Use it to track the import via the Commands API (GET /_api/v3/ws/{ws}/commands/{command_id}).

Example (Nmap XML report):

curl -s -X POST \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/upload_report" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k \
  -F "file=@/path/to/nmap_scan.xml"

Example (Nessus report):

curl -s -X POST \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/upload_report" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k \
  -F "file=@/path/to/nessus_report.nessus"

Example (Burp Suite report):

curl -s -X POST \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/upload_report" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k \
  -F "file=@/path/to/burp_report.xml"

Supported Tool Formats (partial list):

Category Tools Network Scanners Nmap, Masscan, Zmap Vulnerability Scanners Nessus, OpenVAS, Qualys, Nexpose, Retina Web Application Scanners Burp Suite, OWASP ZAP, Nikto, Acunetix, AppScan, w3af DAST/SAST Tools Checkmarx, Fortify, SonarQube, Veracode, Snyk Exploitation Frameworks Metasploit, Core Impact Reconnaissance Shodan, Fierce, DNSrecon, theHarvester Compliance Lynis, CIS-CAT Cloud Security ScoutSuite, Prowler Container Security Trivy, Docker Bench Custom Nuclei, SQLMap, WPScan, SSLyze, testssl.sh

For the full list of 120+ supported plugins, see the Faraday Plugins documentation.

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 No file provided, unrecognized file format, or file parsing error. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission for this workspace. 404 Workspace not found.

Notes:

  • The upload is processed synchronously. Large reports may take several seconds to parse.
  • If the plugin cannot identify the report format, a 400 error is returned with a message indicating the format was not recognized.
  • Duplicate hosts, services, and vulnerabilities are merged automatically using the same deduplication logic as the Bulk Create endpoint.

docs.title: 3. Export Vulnerabilities as CSV (Global)
docs.text:
Export vulnerabilities across all accessible workspaces as a CSV file. This is useful for generating compliance reports or importing vulnerability data into external tools like spreadsheets or SIEM systems.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/vulns/export_csv Auth Required Content-Type Response: text/csv

Query Parameters:

Parameter Type Default Description q string (JSON) (none) Optional filter expression (same format as filter endpoints).

Response: 200 OK

The response body is a CSV file download containing vulnerability data.

Response Headers:

Header Value Content-Type text/csv Content-Disposition attachment; filename="vulnerabilities.csv"

CSV Columns:

Column Description id Vulnerability ID. name Vulnerability name/title. severity Severity level. status Current status (open, closed, re-opened, risk-accepted). description Full description text. resolution Remediation guidance. host_ip IP address of the associated host. host_os Operating system of the associated host. service_name Name of the associated service (if applicable). service_port Port of the associated service (if applicable). workspace Workspace name. references Semicolon-separated list of references. cwe Semicolon-separated list of CWE identifiers. cve Semicolon-separated list of CVE identifiers. date Creation date.

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulns/export_csv" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k \
  -o all_vulnerabilities.csv

Example (with filter):

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulns/export_csv?q=%7B%22filters%22%3A%5B%7B%22name%22%3A%22severity%22%2C%22op%22%3A%22eq%22%2C%22val%22%3A%22critical%22%7D%5D%7D" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k \
  -o critical_vulnerabilities.csv

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Malformed filter expression. 401 Missing or invalid authentication.

docs.title: 4. Export Vulnerabilities as CSV (Workspaced)
docs.text:
Export vulnerabilities from a specific workspace as a CSV file.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/vulns/export_csv Auth Required Content-Type Response: text/csv

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Description workspace_name string Workspace slug to export from.

Query Parameters:

Parameter Type Default Description q string (JSON) (none) Optional filter expression (same format as filter endpoints).

Response: 200 OK

The response body is a CSV file download containing vulnerability data from the specified workspace. The CSV format and columns are identical to the global CSV export.

Response Headers:

Header Value Content-Type text/csv Content-Disposition attachment; filename="{workspace_name}_vulnerabilities.csv"

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/vulns/export_csv" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k \
  -o pentest_q1_vulnerabilities.csv

Example (with filter -- only high and critical):

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/vulns/export_csv?q=%7B%22filters%22%3A%5B%7B%22name%22%3A%22severity%22%2C%22op%22%3A%22in%22%2C%22val%22%3A%5B%22high%22%2C%22critical%22%5D%7D%5D%7D" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k \
  -o pentest_q1_high_critical.csv

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Malformed filter expression. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission for this workspace. 404 Workspace not found.

docs.title: Common Error Codes
docs.text: HTTP Status Meaning 200 Success. Response body contains the exported data or import confirmation. 400 Bad Request. Invalid file, unrecognized format, malformed filter, or missing required fields. 401 Unauthorized. Authentication token is missing, expired, or invalid. 403 Forbidden. Authenticated user does not have permission for the requested workspace. 404 Not Found. The workspace does not exist. 500 Internal Server Error. Unexpected server-side failure (e.g., report parsing crash).

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• All curl examples include -k to skip TLS certificate verification for local development. Remove this flag in production environments with valid certificates.
  • The XML export (export_data) includes all hosts, services, vulnerabilities, and commands in the workspace. For large workspaces this can produce a very large file.
  • The report upload endpoint (upload_report) uses multipart/form-data encoding, not application/json. Ensure the file field name is file.
  • CSV exports include all vulnerabilities matching the optional filter. Without a filter, all vulnerabilities are exported.
  • CSV exports support the same filter syntax used by the GET .../vulns/filter endpoints. The filter expression must be URL-encoded when passed as the q query parameter.
  • The command_id returned by upload_report can be used to query the Commands API to check import status and see how many hosts, services, and vulnerabilities were created.
  • Report processing applies the same deduplication logic as the Bulk Create API: existing hosts are updated, duplicate services are merged, and vulnerability duplicates are handled based on name/description/host/service matching.

docs.title: Hosts API
docs.text:
The Hosts API manages network hosts (assets) discovered during security assessments. Hosts are the fundamental unit of asset inventory in Faraday and serve as the parent object for services and vulnerabilities.

All endpoints require authentication via API token (Authorization: Token <token>) or session cookie.

Base URL: https://localhost:5985

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• Host Schema
  • HostCount Schema
  • Workspaced Endpoints
  • List Hosts
  • Create Host
  • Get Host
  • Update Host (Full)
  • Update Host (Partial)
  • Delete Host
  • Bulk Delete Hosts
  • Bulk Update Hosts
  • Count Hosts
  • Count Hosts Multi-Workspace
  • Filter Hosts
  • List Host Services
  • Count Vulnerabilities per Host
  • Tool Impact History
  • Bulk Create Hosts
  • Global (Non-Workspaced) Endpoints
  • List All Hosts
  • Get Host (Global)
  • Filter Hosts (Global)
  • List Host Services (Global)
  • Count Vulnerabilities per Host (Global)
  • Tool Impact History (Global)
  • Bulk Delete Hosts (Global)
  • Bulk Update Hosts (Global)
  • Count Hosts (Global)
  • Count Hosts Multi-Workspace (Global)
  • Deprecated Endpoints
  • Common Error Codes

docs.title: Host Schema
docs.text:
Property Type Required Description _id integer read-only Internal document ID. _rev string read-only Document revision identifier. command_id integer write-only ID of the command that created this host. Accepted on input but not returned in responses. credentials integer read-only Count of credentials associated with this host. default_gateway string (nullable) no Default gateway address. description string yes Free-text description of the host. hostnames array of strings no DNS hostnames associated with the host. id integer read-only Primary key. Same as _id. importance integer (0--10) no Asset importance rating. 0 = unrated, 10 = critical. ip string no IPv4 or IPv6 address. mac string no MAC address. metadata object read-only Timestamps and user-tracking metadata (create_time, update_time, update_user, etc.). name string read-only Display name. Auto-populated by the server. os string no Operating system fingerprint. owned boolean no Whether the host has been "owned" (compromised) during the assessment. owner string read-only Username of the creator. services integer read-only Count of services discovered on this host. tags array no Tags associated with this host. vulns integer read-only Count of vulnerabilities associated with this host. type string read-only Always "Host". service_summaries array read-only Summary objects for each service (name, port, protocol). severity_counts object read-only Vulnerability counts grouped by severity (critical, high, med, low, info, unclassified). versions array read-only Detected software versions.

Required fields for creation: description

docs.title: Example Host Object
docs.text:
{
  "_id": 42,
  "_rev": "",
  "command_id": null,
  "default_gateway": [],
  "description": "Primary web server",
  "hostnames": ["web.example.com", "www.example.com"],
  "id": 42,
  "importance": 7,
  "ip": "192.168.1.1",
  "mac": "00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E",
  "metadata": {
    "create_time": "2026-02-27T10:30:00+00:00",
    "creator": "admin",
    "owner": "admin",
    "update_action": 0,
    "update_time": "2026-02-27T12:15:00+00:00",
    "update_user": "admin"
  },
  "name": "192.168.1.1",
  "os": "Linux 5.15",
  "owned": false,
  "owner": "admin",
  "services": 3,
  "vulns": 12,
  "type": "Host",
  "service_summaries": [
    "ssh (22/tcp)",
    "http (80/tcp)",
    "https (443/tcp)"
  ],
  "severity_counts": {
    "critical": 1,
    "high": 3,
    "med": 4,
    "low": 2,
    "info": 2,
    "unclassified": 0
  },
  "versions": [],
  "workspace_name": "pentest_q1"
}

docs.title: HostCount Schema
docs.text:
Returned by the countVulns endpoints.

Property Type Description host_id integer Host primary key. critical integer Count of critical-severity vulnerabilities. high integer Count of high-severity vulnerabilities. med integer Count of medium-severity vulnerabilities. low integer Count of low-severity vulnerabilities. info integer Count of informational-severity vulnerabilities. unclassified integer Count of unclassified vulnerabilities. total integer Total vulnerability count.

docs.title: Workspaced Endpoints
docs.text: All workspaced endpoints operate under the path prefix /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/hosts. Replace {workspace_name} with the target workspace slug.

docs.title: List Hosts
docs.text:
Retrieve a paginated list of all hosts in a workspace.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/hosts Auth Required

Query Parameters:

Parameter Type Default Description page integer 0 Page number (zero-indexed). page_size integer 20 Number of results per page. sort string ip Field to sort by. sort_dir string asc Sort direction (asc or desc).

Response: 200 OK

{
  "count": 150,
  "data": [
    { "...host object..." }
  ]
}

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/hosts?page=0&page_size=10" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission for this workspace. 404 Workspace not found.

docs.title: Create Host
docs.text:
Create a new host in the specified workspace.

Method POST Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/hosts Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "ip": "192.168.1.1",
  "description": "Web server",
  "hostnames": ["web.example.com"],
  "os": "Linux",
  "mac": "00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E",
  "importance": 5,
  "owned": false,
  "default_gateway": []
}

Required fields: description

Response: 201 Created

Returns the newly created host object.

Example:

curl -s -X POST \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/hosts" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{
    "ip": "192.168.1.1",
    "description": "Web server",
    "hostnames": ["web.example.com"],
    "os": "Linux"
  }'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Validation error (e.g., missing description). 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission for this workspace. 404 Workspace not found. 409 Host with this IP already exists in the workspace.

docs.title: Get Host
docs.text:
Retrieve a single host by its ID.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/hosts/{object_id} Auth Required

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Description workspace_name string Workspace slug. object_id integer Host ID.

Response: 200 OK

Returns the full host object.

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/hosts/42" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission for this workspace. 404 Host or workspace not found.

docs.title: Update Host (Full)
docs.text:
Replace all mutable fields of a host. Fields not included in the request body are reset to their defaults.

Method PUT Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/hosts/{object_id} Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "ip": "192.168.1.1",
  "description": "Updated web server",
  "hostnames": ["web.example.com", "api.example.com"],
  "os": "Linux 5.15",
  "mac": "00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E",
  "importance": 8,
  "owned": true,
  "default_gateway": ["192.168.1.254"]
}

Response: 200 OK

Returns the updated host object.

Example:

curl -s -X PUT \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/hosts/42" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{
    "ip": "192.168.1.1",
    "description": "Updated web server",
    "hostnames": ["web.example.com", "api.example.com"],
    "os": "Linux 5.15",
    "importance": 8,
    "owned": true
  }'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Validation error. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission for this workspace. 404 Host or workspace not found. 409 IP conflict with another host in the workspace.

docs.title: Update Host (Partial)
docs.text:
Update only the specified fields of a host. Omitted fields remain unchanged.

Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/hosts/{object_id} Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "importance": 10,
  "owned": true
}

Response: 200 OK

Returns the updated host object.

Example:

curl -s -X PATCH \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/hosts/42" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{"importance": 10, "owned": true}'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Validation error. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission for this workspace. 404 Host or workspace not found.

docs.title: Delete Host
docs.text:
Delete a single host and all of its associated services and vulnerabilities.

Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/hosts/{object_id} Auth Required

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Description workspace_name string Workspace slug. object_id integer Host ID.

Response: 204 No Content

No response body.

Example:

curl -s -X DELETE \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/hosts/42" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission for this workspace. 404 Host or workspace not found.

docs.title: Bulk Delete Hosts
docs.text:
Delete multiple hosts in a single request.

Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/hosts Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "ids": [1, 2, 3]
}

Response: 204 No Content

No response body on success.

Example:

curl -s -X DELETE \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/hosts" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{"ids": [1, 2, 3]}'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Invalid request body or empty ids array. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission for this workspace. 404 Workspace not found.

docs.title: Bulk Update Hosts
docs.text:
Update multiple hosts with the same set of field values.

Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/hosts Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "ids": [1, 2],
  "update": {
    "owned": true,
    "importance": 9
  }
}

Response: 200 OK

Returns a count of updated objects.

{
  "updated": 2
}

Example:

curl -s -X PATCH \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/hosts" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{"ids": [1, 2], "update": {"owned": true}}'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Invalid request body, empty ids, or invalid fields in update. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission for this workspace. 404 Workspace not found.

docs.title: Count Hosts
docs.text:
Return the total number of hosts in a workspace, optionally grouped by a field.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/hosts/count Auth Required

Query Parameters:

Parameter Type Default Description group_by string (none) Field to group counts by (e.g., os, owned, importance).

Response: 200 OK

Without group_by:

{
  "count": 150
}

With group_by=os:

{
  "groups": [
    {"count": 80, "os": "Linux"},
    {"count": 45, "os": "Windows"},
    {"count": 25, "os": ""}
  ]
}

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/hosts/count?group_by=os" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission for this workspace. 404 Workspace not found.

docs.title: Count Hosts Multi-Workspace
docs.text:
Return host counts across multiple workspaces.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/hosts/count_multi_workspace Auth Required

Response: 200 OK

{
  "workspaces": {
    "pentest_q1": 150,
    "pentest_q2": 87
  }
}

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/hosts/count_multi_workspace" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission.

docs.title: Filter Hosts
docs.text:
Retrieve hosts matching a filter expression.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/hosts/filter Auth Required

Query Parameters:

Parameter Type Description q string (JSON) Filter expression. See Filter Syntax below. page integer Page number (zero-indexed). page_size integer Results per page.

Response: 200 OK

Same paginated format as List Hosts.

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/hosts/filter?q=%7B%22filters%22%3A%5B%7B%22name%22%3A%22os%22%2C%22op%22%3A%22like%22%2C%22val%22%3A%22%25Linux%25%22%7D%5D%7D" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Decoded q parameter:

{
  "filters": [
    {"name": "os", "op": "like", "val": "%Linux%"}
  ]
}

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Malformed filter expression. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission for this workspace. 404 Workspace not found.

docs.title: Filter Syntax
docs.text:
Filters use a JSON structure with an array of filter objects:

{
  "filters": [
    {"name": "<field>", "op": "<operator>", "val": "<value>"}
  ],
  "conjunction": "AND"
}

Supported operators: eq, ne, lt, le, gt, ge, like, ilike, in, not_in, is_null, is_not_null

docs.title: List Host Services
docs.text:
Retrieve all services associated with a specific host.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/hosts/{host_id}/services Auth Required

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Description workspace_name string Workspace slug. host_id integer Host ID.

Response: 200 OK

{
  "count": 3,
  "data": [
    {
      "id": 101,
      "name": "ssh",
      "port": 22,
      "protocol": "tcp",
      "status": "open",
      "version": "OpenSSH 8.9",
      "description": "",
      "host_id": 42
    }
  ]
}

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/hosts/42/services" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission for this workspace. 404 Host or workspace not found.

docs.title: Count Vulnerabilities per Host
docs.text:
Return vulnerability counts grouped by severity for hosts.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/hosts/countVulns Auth Required

Response: 200 OK

Returns an array of HostCount objects.

[
  {
    "host_id": 42,
    "critical": 1,
    "high": 3,
    "med": 4,
    "low": 2,
    "info": 2,
    "unclassified": 0,
    "total": 12
  },
  {
    "host_id": 43,
    "critical": 0,
    "high": 1,
    "med": 2,
    "low": 5,
    "info": 3,
    "unclassified": 0,
    "total": 11
  }
]

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/hosts/countVulns" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission for this workspace. 404 Workspace not found.

docs.title: Tool Impact History
docs.text:
Retrieve the history of tools (commands) that have interacted with a specific host.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/hosts/{host_id}/tools_history Auth Required

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Description workspace_name string Workspace slug. host_id integer Host ID.

Response: 200 OK

[
  {
    "command_id": 5,
    "command": "nmap -sV 192.168.1.1",
    "tool": "nmap",
    "user": "admin",
    "date": "2026-02-27T10:30:00+00:00",
    "hosts_count": 1,
    "services_count": 3,
    "vulns_count": 5
  }
]

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/hosts/42/tools_history" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission for this workspace. 404 Host or workspace not found.

docs.title: Bulk Create Hosts
docs.text:
Create multiple hosts in a single request.

Method POST Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/hosts/bulk_create Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "hosts": [
    {
      "ip": "192.168.1.10",
      "description": "Database server",
      "os": "Linux",
      "hostnames": ["db.example.com"]
    },
    {
      "ip": "192.168.1.11",
      "description": "Application server",
      "os": "Linux",
      "hostnames": ["app.example.com"]
    }
  ]
}

Response: 201 Created

{
  "created": 2,
  "hosts": [
    { "...host object..." },
    { "...host object..." }
  ]
}

Example:

curl -s -X POST \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest_q1/hosts/bulk_create" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{
    "hosts": [
      {"ip": "192.168.1.10", "description": "Database server", "os": "Linux"},
      {"ip": "192.168.1.11", "description": "App server", "os": "Linux"}
    ]
  }'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Validation error in one or more host objects. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission for this workspace. 404 Workspace not found. 409 One or more hosts conflict with existing IPs.

docs.title: Global (Non-Workspaced) Endpoints
docs.text:
Important: These global host endpoints are not declared in the published 5.10.0 OpenAPI specification (faraday_swagger.json). They exist in the codebase spec for version 5.17.0+. The paths and behaviors described below are derived from the codebase and may not be available in all Faraday deployments.

Global endpoints operate under the path prefix /_api/v3/hosts and provide read-only cross-workspace access (with the exception of bulk delete and bulk update). No {workspace_name} is required.

docs.title: List All Hosts
docs.text:
Retrieve a paginated list of hosts across all accessible workspaces.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/hosts Auth Required

Query Parameters:

Parameter Type Default Description page integer 0 Page number (zero-indexed). page_size integer 20 Number of results per page. sort string ip Field to sort by. sort_dir string asc Sort direction (asc or desc).

Response: 200 OK

{
  "count": 500,
  "data": [
    { "...host object with workspace_name..." }
  ]
}

Each host object includes workspace_name to identify which workspace it belongs to.

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/hosts?page=0&page_size=50" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication.

docs.title: Get Host (Global)
docs.text:
Retrieve a single host by ID without specifying a workspace.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/hosts/{object_id} Auth Required

Response: 200 OK

Returns the full host object including workspace_name.

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/hosts/42" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission for the host's workspace. 404 Host not found.

docs.title: Filter Hosts (Global)
docs.text:
Filter hosts across all accessible workspaces.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/hosts/filter Auth Required

Query Parameters:

Same as Filter Hosts (workspaced version). Supports q, page, and page_size.

Response: 200 OK

Same paginated format as List All Hosts.

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/hosts/filter?q=%7B%22filters%22%3A%5B%7B%22name%22%3A%22os%22%2C%22op%22%3A%22like%22%2C%22val%22%3A%22%25Windows%25%22%7D%5D%7D" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Malformed filter expression. 401 Missing or invalid authentication.

docs.title: List Host Services (Global)
docs.text:
Retrieve services for a host without specifying a workspace.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/hosts/{host_id}/services Auth Required

Response: 200 OK

Same format as List Host Services.

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/hosts/42/services" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission for the host's workspace. 404 Host not found.

docs.title: Count Vulnerabilities per Host (Global)
docs.text:
Return vulnerability counts grouped by severity across all accessible workspaces.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/hosts/countVulns Auth Required

Response: 200 OK

Returns an array of HostCount objects.

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/hosts/countVulns" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication.

docs.title: Tool Impact History (Global)
docs.text:
Retrieve tool history for a host without specifying a workspace.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/hosts/{host_id}/tools_history Auth Required

Response: 200 OK

Same format as Tool Impact History.

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/hosts/42/tools_history" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission for the host's workspace. 404 Host not found.

docs.title: Bulk Delete Hosts (Global)
docs.text:
Delete multiple hosts across workspaces.

Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/hosts Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "ids": [1, 2, 3]
}

Response: 200 OK

{
  "deleted": 3
}

Example:

curl -s -X DELETE \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/hosts" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{"ids": [1, 2, 3]}'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Invalid request body or empty ids array. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission for one or more host workspaces.

docs.title: Bulk Update Hosts (Global)
docs.text:
Update multiple hosts across workspaces with the same field values.

Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/hosts Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "ids": [1, 2],
  "update": {
    "owned": true
  }
}

Response: 200 OK

{
  "updated": 2
}

Example:

curl -s -X PATCH \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/hosts" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{"ids": [1, 2], "update": {"owned": true}}'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Invalid request body, empty ids, or invalid fields in update. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks permission for one or more host workspaces.

docs.title: Count Hosts (Global)
docs.text:
Return the total number of hosts across all accessible workspaces.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/hosts/count Auth Required

Query Parameters:

Parameter Type Default Description group_by string (none) Field to group counts by.

Response: 200 OK

Same format as Count Hosts.

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/hosts/count" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication.

docs.title: Count Hosts Multi-Workspace (Global)
docs.text:
Return host counts broken down by workspace.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/hosts/count_multi_workspace Auth Required

Response: 200 OK

{
  "workspaces": {
    "pentest_q1": 150,
    "pentest_q2": 87,
    "internal_audit": 42
  }
}

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/hosts/count_multi_workspace" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication.

docs.title: Deprecated Endpoints
docs.text:
Deprecated Path Replacement

The legacy bulk_delete path is intentionally blocked via SKIP_RULES. Any requests to it will return 404. Use the standard DELETE on the collection endpoint with an ids array in the request body instead.

docs.title: Common Error Codes
docs.text: HTTP Status Meaning 200 Success. Response body contains the requested data. 201 Created. New resource was successfully created. 204 No Content. Resource was deleted successfully. 400 Bad Request. Malformed JSON, missing required fields, or invalid filter expression. 401 Unauthorized. Authentication token is missing, expired, or invalid. 403 Forbidden. Authenticated user does not have permission for the requested workspace or resource. 404 Not Found. The workspace, host, or endpoint does not exist. 409 Conflict. A host with the same IP already exists in the workspace. 500 Internal Server Error. Unexpected server-side failure.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
All requests must include one of the following:

API Token (recommended):

Authorization: Token <your_api_token>

Obtain a token from the Faraday web UI under Settings > API Tokens, or via:

curl -s -X POST \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/token" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{"username": "faraday", "password": "changeme"}'

Session Cookie:

Authenticate via the login endpoint and pass the session cookie with subsequent requests.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• All curl examples include -k to skip TLS certificate verification for local development. Remove this flag in production environments with valid certificates.
  • Pagination is zero-indexed: page=0 returns the first page.
  • The importance field accepts values from 0 (unrated) to 10 (critical asset).
  • Host IPs must be unique within a workspace. Attempting to create a duplicate returns 409 Conflict.
  • Deleting a host cascades to its services and vulnerabilities.

docs.title: Licenses API
docs.text:
Base URL: https://localhost:5985 API Version: v3 Authentication: Cookie-based session or API token (Authorization: Token <token>) Content-Type: application/json

The Licenses API manages software license records associated with assets discovered during security assessments. Licenses track commercial software products, their validity periods, and license types. This endpoint is useful for organizations that need to maintain an inventory of software licenses alongside their vulnerability management workflow.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• License Schema
  • Endpoints
  • List Licenses
  • Create License
  • Get License
  • Full Update License
  • Partial Update License
  • Delete License
  • Common Error Codes

docs.title: License Schema
docs.text:
Property Type Required Description id integer read-only Primary key. _id integer read-only Internal document ID. Same as id. product string yes Name of the licensed software product. start date no License start date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., "2026-01-01"). end date no License expiration date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., "2027-01-01"). lictype string no License type (e.g., "commercial", "trial", "open-source", "enterprise"). Free-form text. notes string no Additional notes about the license.

Required fields for creation: product

docs.title: Example License Object
docs.text:
{
  "id": 1,
  "_id": 1,
  "product": "Nessus Professional",
  "start": "2026-01-01",
  "end": "2027-01-01",
  "lictype": "commercial",
  "notes": "Annual subscription. Renewal contact: sales@tenable.com"
}

docs.title: Endpoints
docs.text: All endpoints operate under the path prefix /_api/v3/licenses. Licenses are global resources and do not require a workspace context.

docs.title: 1. List Licenses
docs.text:
Retrieve a paginated list of all license records.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/licenses Auth Required

Query Parameters:

Parameter Type Default Description page integer 0 Page number (zero-indexed). page_size integer 20 Number of results per page. sort string product Field to sort by. sort_dir string asc Sort direction (asc or desc).

Response: 200 OK

{
  "count": 3,
  "data": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "_id": 1,
      "product": "Burp Suite Professional",
      "start": "2026-01-15",
      "end": "2027-01-15",
      "lictype": "commercial",
      "notes": "Team license, 5 seats"
    },
    {
      "id": 2,
      "_id": 2,
      "product": "Nessus Professional",
      "start": "2026-01-01",
      "end": "2027-01-01",
      "lictype": "commercial",
      "notes": ""
    }
  ]
}

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/licenses?page=0&page_size=10" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication.

docs.title: 2. Create License
docs.text:
Create a new license record.

Method POST Path /_api/v3/licenses Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "product": "Metasploit Pro",
  "start": "2026-03-01",
  "end": "2027-03-01",
  "lictype": "commercial",
  "notes": "Purchased via Rapid7 reseller"
}

Required fields: product

Response: 201 Created

Returns the newly created license object.

Example:

curl -s -X POST \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/licenses" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{
    "product": "Metasploit Pro",
    "start": "2026-03-01",
    "end": "2027-03-01",
    "lictype": "commercial",
    "notes": "Purchased via Rapid7 reseller"
  }'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Validation error (e.g., missing product, invalid date format). 401 Missing or invalid authentication.

docs.title: 3. Get License
docs.text:
Retrieve a single license record by its ID.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/licenses/{object_id} Auth Required

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Description license_id integer License ID.

Response: 200 OK

Returns the full license object.

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/licenses/1" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 404 License not found.

docs.title: 4. Full Update License
docs.text:
Replace all mutable fields of a license. Fields not included in the request body are reset to their defaults.

Method PUT Path /_api/v3/licenses/{object_id} Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "product": "Metasploit Pro",
  "start": "2026-03-01",
  "end": "2028-03-01",
  "lictype": "enterprise",
  "notes": "Renewed for 2 years. PO #12345."
}

Response: 200 OK

Returns the updated license object.

Example:

curl -s -X PUT \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/licenses/1" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{
    "product": "Metasploit Pro",
    "start": "2026-03-01",
    "end": "2028-03-01",
    "lictype": "enterprise",
    "notes": "Renewed for 2 years. PO #12345."
  }'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Validation error. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 404 License not found.

docs.title: 5. Partial Update License
docs.text:
Update only the specified fields of a license. Omitted fields remain unchanged.

Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/licenses/{object_id} Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "end": "2028-03-01",
  "notes": "Extended for an additional year."
}

Response: 200 OK

Returns the updated license object.

Example:

curl -s -X PATCH \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/licenses/1" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{"end": "2028-03-01", "notes": "Extended for an additional year."}'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Validation error. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 404 License not found.

docs.title: 6. Delete License
docs.text:
Delete a single license record.

Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/licenses/{object_id} Auth Required

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Description license_id integer License ID.

Response: 204 No Content

No response body.

Example:

curl -s -X DELETE \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/licenses/1" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 404 License not found.

docs.title: Common Error Codes
docs.text: HTTP Status Meaning 200 Success. Response body contains the requested data. 201 Created. New license was successfully created. 204 No Content. License was deleted successfully. 400 Bad Request. Malformed JSON, missing required fields, or invalid date format. 401 Unauthorized. Authentication token is missing, expired, or invalid. 404 Not Found. The license or endpoint does not exist. 500 Internal Server Error. Unexpected server-side failure.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• All curl examples include -k to skip TLS certificate verification for local development. Remove this flag in production environments with valid certificates.
  • Pagination is zero-indexed: page=0 returns the first page.
  • Licenses are global resources and not scoped to workspaces.
  • The start and end date fields accept ISO 8601 date format (YYYY-MM-DD). Time components are ignored.
  • The lictype field is free-form text. There is no enforced list of valid license types.
  • The Licenses API does not currently support bulk delete or bulk update operations.

docs.title: OpenAPI/Swagger Specification Audit & Comparison
docs.text: Generated: 2026-02-27 Phase: 05 — API & Reference Documentation Applies to: Faraday v5.17.0 (codebase spec) vs v5.10.0 (published wiki spec) Latest release: v5.19.0

docs.title: Executive Summary
docs.text: The Faraday API has two divergent OpenAPI specs: the codebase version (5.17.0) and the published docs version (5.10.0). Neither is complete. The published spec is 7 minor versions behind and missing critical new features, while the codebase spec is missing enterprise/commercial endpoints. Additionally, the api.faradaysec.com domain is defunct (NXDOMAIN/ECONNREFUSED).

docs.title: Key Metrics
docs.text: Metric Codebase Spec (5.17.0) Published Wiki Spec (5.10.0) OpenAPI version 3.0.2 3.0.2 Product version 5.17.0 5.10.0 Total unique paths 136 202 Endpoints (method+path) 232 332 Tags (resource groups) 29 42 Component schemas 35 51 Server URL http://localhost:5985 http://localhost:5985/_api Common paths 0 (different prefix conventions)

docs.title: Severity Summary
docs.text: Issue Severity api.faradaysec.com domain defunct CRITICAL Published spec 7 versions behind CRITICAL Zero shared path prefix (incompatible specs) HIGH Codebase spec missing Flask-Security auth endpoints HIGH Wiki spec missing newer features (agent exec, filters, etc.) HIGH Both specs behind latest release (5.19.0) MEDIUM

docs.title: 1. Metadata Comparison
docs.text:
Field Codebase (GitLab) Published (Wiki) Title Faraday 5.17.0 API Faraday 5.10.0 API API version v3 v3 OpenAPI version 3.0.2 3.0.2 Server URL http://localhost:5985 http://localhost:5985/_api File location repos/faraday-gitlab/faraday/openapi/faraday_swagger.json repos/faraday-wiki/docs/files/faraday_swagger.json File size 308 KB ~350 KB

Key difference: The server URL base path differs. The codebase spec uses the full /_api prefix in each path (e.g., /_api/v3/agents), while the wiki spec omits it and puts /_api in the server URL. This means there are zero common paths when comparing literally, even though they describe the same API.

docs.title: 2.1 HTTP Method Distribution
docs.text:
Method Codebase (5.17.0) Wiki (5.10.0) Delta GET 112 155 -43 POST 35 71 -36 PUT 20 28 -8 DELETE 32 39 -7 PATCH 33 39 -6 Total 232 332 -100

The wiki spec has 100 more endpoint definitions, primarily due to enterprise/commercial features (integrations, reports, analytics, 2FA, notifications, etc.) that are not in the community codebase spec.

docs.title: 2.2 Paths Only in Codebase Spec (5.17.0) — 39 Unique
docs.text:
After normalizing for the /_api prefix difference, these paths are conceptually new in the 5.17.0 spec (not present in the 5.10.0 wiki spec):

Category Paths Notes Agent Executions agent_executions, agent_executions/filter, agent_executions/{object_id} Entirely new resource group Agent Schedule additions agents_schedule/count_schedulers, agents_schedule/filter New sub-endpoints Agent additions agents/filter, agents/save_parameters New agent features Global Hosts (non-workspaced) hosts, hosts/count, hosts/countVulns, hosts/count_multi_workspace, hosts/filter, hosts/{host_id}/services, hosts/{host_id}/tools_history, hosts/{object_id} Global (non-workspace-scoped) host endpoints Global Services (non-workspaced) services, services/count, services/filter, services/{object_id} Global service endpoints Global Vulns (non-workspaced) vulns, vulns/count, vulns/count_multi_workspace, vulns/export_csv, vulns/filter, vulns/top_users, vulns/{object_id}, vulns/{vuln_id}/attachment, vulns/{vuln_id}/attachment/{filename} Global vulnerability endpoints Settings additions settings/dashboard, settings/elk, settings/query_limits New settings modules Workspace additions ws/bulk_update, ws/{id}/activate/, ws/{id}/change_readonly/, ws/{id}/deactivate/ Workspace management Workspaced additions credential/filter, credential/import_csv Credential filter & CSV import

docs.title: 2.3 Paths Only in Wiki Spec (5.10.0) — Enterprise/Commercial
docs.text:
These 105+ paths in the wiki spec represent enterprise features absent from the community codebase:
