docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: A JSON object containing only the fields to update.

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
• Status: 200 OK
  • Body: Updated Task object

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
# Change the value a task sets
curl -s -X PATCH \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "value": "false",
    "description": "Mark vulnerability as unconfirmed"
  }' \
  https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/tasks/1

{
  "id": 1,
  "name": "Set confirmed and tag",
  "description": "Mark vulnerability as unconfirmed",
  "command": "UPDATE",
  "field": "confirmed",
  "value": "false",
  "custom_field": null,
  "job_id": 1,
  "target": null,
  "create_date": "2026-02-15T10:03:00+00:00",
  "update_date": "2026-02-27T14:15:00+00:00"
}

docs.title: Error Codes
docs.text: Status Description 400 Bad request -- validation error 401 Unauthorized -- missing or invalid authentication 403 Forbidden -- insufficient permissions 404 Not found -- no task with the given ID

docs.title: 6. Delete Task
docs.text:
Permanently delete a task. If the task is referenced by any Jobs, it will be removed from those Job associations.

Warning: Deleting a task that is actively referenced by Jobs may cause those Jobs to have incomplete task lists. Verify no Jobs depend on this task before deleting.

Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/tasks/{object_id} Authentication Required (session cookie or API token)

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description object_id integer Yes The task ID

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
• Status: 204 No Content
  • Body: Empty

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -X DELETE \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/tasks/5

No response body is returned on success.

docs.title: Error Codes
docs.text: Status Description 401 Unauthorized -- missing or invalid authentication 403 Forbidden -- insufficient permissions 404 Not found -- no task with the given ID

docs.title: 7. Get Task Jobs
docs.text:
Retrieve the list of jobs that reference a specific task. Useful for understanding the impact of modifying or deleting a task.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/tasks/{task_id}/jobs Authentication Required (session cookie or API token)

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description task_id integer Yes The task ID

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
• Status: 200 OK
  • Body: Array of Job objects that include this task

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/tasks/1/jobs

[
  {
    "id": 1,
    "name": "Auto-confirm critical",
    "description": "Automatically confirm vulnerabilities with critical severity",
    "model": "vulnerability",
    "enabled": true,
    "rules": {},
    "rules_json": {
      "type": "AND",
      "is_root": true,
      "children": [
        {
          "type": "leaf",
          "field": "severity",
          "operator": "==",
          "data": "critical"
        }
      ]
    },
    "tasks": [
      {
        "id": 1,
        "name": "Set confirmed",
        "command": "UPDATE",
        "field": "confirmed",
        "value": "true"
      }
    ],
    "pipelines": [
      {
        "id": 1,
        "name": "Auto-triage critical vulns"
      }
    ],
    "tasks_ids": [1],
    "metadata": {},
    "create_date": "2026-02-15T10:05:00+00:00",
    "update_date": "2026-02-20T14:30:00+00:00"
  }
]

docs.title: Error Codes
docs.text: Status Description 401 Unauthorized -- missing or invalid authentication 404 Not found -- no task with the given ID

docs.title: 8. Get Task Fields
docs.text:
Retrieve the list of available fields that can be targeted by task actions, grouped by model type. This endpoint is useful for dynamically populating field selectors in a UI when creating or editing tasks.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/tasks/fields Authentication Required (session cookie or API token)

docs.title: Query Parameters
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
• Status: 200 OK
  • Body: Object mapping model types to arrays of available fields

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/tasks/fields

{
  "vulnerability": [
    {
      "name": "confirmed",
      "type": "boolean",
      "description": "Whether the vulnerability is confirmed"
    },
    {
      "name": "severity",
      "type": "string",
      "description": "Vulnerability severity level",
      "values": ["critical", "high", "medium", "low", "informational", "unclassified"]
    },
    {
      "name": "status",
      "type": "string",
      "description": "Vulnerability status",
      "values": ["open", "closed", "re-opened", "risk-accepted"]
    },
    {
      "name": "tags",
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Tags assigned to the vulnerability"
    },
    {
      "name": "description",
      "type": "string",
      "description": "Vulnerability description text"
    },
    {
      "name": "resolution",
      "type": "string",
      "description": "Remediation/resolution text"
    }
  ],
  "host": [
    {
      "name": "os",
      "type": "string",
      "description": "Host operating system"
    },
    {
      "name": "description",
      "type": "string",
      "description": "Host description"
    },
    {
      "name": "tags",
      "type": "array",
      "description": "Tags assigned to the host"
    }
  ],
  "service": [
    {
      "name": "name",
      "type": "string",
      "description": "Service name"
    },
    {
      "name": "description",
      "type": "string",
      "description": "Service description"
    },
    {
      "name": "status",
      "type": "string",
      "description": "Service status"
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Error Codes
docs.text: Status Description 401 Unauthorized -- missing or invalid authentication

docs.title: Error Reference
docs.text:
All error responses follow the standard Faraday API error format:

{
  "messages": ["Error description here"]
}

docs.title: Common Error Codes
docs.text: Status Code Name Description 400 Bad Request The request body is malformed, missing required fields, or contains invalid values. For Jobs, ensure model, rules_json, and tasks_ids are provided. For Tasks, ensure command is provided 401 Unauthorized No valid authentication credentials were provided. Include a session cookie or Authorization: Token <token> header 403 Forbidden The authenticated user does not have permission to perform this action 404 Not Found The specified resource ID does not exist 409 Conflict A resource with the same identifying attributes already exists 500 Internal Server Error An unexpected server error occurred. Check server logs for details

docs.title: Validation Error Examples
docs.text:
# Attempt to create a job without the required 'model' field
curl -s -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "Invalid job", "rules_json": {}, "tasks_ids": [1]}' \
  https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/jobs

{
  "messages": ["model: Missing data for required field."]
}

# Attempt to create a task without the required 'command' field
curl -s -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "Invalid task", "field": "confirmed", "value": "true"}' \
  https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/tasks

{
  "messages": ["command: Missing data for required field."]
}

# Attempt to create a job referencing a non-existent task
curl -s -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "Bad ref job", "model": "vulnerability", "rules_json": {}, "tasks_ids": [9999]}' \
  https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/jobs

{
  "messages": ["tasks_ids: Task with ID 9999 does not exist."]
}

docs.title: Related Documentation
docs.text:
• API Endpoint Reference (README) -- Overview of all Faraday API endpoint groups
  • Authentication -- Login, session management, and API tokens
  • Agents API -- Manage agents that feed data into workflows
  • Agent Schedules API -- Schedule automated agent runs that can trigger pipeline execution
  • Vulnerabilities API -- The primary data model targeted by workflow automation
  • Custom Fields -- Define custom fields that Tasks can act upon
  • Faraday Workflow Documentation -- User guide for configuring workflows in the Faraday UI

docs.title: Search Filters API
docs.text:
Base URL: https://localhost:5985 API Version: v3 Authentication: Cookie-based session or API token (Authorization: Token <token>) Content-Type: application/json

The Search Filters API manages saved filter queries that can be reused across the Faraday UI and API. Filters allow users to save complex query expressions with a human-readable name for quick access. Saved filters appear in the Faraday web interface filter dropdowns and can be referenced by name or applied programmatically.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• SearchFilter Schema
  • Endpoints
  • List Search Filters
  • Create Search Filter
  • Get Search Filter
  • Full Update Search Filter
  • Partial Update Search Filter
  • Delete Search Filter
  • Bulk Delete Search Filters
  • Bulk Update Search Filters
  • Common Error Codes

docs.title: SearchFilter Schema
docs.text:
Property Type Required Description id integer read-only Primary key. name string yes Display name for the saved filter. json_query string yes JSON-encoded filter expression used by the API. This is the machine-readable query string passed to filter endpoints via the q parameter. user_query string yes Human-readable representation of the filter query, displayed in the UI search bar.

Required fields for creation: name, json_query, user_query

docs.title: Example SearchFilter Object
docs.text:
{
  "id": 1,
  "name": "Critical Vulnerabilities",
  "json_query": "{\"filters\":[{\"name\":\"severity\",\"op\":\"eq\",\"val\":\"critical\"}]}",
  "user_query": "severity=critical"
}

docs.title: Filter Expression Format
docs.text:
The json_query field stores a JSON filter expression matching the format used by Faraday's filter endpoints:

{
  "filters": [
    {"name": "<field>", "op": "<operator>", "val": "<value>"}
  ],
  "conjunction": "AND"
}

Supported operators: eq, ne, lt, le, gt, ge, like, ilike, in, not_in, is_null, is_not_null

The user_query field is a simplified text representation intended for display purposes (e.g., "severity=critical AND status=open").

docs.title: Endpoints
docs.text: All endpoints operate under the path prefix /_api/v3/searchfilter. Search filters are global resources tied to the authenticated user and do not require a workspace context.

docs.title: 1. List Search Filters
docs.text:
Retrieve a paginated list of all saved search filters for the authenticated user.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/searchfilter Auth Required

Query Parameters:

Parameter Type Default Description page integer 0 Page number (zero-indexed). page_size integer 20 Number of results per page. sort string name Field to sort by. sort_dir string asc Sort direction (asc or desc).

Response: 200 OK

{
  "count": 5,
  "data": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "name": "Critical Vulnerabilities",
      "json_query": "{\"filters\":[{\"name\":\"severity\",\"op\":\"eq\",\"val\":\"critical\"}]}",
      "user_query": "severity=critical"
    },
    {
      "id": 2,
      "name": "Open High and Critical",
      "json_query": "{\"filters\":[{\"name\":\"severity\",\"op\":\"in\",\"val\":[\"high\",\"critical\"]},{\"name\":\"status\",\"op\":\"eq\",\"val\":\"open\"}],\"conjunction\":\"AND\"}",
      "user_query": "severity in [high, critical] AND status=open"
    }
  ]
}

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/searchfilter" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication.

docs.title: 2. Create Search Filter
docs.text:
Save a new search filter.

Method POST Path /_api/v3/searchfilter Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "name": "Unresolved Web Vulns",
  "json_query": "{\"filters\":[{\"name\":\"type\",\"op\":\"eq\",\"val\":\"VulnerabilityWeb\"},{\"name\":\"status\",\"op\":\"ne\",\"val\":\"closed\"}],\"conjunction\":\"AND\"}",
  "user_query": "type=VulnerabilityWeb AND status!=closed"
}

Required fields: name, json_query, user_query

Response: 201 Created

Returns the newly created search filter object.

Example:

curl -s -X POST \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/searchfilter" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{
    "name": "Unresolved Web Vulns",
    "json_query": "{\"filters\":[{\"name\":\"type\",\"op\":\"eq\",\"val\":\"VulnerabilityWeb\"},{\"name\":\"status\",\"op\":\"ne\",\"val\":\"closed\"}],\"conjunction\":\"AND\"}",
    "user_query": "type=VulnerabilityWeb AND status!=closed"
  }'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Validation error (e.g., missing required fields). 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 409 A search filter with this name already exists.

docs.title: 3. Get Search Filter
docs.text:
Retrieve a single saved search filter by its ID.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/searchfilter/{filter_id} Auth Required

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Description filter_id integer Search filter ID.

Response: 200 OK

Returns the full search filter object.

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/searchfilter/1" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 404 Search filter not found.

docs.title: 4. Full Update Search Filter
docs.text:
Replace all mutable fields of a search filter. Fields not included in the request body are reset to their defaults.

Method PUT Path /_api/v3/searchfilter/{filter_id} Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "name": "Critical and High Vulns (Open)",
  "json_query": "{\"filters\":[{\"name\":\"severity\",\"op\":\"in\",\"val\":[\"critical\",\"high\"]},{\"name\":\"status\",\"op\":\"eq\",\"val\":\"open\"}],\"conjunction\":\"AND\"}",
  "user_query": "severity in [critical, high] AND status=open"
}

Response: 200 OK

Returns the updated search filter object.

Example:

curl -s -X PUT \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/searchfilter/1" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{
    "name": "Critical and High Vulns (Open)",
    "json_query": "{\"filters\":[{\"name\":\"severity\",\"op\":\"in\",\"val\":[\"critical\",\"high\"]},{\"name\":\"status\",\"op\":\"eq\",\"val\":\"open\"}],\"conjunction\":\"AND\"}",
    "user_query": "severity in [critical, high] AND status=open"
  }'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Validation error. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 404 Search filter not found. 409 Name conflict with another search filter.

docs.title: 5. Partial Update Search Filter
docs.text:
Update only the specified fields of a search filter. Omitted fields remain unchanged.

Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/searchfilter/{filter_id} Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "name": "Critical Vulns Only"
}

Response: 200 OK

Returns the updated search filter object.

Example:

curl -s -X PATCH \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/searchfilter/1" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{"name": "Critical Vulns Only"}'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Validation error. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 404 Search filter not found.

docs.title: 6. Delete Search Filter
docs.text:
Delete a single saved search filter.

Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/searchfilter/{filter_id} Auth Required

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Description filter_id integer Search filter ID.

Response: 204 No Content

No response body.

Example:

curl -s -X DELETE \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/searchfilter/1" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 404 Search filter not found.

docs.title: 7. Bulk Delete Search Filters
docs.text:
Delete multiple saved search filters in a single request.

Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/searchfilter Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "ids": [1, 2, 3]
}

Response: 204 No Content

No response body.

Example:

curl -s -X DELETE \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/searchfilter" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{"ids": [1, 2, 3]}'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Invalid request body or empty ids array. 401 Missing or invalid authentication.

docs.title: 8. Bulk Update Search Filters
docs.text:
Update multiple saved search filters with the same set of field values.

Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/searchfilter Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "ids": [1, 2],
  "update": {
    "user_query": "Updated filter description"
  }
}

Response: 204 No Content

No response body on success.

{
  "updated": 2
}

Example:

curl -s -X PATCH \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/searchfilter" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{"ids": [1, 2], "update": {"user_query": "Updated filter description"}}'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Invalid request body, empty ids, or invalid fields in update. 401 Missing or invalid authentication.

docs.title: Common Error Codes
docs.text: HTTP Status Meaning 200 Success. Response body contains the requested data. 201 Created. New search filter was successfully created. 204 No Content. Search filter was deleted successfully. 400 Bad Request. Malformed JSON or missing required fields. 401 Unauthorized. Authentication token is missing, expired, or invalid. 404 Not Found. The search filter or endpoint does not exist. 409 Conflict. A search filter with the same name already exists. 500 Internal Server Error. Unexpected server-side failure.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• All curl examples include -k to skip TLS certificate verification for local development. Remove this flag in production environments with valid certificates.
  • Pagination is zero-indexed: page=0 returns the first page.
  • The json_query field must contain a valid JSON string. Ensure proper escaping when constructing the value programmatically.
  • Search filters are associated with the authenticated user. Each user has their own set of saved filters.
  • The json_query value is typically URL-encoded when passed as the q parameter to filter endpoints (e.g., GET /_api/v3/ws/{ws}/vulns/filter?q=<url-encoded-json>).
  • The user_query field is for display purposes only and is not parsed by the server. Ensure it accurately reflects the json_query for consistency in the UI.

docs.title: Services API
docs.text:
API Version: v3 Base URL: https://<faraday-server>/_api Authentication: Cookie-based session or API token (Authorization: Token <token>) Content-Type: application/json

Services represent network services (ports) discovered on hosts within a workspace. Each service belongs to a single host and can have zero or more vulnerabilities associated with it.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• Service Schema
  • Workspaced Endpoints
  • List Services
  • Create Service
  • Get Service
  • Update Service (Full)
  • Update Service (Partial)
  • Delete Service
  • Bulk Delete Services
  • Bulk Update Services
  • Count Services
  • Filter Services
  • Global Endpoints
  • List All Services
  • Get Service by ID
  • Filter Services Globally
  • Bulk Delete Services (Global)
  • Bulk Update Services (Global)
  • Count Services (Global)
  • Common Error Responses
  • Usage Notes

docs.title: Service Schema
docs.text: Field Type Required Description _id integer -- Internal object identifier (read-only). _rev string -- Revision hash (read-only). id integer -- Service ID (read-only, same as _id). name string No Service name (e.g. "http", "ssh", "ftp"). description string No Free-text description of the service. port integer Yes Port number (e.g. 80, 443, 22). ports string Yes Port number as a string (e.g. "80", "443"). protocol string Yes Transport protocol (e.g. "tcp", "udp"). status string No Port status (e.g. "open", "closed", "filtered"). version string No Service version banner (e.g. "Apache 2.4.41", "OpenSSH 8.2"). owned boolean No Whether the service has been marked as owned. Default: false. owner string No Username of the object owner (read-only, set by server). host_id integer No ID of the parent host (read-only). parent integer No Parent host ID. Used when creating a service to attach it to a host. parent_name string No Hostname of the parent host (read-only). command_id integer No ID of the command that created this service (read-only). vulns integer No Count of vulnerabilities associated with this service (read-only). metadata object No Timestamps and metadata (read-only). Contains create_time, update_time, update_user, update_action, creator, owner, command_id. summary string No Service summary text (read-only). credentials integer No Count of credentials associated with this service (read-only). tags array No Tags associated with this service. type string No Object type identifier (read-only, typically "Service").

docs.title: Example Service Object
docs.text:
{
  "_id": 42,
  "_rev": "",
  "id": 42,
  "name": "http",
  "description": "Web server",
  "port": 80,
  "ports": "80",
  "protocol": "tcp",
  "status": "open",
  "version": "Apache 2.4.41",
  "owned": false,
  "owner": "faraday",
  "host_id": 1,
  "parent": 1,
  "parent_name": "192.168.1.10",
  "command_id": null,
  "vulns": 3,
  "metadata": {
    "create_time": "2026-02-27T10:30:00+00:00",
    "update_time": "2026-02-27T10:30:00+00:00",
    "update_user": "faraday",
    "update_action": 0,
    "creator": "faraday",
    "owner": "faraday",
    "command_id": null
  },
  "summary": {},
  "type": "Service",
  "workspace_name": "pentest"
}

docs.title: Workspaced Endpoints
docs.text:
These endpoints operate within a specific workspace. Replace {workspace_name} with the target workspace name.

Base path: /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/services

docs.title: 1. List Services
docs.text:
Retrieve a paginated list of all services in a workspace.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/services Auth Session cookie or Authorization: Token <token>

docs.title: Query Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Default Description page integer 0 Page number (0-indexed). page_size integer 20 Number of results per page. sort string -- Field to sort by (e.g. port, name, status). sort_dir string asc Sort direction: asc or desc.

docs.title: Response: 200 OK
docs.text:
{
  "count": 2,
  "data": [
    {
      "_id": 42,
      "id": 42,
      "name": "http",
      "port": 80,
      "ports": "80",
      "protocol": "tcp",
      "status": "open",
      "version": "Apache 2.4.41",
      "owned": false,
      "owner": "faraday",
      "host_id": 1,
      "parent": 1,
      "parent_name": "192.168.1.10",
      "vulns": 3,
      "metadata": {
        "create_time": "2026-02-27T10:30:00+00:00",
        "update_time": "2026-02-27T10:30:00+00:00",
        "update_user": "faraday",
        "update_action": 0,
        "creator": "faraday",
        "owner": "faraday",
        "command_id": null
      },
      "workspace_name": "pentest"
    },
    {
      "_id": 43,
      "id": 43,
      "name": "ssh",
      "port": 22,
      "ports": "22",
      "protocol": "tcp",
      "status": "open",
      "version": "OpenSSH 8.2",
      "owned": false,
      "owner": "faraday",
      "host_id": 1,
      "parent": 1,
      "parent_name": "192.168.1.10",
      "vulns": 1,
      "metadata": {
        "create_time": "2026-02-27T10:31:00+00:00",
        "update_time": "2026-02-27T10:31:00+00:00",
        "update_user": "faraday",
        "update_action": 0,
        "creator": "faraday",
        "owner": "faraday",
        "command_id": null
      },
      "workspace_name": "pentest"
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest/services?page=0&page_size=10&sort=port&sort_dir=asc"

docs.title: 2. Create Service
docs.text:
Create a new service attached to an existing host in the workspace.

Method POST Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/services Auth Session cookie or Authorization: Token <token>

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: Field Type Required Description name string No Service name (e.g. "http"). port integer Yes Port number. ports string Yes Port number as string. protocol string Yes Transport protocol (e.g. "tcp"). status string No Port status (e.g. "open"). version string No Service version string. description string No Free-text description. owned boolean No Mark as owned. Default: false. parent integer No Host ID to attach the service to.

docs.title: Request Example
docs.text:
{
  "name": "http",
  "port": 80,
  "ports": "80",
  "protocol": "tcp",
  "status": "open",
  "version": "Apache 2.4.41",
  "description": "Main web server",
  "parent": 1
}

docs.title: Response: 201 Created
docs.text:
Returns the full service object with server-assigned fields (_id, id, metadata, owner, etc.).

{
  "_id": 44,
  "_rev": "",
  "id": 44,
  "name": "http",
  "description": "Main web server",
  "port": 80,
  "ports": "80",
  "protocol": "tcp",
  "status": "open",
  "version": "Apache 2.4.41",
  "owned": false,
  "owner": "faraday",
  "host_id": 1,
  "parent": 1,
  "parent_name": "192.168.1.10",
  "command_id": null,
  "vulns": 0,
  "metadata": {
    "create_time": "2026-02-27T11:00:00+00:00",
    "update_time": "2026-02-27T11:00:00+00:00",
    "update_user": "faraday",
    "update_action": 0,
    "creator": "faraday",
    "owner": "faraday",
    "command_id": null
  },
  "type": "Service",
  "workspace_name": "pentest"
}

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -X POST -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest/services" \
  -d '{
    "name": "http",
    "port": 80,
    "ports": "80",
    "protocol": "tcp",
    "status": "open",
    "version": "Apache 2.4.41",
    "parent": 1
  }'

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text: Code Condition 400 Missing required fields (port, ports, protocol), or invalid field values. 401 Not authenticated. 404 Workspace not found, or parent host does not exist.

docs.title: 3. Get Service
docs.text:
Retrieve a single service by its ID.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/services/{object_id} Auth Session cookie or Authorization: Token <token>

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description workspace_name string Target workspace name. object_id integer Service ID.

docs.title: Response: 200 OK
docs.text: Returns the full service object (same schema as the create response).

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest/services/42"

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text: Code Condition 401 Not authenticated. 404 Workspace or service not found.

docs.title: 4. Update Service (Full)
docs.text:
Replace all fields of a service. All mutable fields must be provided; omitted fields will be reset to defaults.

Method PUT Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/services/{object_id} Auth Session cookie or Authorization: Token <token>

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: Same schema as Create Service. All required fields must be present.

docs.title: Request Example
docs.text:
{
  "name": "https",
  "port": 443,
  "ports": "443",
  "protocol": "tcp",
  "status": "open",
  "version": "nginx 1.21",
  "description": "Updated to HTTPS",
  "owned": false,
  "parent": 1
}

docs.title: Response: 200 OK
docs.text: Returns the updated service object.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -X PUT -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest/services/42" \
  -d '{
    "name": "https",
    "port": 443,
    "ports": "443",
    "protocol": "tcp",
    "status": "open",
    "version": "nginx 1.21",
    "description": "Updated to HTTPS",
    "owned": false,
    "parent": 1
  }'

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text: Code Condition 400 Missing required fields or invalid values. 401 Not authenticated. 404 Workspace or service not found.

docs.title: 5. Update Service (Partial)
docs.text:
Update one or more fields of a service without replacing the entire object.

Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/services/{object_id} Auth Session cookie or Authorization: Token <token>

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: Only include the fields you want to change.

docs.title: Request Example
docs.text:
{
  "status": "closed",
  "version": "Apache 2.4.52"
}

docs.title: Response: 200 OK
docs.text: Returns the updated service object.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -X PATCH -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest/services/42" \
  -d '{
    "status": "closed",
    "version": "Apache 2.4.52"
  }'

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text: Code Condition 400 Invalid field values. 401 Not authenticated. 404 Workspace or service not found.

docs.title: 6. Delete Service
docs.text:
Delete a single service by its ID. This also removes the association with any linked vulnerabilities.

Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/services/{object_id} Auth Session cookie or Authorization: Token <token>

docs.title: Response: 204 No Content
docs.text: Empty response body on success.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -X DELETE -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest/services/42"

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text: Code Condition 401 Not authenticated. 404 Workspace or service not found.

docs.title: 7. Bulk Delete Services
docs.text:
Delete multiple services in a single request.

Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/services Auth Session cookie or Authorization: Token <token>

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text:
{
  "ids": [42, 43, 44]
}
Field Type Required Description ids array of integers Yes List of service IDs to delete.

docs.title: Response: 200 OK
docs.text:
Returns a confirmation with the count of deleted services.

{
  "deleted": 3
}

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -X DELETE -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest/services" \
  -d '{"ids": [42, 43, 44]}'

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text: Code Condition 400 Missing or empty ids array. 401 Not authenticated. 404 Workspace not found.

docs.title: 8. Bulk Update Services
docs.text:
Update fields on multiple services at once.

Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/services Auth Session cookie or Authorization: Token <token>

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text:
{
  "ids": [42, 43],
  "update": {
    "owned": true,
    "status": "open"
  }
}
Field Type Required Description ids array of integers Yes List of service IDs to update. update object Yes Key-value pairs of fields to update.

docs.title: Response: 200 OK
docs.text:
Returns a confirmation with the count of updated services.

{
  "updated": 2
}

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -X PATCH -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest/services" \
  -d '{
    "ids": [42, 43],
    "update": {
      "owned": true,
      "status": "open"
    }
  }'

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text: Code Condition 400 Missing ids or update, or invalid field values. 401 Not authenticated. 404 Workspace not found.

docs.title: 9. Count Services
docs.text:
Get the total number of services in a workspace.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/services/count Auth Session cookie or Authorization: Token <token>

docs.title: Response: 200 OK
docs.text:
{
  "count": 127
}

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest/services/count"

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text: Code Condition 401 Not authenticated. 404 Workspace not found.

docs.title: 10. Filter Services
docs.text:
Retrieve services matching a filter expression. Supports the same query parameter filters available throughout the Faraday API.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/services/filter Auth Session cookie or Authorization: Token <token>

docs.title: Query Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description q string (JSON) Filter query in Faraday filter syntax. page integer Page number (0-indexed). page_size integer Items per page. sort string Field to sort by. sort_dir string Sort direction (asc or desc).

docs.title: Filter Syntax
docs.text:
Filters are passed as a JSON-encoded q parameter. The general structure is:

q={"filters": [{"name": "<field>", "op": "<operator>", "val": "<value>"}]}

Supported operators: eq, neq, gt, gte, lt, lte, like, ilike, in, not_in, is_null, is_not_null

docs.title: Response: 200 OK
docs.text: Same paginated response format as List Services.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
Filter by port:

curl -s -G -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest/services/filter" \
  --data-urlencode 'q={"filters": [{"name": "port", "op": "eq", "val": 80}]}'

Filter by status "open" and protocol "tcp":

curl -s -G -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest/services/filter" \
  --data-urlencode 'q={"filters": [{"name": "status", "op": "eq", "val": "open"}, {"name": "protocol", "op": "eq", "val": "tcp"}]}'

Filter services with vulnerabilities:

curl -s -G -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest/services/filter" \
  --data-urlencode 'q={"filters": [{"name": "vulns", "op": "gt", "val": 0}]}'

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text: Code Condition 400 Malformed filter query. 401 Not authenticated. 404 Workspace not found.

docs.title: Global Endpoints
docs.text:
Important: These global service endpoints are not declared in the published 5.10.0 OpenAPI specification (faraday_swagger.json). They exist in the codebase spec for version 5.17.0+. The paths and behaviors described below are derived from the codebase and may not be available in all Faraday deployments.

Global endpoints provide read-only (and bulk operation) access to services across all workspaces the authenticated user has access to. These are useful for dashboards and cross-workspace reporting.

Base path: /_api/v3/services

docs.title: 1. List All Services
docs.text:
Retrieve a paginated list of services across all accessible workspaces.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/services Auth Session cookie or Authorization: Token <token>

docs.title: Query Parameters
docs.text: Same pagination and sorting parameters as List Services (page, page_size, sort, sort_dir).

docs.title: Response: 200 OK
docs.text: Same paginated response format as List Services. Each service object includes workspace_name to identify its workspace.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/services?page=0&page_size=50"

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text: Code Condition 401 Not authenticated.

docs.title: 2. Get Service by ID
docs.text:
Retrieve a single service by its ID without specifying a workspace.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/services/{object_id} Auth Session cookie or Authorization: Token <token>

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description object_id integer Service ID.

docs.title: Response: 200 OK
docs.text: Returns the full service object including workspace_name.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/services/42"

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text: Code Condition 401 Not authenticated. 404 Service not found or user lacks access to its workspace.

docs.title: 3. Filter Services Globally
docs.text:
Filter services across all accessible workspaces.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/services/filter Auth Session cookie or Authorization: Token <token>

docs.title: Query Parameters
docs.text: Same filter parameters as Filter Services (workspaced).

docs.title: Response: 200 OK
docs.text: Same paginated response format as List Services.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -G -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/services/filter" \
  --data-urlencode 'q={"filters": [{"name": "port", "op": "eq", "val": 443}]}'

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text: Code Condition 400 Malformed filter query. 401 Not authenticated.

docs.title: 4. Bulk Delete Services (Global)
docs.text:
Delete multiple services across workspaces.

Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/services Auth Session cookie or Authorization: Token <token>

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text:
{
  "ids": [42, 43, 100]
}
Field Type Required Description ids array of integers Yes List of service IDs to delete.

docs.title: Response: 200 OK
docs.text:
{
  "deleted": 3
}

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -X DELETE -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/services" \
  -d '{"ids": [42, 43, 100]}'

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text: Code Condition 400 Missing or empty ids array. 401 Not authenticated.

docs.title: 5. Bulk Update Services (Global)
docs.text:
Update fields on multiple services across workspaces.

Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/services Auth Session cookie or Authorization: Token <token>

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text:
{
  "ids": [42, 43, 100],
  "update": {
    "owned": true
  }
}
Field Type Required Description ids array of integers Yes List of service IDs to update. update object Yes Key-value pairs of fields to update.

docs.title: Response: 200 OK
docs.text:
{
  "updated": 3
}

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -X PATCH -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/services" \
  -d '{
    "ids": [42, 43, 100],
    "update": {
      "owned": true
    }
  }'

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text: Code Condition 400 Missing ids or update, or invalid field values. 401 Not authenticated.

docs.title: 6. Count Services (Global)
docs.text:
Get the total number of services across all accessible workspaces.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/services/count Auth Session cookie or Authorization: Token <token>

docs.title: Response: 200 OK
docs.text:
{
  "count": 1482
}

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/services/count"

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text: Code Condition 401 Not authenticated.

docs.title: Common Error Responses
docs.text: All endpoints may return the following standard error responses:

docs.title: 400 Bad Request
docs.text:
Returned when the request body is malformed, required fields are missing, or field values are invalid.

{
  "messages": ["Missing required field: port"]
}

docs.title: 401 Unauthorized
docs.text:
Returned when the request lacks valid authentication credentials.

{
  "messages": ["Unauthorized"]
}

docs.title: 404 Not Found
docs.text:
Returned when the workspace, service, or parent host does not exist.

{
  "messages": ["Not Found"]
}

docs.title: Relationship to Hosts
docs.text:
• Every service must belong to a host. When creating a service, use the parent field to specify the host ID.
  • The host_id and parent fields both reference the parent host. host_id is read-only and set by the server; parent is used in write operations.
  • Deleting a host will cascade-delete all its services.

docs.title: Relationship to Vulnerabilities
docs.text:
• Vulnerabilities can be associated with a service via the vulnerability's service_id field.
  • The vulns field on a service object is a read-only count of associated vulnerabilities.
  • Deleting a service does not automatically delete its vulnerabilities; they become unlinked.

docs.title: Port and Ports Fields
docs.text:
• Both port (integer) and ports (string) are required when creating or fully updating a service. They should contain the same value in their respective types.
  • The ports field exists for historical compatibility and should match the port value as a string.

docs.title: Duplicate Services
docs.text: • Faraday identifies services by the combination of host_id + port + protocol within a workspace. Attempting to create a duplicate may result in an update of the existing service rather than an error, depending on the server configuration and ingestion method.

docs.title: Filtering by Host
docs.text:
To retrieve all services for a specific host, use the filter endpoint:

curl -s -G -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest/services/filter" \
  --data-urlencode 'q={"filters": [{"name": "host_id", "op": "eq", "val": 1}]}'

Alternatively, services for a host can also be retrieved via the Hosts API:

curl -s -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest/hosts/1/services"

docs.title: Settings API
docs.text:
Base URL: https://localhost:5985 API Version: v3 Authentication: Cookie-based session or API token (Authorization: Token <token>) Content-Type: application/json

The Settings API provides access to server-side configuration modules that control various aspects of the Faraday platform. Each settings module is identified by name and exposes a GET endpoint to read current values and a PATCH endpoint to update them. These endpoints were introduced after version 5.10.0.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• Settings Overview
  • Endpoints
  • Get Dashboard Settings
  • Update Dashboard Settings
  • Get ELK Settings
  • Update ELK Settings
  • Get Reports Settings
  • Update Reports Settings
  • Get Query Limits Settings
  • Update Query Limits Settings
  • Enterprise Settings Modules
  • Common Error Codes

docs.title: Settings Overview
docs.text:
Settings are organized into modules. Each module manages a specific area of configuration.

Module Path Description dashboard /_api/v3/settings/dashboard Dashboard display and behavior settings. elk /_api/v3/settings/elk ELK/Elasticsearch integration settings. reports /_api/v3/settings/reports Report generation settings. query_limits /_api/v3/settings/query_limits API query limits and pagination defaults.

Each module supports two operations:

Operation Method Description Read GET Returns the current settings for the module. Update PATCH Performs a partial update of the module settings. Only the provided fields are changed; omitted fields retain their current values.

docs.title: 1. Get Dashboard Settings
docs.text:
Retrieve the current dashboard configuration.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/settings/dashboard Auth Required

Response: 200 OK

{
  "show_vulns_by_price": false,
  "vulnerability_score": true
}

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/settings/dashboard" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks admin permissions.

docs.title: 2. Update Dashboard Settings
docs.text:
Update the dashboard configuration. Only the provided fields are changed.

Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/settings/dashboard Auth Required (admin)

Request Body:

{
  "show_vulns_by_price": true
}

Response: 200 OK

Returns the full updated settings object.

{
  "show_vulns_by_price": true,
  "vulnerability_score": true
}

Example:

curl -s -X PATCH \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/settings/dashboard" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{"show_vulns_by_price": true}'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Invalid settings values. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks admin permissions.

docs.title: 3. Get ELK Settings
docs.text:
Retrieve the current ELK/Elasticsearch integration configuration.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/settings/elk Auth Required

Response: 200 OK

{
  "enabled": false,
  "elasticsearch_url": "",
  "kibana_url": "",
  "index_prefix": "faraday"
}

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/settings/elk" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks admin permissions.

docs.title: 4. Update ELK Settings
docs.text:
Update the ELK/Elasticsearch integration configuration.

Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/settings/elk Auth Required (admin)

Request Body:

{
  "enabled": true,
  "elasticsearch_url": "https://elk.example.com:9200",
  "kibana_url": "https://kibana.example.com:5601",
  "index_prefix": "faraday_prod"
}

Response: 200 OK

Returns the full updated settings object.

{
  "enabled": true,
  "elasticsearch_url": "https://elk.example.com:9200",
  "kibana_url": "https://kibana.example.com:5601",
  "index_prefix": "faraday_prod"
}

Example:

curl -s -X PATCH \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/settings/elk" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{
    "enabled": true,
    "elasticsearch_url": "https://elk.example.com:9200",
    "kibana_url": "https://kibana.example.com:5601"
  }'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Invalid settings values (e.g., malformed URL). 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks admin permissions.

docs.title: 5. Get Reports Settings
docs.text:
Retrieve the current report generation configuration.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/settings/reports Auth Required

Response: 200 OK

{
  "custom_templates_folder": "",
  "executive_report_enabled": true,
  "markdown_enabled": true
}

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/settings/reports" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks admin permissions.

docs.title: 6. Update Reports Settings
docs.text:
Update the report generation configuration.

Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/settings/reports Auth Required (admin)

Request Body:

{
  "custom_templates_folder": "/opt/faraday/report_templates",
  "executive_report_enabled": true
}

Response: 200 OK

Returns the full updated settings object.

{
  "custom_templates_folder": "/opt/faraday/report_templates",
  "executive_report_enabled": true,
  "markdown_enabled": true
}

Example:

curl -s -X PATCH \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/settings/reports" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{"custom_templates_folder": "/opt/faraday/report_templates"}'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Invalid settings values (e.g., folder path does not exist). 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks admin permissions.

docs.title: 7. Get Query Limits Settings
docs.text:
Retrieve the current API query limits and pagination defaults.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/settings/query_limits Auth Required

Response: 200 OK

{
  "default_page_size": 20,
  "max_page_size": 1000,
  "search_page_size": 100
}

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/settings/query_limits" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks admin permissions.

docs.title: 8. Update Query Limits Settings
docs.text:
Update the API query limits and pagination defaults.

Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/settings/query_limits Auth Required (admin)

Request Body:

{
  "default_page_size": 50,
  "max_page_size": 500
}

Response: 200 OK

Returns the full updated settings object.

{
  "default_page_size": 50,
  "max_page_size": 500,
  "search_page_size": 100
}

Example:

curl -s -X PATCH \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/settings/query_limits" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{"default_page_size": 50, "max_page_size": 500}'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Invalid settings values (e.g., negative numbers, max_page_size smaller than default_page_size). 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 403 User lacks admin permissions.

docs.title: Enterprise Settings Modules
docs.text:
Enterprise and paid editions of Faraday may include additional settings modules not available in the community edition. These follow the same GET/PATCH pattern:

Module Path Description ldap /_api/v3/settings/ldap LDAP authentication integration (server URL, base DN, bind credentials). saml /_api/v3/settings/saml SAML 2.0 SSO integration (IdP metadata URL, entity ID, certificates). smtp /_api/v3/settings/smtp SMTP email settings for notifications (server, port, credentials, TLS). notifications /_api/v3/settings/notifications Notification preferences (email alerts, webhook triggers). vulnerabilities /_api/v3/settings/vulnerabilities Vulnerability management defaults (auto-close, deduplication rules). enrichment /_api/v3/settings/enrichment Vulnerability enrichment settings (CVE/CVSS auto-lookup, threat intelligence feeds).

Note: Attempting to access an enterprise module on a community edition will return 404 Not Found.

docs.title: Common Error Codes
docs.text: HTTP Status Meaning 200 Success. Response body contains the current or updated settings. 400 Bad Request. Invalid settings values in the request body. 401 Unauthorized. Authentication token is missing, expired, or invalid. 403 Forbidden. Settings modification requires admin privileges. 404 Not Found. The settings module does not exist (or is not available in the current edition). 500 Internal Server Error. Unexpected server-side failure.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• All curl examples include -k to skip TLS certificate verification for local development. Remove this flag in production environments with valid certificates.
  • Settings endpoints use PATCH for updates (not PUT). Only fields present in the request body are modified; all other fields retain their current values.
  • Reading settings (GET) requires authentication. Modifying settings (PATCH) requires admin-level privileges.
  • Settings changes take effect immediately without requiring a server restart.
  • The response schema for each module varies. The examples above show representative fields; actual fields depend on the Faraday version and edition.
  • These settings endpoints were introduced after Faraday 5.10.0. They are not present in earlier versions.

docs.title: SSO & LDAP Integration Guide
docs.text:
Faraday Server v5.10.0+ — Configuration reference for LDAP and SAML single sign-on integration.

Edition note: LDAP and SAML are commercial features. The community edition supports only local user authentication.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Faraday supports two external authentication backends:

Method Protocol Local Users API Tokens LDAP LDAP/LDAPS Disabled (mutual exclusion) Yes, after session SAML SAML 2.0 Available alongside SAML Yes, after session

Constraint: LDAP and SAML cannot be used simultaneously. You must choose one or use local authentication.

docs.title: Via Command Line
docs.text:
faraday-manage settings -a update ldap

Interactive prompts:

Update settings for: ldap
enabled [False]:
use_local_roles [True]:
default_local_role [admin]:
admin_group []:
asset_owner_group []:
client_group []:
pentester_group []:
disconnect_timeout [2.0]:
server []:
port [389]:
use_ldaps [False]:
use_start_tls [False]:
domain []:
domain_dn []:
bind_format [CN]:
bind_dn []:
user_class [user]:
user_attribute [sAMAccountName]:
group_class [group]:
paginated_fetch [False]:
Do you confirm your changes on ldap?
[Y/n]:

docs.title: Via UI
docs.text: Navigate to User menu (top-right) → Preferences → LDAP and toggle the "Activate LDAP" slider. Fill in the configuration fields and click Save.

docs.title: Connection
docs.text: Setting Default Description enabled False Enable LDAP authentication server (empty) LDAP server hostname or IP port 389 LDAP port (389 for plain, 636 for LDAPS) use_ldaps False Use LDAPS (SSL/TLS on port 636) use_start_tls False Use STARTTLS upgrade on plain port disconnect_timeout 2.0 Connection timeout in seconds

docs.title: Directory Structure
docs.text: Setting Default Description domain (empty) Active Directory domain (e.g., example.com) domain_dn (empty) Base DN (e.g., DC=example,DC=com) bind_format CN Bind DN format: CN or full DN bind_dn (empty) Service account bind DN user_class user LDAP object class for users user_attribute sAMAccountName Attribute used as login username group_class group LDAP object class for groups paginated_fetch False Use paged result controls for large directories

docs.title: Role Mapping
docs.text: Setting Default Description use_local_roles True Use Faraday role assignments instead of LDAP groups default_local_role admin Default role for new LDAP users admin_group (empty) LDAP group DN → admin role pentester_group (empty) LDAP group DN → pentester role asset_owner_group (empty) LDAP group DN → asset_owner role client_group (empty) LDAP group DN → client role

docs.title: Important Warnings
docs.text:
• No hybrid mode: Enabling LDAP disables local user authentication. You cannot have both LDAP and local users active simultaneously.
  • use_local_roles = True with no groups: When this is set and no group mappings are configured, any user in Active Directory can log in to Faraday with the default_local_role. Restrict access by mapping specific LDAP groups.
  • Restart required: Changes take effect only after restarting the server: 
    systemctl restart faraday-server

docs.title: LDAP + API Tokens
docs.text:
LDAP-authenticated users can obtain API tokens just like local users:

  1. User logs in via LDAP (credentials validated against AD)
  2. Server creates a session
  3. User calls GET /_api/v3/token to obtain a JWT
  4. Token is used for subsequent API calls

The JWT validation_check claim may behave differently for LDAP users who do not have a locally stored password.

docs.title: LDAP with Okta
docs.text: For LDAP authentication via Okta's LDAP Interface, see the dedicated guide in the Faraday wiki: LDAP-OKTA.md.

docs.title: Via Command Line
docs.text:
faraday-manage settings -a update saml

Interactive prompts:

Update settings for: saml
enabled [False]:
display_name [Identity Provider]:
user_role [admin]:
admin_group []:
attribute_identifier []:
sso_url [http://localhost/saml/login]:
sp_certificate []:
sp_private_key []:
idp_certificate []:
Do you confirm your changes on saml?
[Y/n]:

docs.title: Via UI
docs.text: Navigate to User menu (top-right) → Preferences → Authentication → SAML and configure the settings.

docs.title: Settings Reference
docs.text: Setting Default Description enabled False Enable SAML SSO display_name Identity Provider Name shown on the login page user_role admin Default role for new SAML users admin_group (empty) SAML group attribute → admin role attribute_identifier (empty) SAML attribute used as username in Faraday sso_url http://localhost/saml/login Identity Provider login URL sp_certificate (empty) Service Provider certificate (PEM format) sp_private_key (empty) Service Provider private key (PEM format) idp_certificate (empty) Identity Provider certificate (PEM format)

docs.title: SAML Endpoints
docs.text:
Faraday exposes these SAML endpoints:

Endpoint Purpose /_api/saml/login Initiate SAML SSO login /_api/saml/acs Assertion Consumer Service (IdP callback) /_api/saml/logout Single logout /_api/saml/metadata.xml SP metadata for IdP configuration

docs.title: Generating SP Certificates
docs.text:
Each Faraday instance needs its own SP certificate/key pair:

openssl req -newkey rsa:2048 -nodes -keyout key.pem -x509 -days 1825 -out certificate.pem
  • certificate.pem → SP Certificate setting
  • key.pem → SP Private Key setting

docs.title: SAML + Local Users
docs.text: Unlike LDAP, SAML can coexist with local user accounts. Users can log in via either method.

docs.title: Supported Identity Providers
docs.text: Provider Callback URL Login URI Auth0 https://<domain>/_api/saml/acs https://<domain>/_api/saml/login Azure AD https://<domain>/_api/saml/acs https://<domain>/_api/saml/login Okta https://<domain>/_api/saml/acs https://<domain>/_api/saml/login Google https://<domain>/_api/saml/acs https://<domain>/_api/saml/login

docs.title: Auth0 Setup Summary
docs.text:
1. Create a new application in Auth0
  2. Enable the SAML2 WEB APP addon
  3. From the Usage tab, copy:
  4. Issuer → Faraday's Identity ID
  5. Identity Provider Login URL → Faraday's SSO URL
  6. Download Identity Provider Certificate → Faraday's IdP Certificate
  7. Set the Application Callback URL to https://<domain>/_api/saml/acs
  8. Set Application Login URI to https://<domain>/_api/saml/login
  9. Set Allowed Logout URLs to https://<domain>/_api/saml/logout
  10. Generate SP certificate/key and configure in Faraday

docs.title: Azure AD Setup Summary
docs.text:
1. Create an Enterprise Application in Azure Portal
  2. Select Non-gallery application type
  3. Configure Single sign-on → SAML
  4. Set Identifier (Entity ID) to https://<domain>/_api/saml/metadata.xml
  5. Set Reply URL to https://<domain>/_api/saml/acs
  6. Download the Certificate (Base64) → Faraday's IdP Certificate
  7. Copy Microsoft Entra Identifier → Faraday's Identity ID
  8. Copy Login URL → Faraday's SSO URL
  9. Generate SP certificate/key and configure in Faraday

docs.title: SAML + API Tokens
docs.text:
SAML-authenticated users can obtain API tokens just like local users:

  1. User logs in via SAML SSO flow
  2. After successful assertion, server creates a session
  3. User calls GET /_api/v3/token to obtain a JWT
  4. Token is used for subsequent API calls

This allows automated workflows even when primary authentication is via SSO.

docs.title: Configuration Comparison
docs.text: Feature LDAP SAML Local user coexistence No Yes Mutual exclusion With SAML With LDAP Configuration method CLI or UI CLI or UI Server restart required Yes Yes Group-to-role mapping Yes (4 groups) Yes (admin group) Default role Configurable Configurable API token support Yes (after login) Yes (after login) Edition Commercial Commercial

docs.title: Troubleshooting SSO
docs.text: Issue Cause Solution LDAP login fails silently Server not restarted systemctl restart faraday-server "Any AD user can log in" use_local_roles=True with no groups Map specific LDAP groups to roles SAML callback 404 Wrong callback URL in IdP Verify URL is https://<domain>/_api/saml/acs Certificate mismatch Wrong certificate pasted Re-download IdP cert, regenerate SP cert SAML + LDAP conflict Both enabled Disable one; they are mutually exclusive Local users locked out LDAP enabled Disable LDAP via faraday-manage settings -a update ldap

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• [[README]] — Complete authentication guide
  • [[authentication-endpoints]] — Endpoint specifications
  • [[token-lifecycle]] — JWT token internals

docs.title: System Info API
docs.text:
API Version: v3 Spec Version: Faraday 5.10.0 Base URL: https://localhost:5985 Authentication: Cookie-based session or API token (Authorization: Token <token>) unless noted otherwise Content-Type: application/json

These endpoints expose server metadata, public configuration, user preferences, and the OpenAPI specification. They are useful for client bootstrapping, feature-flag detection, and version checks.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• Endpoints
  • Get Server Info
  • Get Public Configuration
  • Get User Preferences
  • Update User Preferences
  • Get OpenAPI Specification
  • Error Responses
  • See Also

docs.title: Endpoints
docs.text: # Method Path Auth Summary 1 GET /_api/v3/info Required Get server info 2 GET /_api/config None Get public configuration 3 GET /_api/v3/preferences Required Get user preferences 4 POST /_api/v3/preferences Required Update user preferences 5 GET /_api/v3/swagger Required Get OpenAPI specification

docs.title: 1. Get Server Info
docs.text:
Retrieve Faraday server information including the running version, installed plugins, and instance metadata.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/info Auth Required

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
Status Description 200 Success. Returns server information object 401 Unauthorized -- not authenticated

Response Schema:

Field Type Description Faraday Server string Human-readable product name Version string Running server version (e.g., "5.10.0") plugins array of strings List of installed plugin identifiers subversion string Git commit hash or sub-version identifier

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -X GET https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/info \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -k

Success (200):

{
  "Faraday Server": "Faraday Server",
  "Version": "5.10.0",
  "plugins": [
    "acunetix",
    "arachni",
    "burp",
    "nessus",
    "nmap",
    "openvas",
    "qualys",
    "zap"
  ],
  "subversion": "abc123def"
}

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• This endpoint is commonly used by clients (including faraday-cli) to verify connectivity and confirm the server version before issuing further requests.
  • The plugins array lists the plugin identifiers recognized by the server for report import. The actual number of plugins may exceed 120.

docs.title: 2. Get Public Configuration
docs.text:
Retrieve the public server configuration. This endpoint does not require authentication and is used by clients during initial bootstrapping to discover server capabilities and feature flags.

Method GET Path /_api/config Auth Not required (is_public = True)

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
Status Description 200 Success. Returns the public configuration object

Response Schema:

The response is a JSON object whose keys vary depending on the server edition (Community vs. Professional/Corporate) and configuration. Common fields include:

Field Type Description ver string Server version string lic object License information (edition, expiration, limits) osint boolean Whether OSINT features are enabled smtp boolean Whether SMTP (email) is configured ldap boolean Whether LDAP authentication is enabled saml boolean Whether SAML/SSO authentication is enabled registration boolean Whether self-registration is enabled show_cli_banner boolean Whether to display CLI promotional banner

Note: Fields may be added or removed across server versions. Clients should handle unknown keys gracefully.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
# No authentication header needed
curl -s -X GET https://localhost:5985/_api/config \
  -k

Success (200):

{
  "ver": "5.10.0",
  "lic": {
    "edition": "community",
    "expiration": null,
    "max_users": null
  },
  "osint": false,
  "smtp": false,
  "ldap": false,
  "saml": false,
  "registration": false,
  "show_cli_banner": true
}

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• This is one of the few endpoints that does not require any form of authentication. It is marked with is_public = True in the Faraday codebase.
  • The response is intentionally limited to non-sensitive configuration data. Sensitive settings (database credentials, secret keys, etc.) are never exposed through this endpoint.
  • The web UI calls this endpoint on page load to determine which login methods (local, LDAP, SAML) to display.

docs.title: 3. Get User Preferences
docs.text:
Retrieve the stored preferences for the currently authenticated user. Preferences are arbitrary key-value pairs that the Faraday UI and clients use to persist user-specific settings (e.g., default workspace, table column visibility, dashboard layout).

Method GET Path /_api/v3/preferences Auth Required

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
Status Description 200 Success. Returns the preferences object 401 Unauthorized -- not authenticated

Response Schema:

The response is a JSON object containing all stored preference key-value pairs for the authenticated user. The structure is free-form and determined by the client application.

Field Type Description preferences object Dictionary of user preference key-value pairs

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -X GET https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/preferences \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -k

Success (200):

{
  "preferences": {
    "default_workspace": "pentest_q1",
    "dashboard_layout": "compact",
    "vuln_table_columns": ["name", "severity", "status", "hostnames"],
    "items_per_page": 50
  }
}

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• If the user has no stored preferences, the response returns an empty preferences object ({}).
  • Preferences are user-scoped. Each authenticated user has their own independent set of preferences.

docs.title: 4. Update User Preferences
docs.text:
Create or update preferences for the currently authenticated user. The request body is merged with existing preferences -- keys present in the request body overwrite existing values, while keys not included remain unchanged.

Method POST Path /_api/v3/preferences Auth Required Content-Type application/json

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text:
A JSON object containing the preference key-value pairs to set or update.

Field Type Required Description (any key) any -- Preference keys and their values. Supports strings, numbers, booleans, arrays, and nested objects

docs.title: Response
docs.text: Status Description 200 Success. Preferences updated. Returns the updated preferences object 400 Bad request -- malformed JSON 401 Unauthorized -- not authenticated

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -X POST https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/preferences \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{
    "default_workspace": "pentest_q2",
    "items_per_page": 100,
    "dark_mode": true
  }'

Success (200):

{
  "preferences": {
    "default_workspace": "pentest_q2",
    "dashboard_layout": "compact",
    "vuln_table_columns": ["name", "severity", "status", "hostnames"],
    "items_per_page": 100,
    "dark_mode": true
  }
}

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• To remove a preference key, set its value to null.
  • The server does not validate the semantic meaning of preference keys. Any valid JSON value is accepted.
  • Preferences are persisted server-side and available across devices and sessions for the same user account.

docs.title: 5. Get OpenAPI Specification
docs.text:
Retrieve the full OpenAPI/Swagger JSON specification for the Faraday REST API. This returns the contents of the faraday_swagger.json file bundled with the server.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/swagger Auth Required

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: None.

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
Status Description 200 Success. Returns the full OpenAPI 2.0 (Swagger) specification as JSON 401 Unauthorized -- not authenticated

Response Schema:

The response is a complete OpenAPI 2.0 specification document. Key top-level fields include:

Field Type Description swagger string OpenAPI version (always "2.0") info object API metadata including title, version, and description basePath string API base path (typically "/_api") paths object All API path definitions with operations definitions object Reusable schema definitions (models) securityDefinitions object Authentication scheme definitions

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -X GET https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/swagger \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -k | python3 -m json.tool | head -20

Success (200) -- truncated:

{
  "swagger": "2.0",
  "info": {
    "title": "Faraday API",
    "version": "5.10.0",
    "description": "Faraday Security REST API"
  },
  "basePath": "/_api",
  "paths": {
    "/_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/hosts": {
      "get": { "..." },
      "post": { "..." }
    }
  },
  "definitions": { "..." },
  "securityDefinitions": { "..." }
}

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• The specification document is large (300KB+). Clients should cache the response rather than fetching it on every request.
  • This endpoint serves the same content as the static faraday/openapi/faraday_swagger.json file in the Faraday source tree.
  • The spec can be loaded into Swagger UI, Postman, or any OpenAPI-compatible tool for interactive API exploration.
  • Some endpoints that exist in the running application (e.g., Flask-Security login/logout, activity_feed, comment_unique) are not represented in the OpenAPI spec. See the individual endpoint documentation pages for details.

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text:
All endpoints return errors in the standard Faraday format:

{
  "messages": ["Error description here"]
}
Code Meaning 400 Bad request -- malformed JSON or invalid input 401 Unauthorized -- authentication required or token expired 500 Internal server error -- check server logs

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• [[README]] -- API endpoint index and common patterns
  • [[authentication]] -- Authentication and session management
  • Faraday CLI -- CLI client that uses /_api/v3/info and /_api/config for bootstrapping
  • MkDocs Site -- Public documentation

docs.title: API Token Lifecycle & Internals
docs.text: Faraday Server v5.10.0+ — Technical reference for JWT token generation, validation, expiration, and revocation internals.

docs.title: Token Generation
docs.text:
Source: faraday/server/models.py — User.get_token() (line 2728)

When a client calls GET /_api/v3/token, the server executes flask_login.current_user.get_token(), which:

  1. Reads the user's fs_uniquifier (a UUID assigned by Flask-Security)
  2. Hashes the user's current password using hash_data()
  3. Creates a JWT payload with four claims
  4. Signs the JWT using HS512 with the Flask app's SECRET_KEY
  5. Returns the encoded JWT string

docs.title: JWT Structure
docs.text:
Header.Payload.Signature

Header: 

{
  "alg": "HS512",
  "iat": 1709031578,
  "exp": 1709074778
}

Payload: 

{
  "user_id": "f3ca5266-9e6e-4740-b45b-7f63bdec37e0",
  "validation_check": "$5$rounds=535000$T6ws/Z0aGQmtRq3I$iE.NHmhh3VGHxUr.uZQ4dghEUQqbKOiXh9TgkMDmAr0",
  "iat": 1709031578,
  "exp": 1709074778
}

Claim Type Description user_id string (UUID) User's fs_uniquifier — stable identifier that survives password changes validation_check string SHA-256/crypt hash of the user's password at token creation time iat integer Issued-at timestamp (Unix epoch seconds) exp integer Expiration timestamp = iat + api_token_expiration setting

docs.title: Code Reference
docs.text:
# faraday/server/models.py, line 2728
def get_token(self):
    user_id = self.fs_uniquifier
    hashed_data = hash_data(self.password) if self.password else None
    iat = int(time.time())
    exp = iat + int(faraday_server.api_token_expiration)
    jwt_data = {
        'user_id': user_id,
        'validation_check': hashed_data,
        'iat': iat,
        'exp': exp
    }
    return jwt.encode(jwt_data, app.config['SECRET_KEY'], algorithm="HS512")

docs.title: Token Validation
docs.text:
Source: faraday/server/app.py — verify_token() (line 204)

On every authenticated request with Authorization: Token <jwt>, the server:

  1. Decodes the JWT using HS512 with the app's SECRET_KEY
  2. Checks signature — rejects if the signature is invalid (InvalidSignatureError)
  3. Checks expiration — rejects if exp < current time (ExpiredSignatureError)
  4. Looks up the user by user_id (the fs_uniquifier UUID)
  5. Validates the password hash — compares validation_check against the user's current password using verify_hash()

If any step fails, the token is rejected with 401 Unauthorized.

docs.title: Code Reference
docs.text:
# faraday/server/app.py, line 204
def verify_token(token):
    try:
        data = jwt.decode(token, app.config['SECRET_KEY'], algorithms=["HS512"])
        user_id = data["user_id"]
        user = User.query.filter_by(fs_uniquifier=user_id).first()
        if not user or not verify_hash(data['validation_check'], user.password):
            logger.warning('Invalid auth token. token invalid after password change')
            return None
        return user
    except jwt.ExpiredSignatureError:
        return None  # valid token, but expired
    except jwt.InvalidSignatureError:
        return None  # invalid token

docs.title: Validation Flow Diagram
docs.text:
Request arrives with Authorization: Token <jwt>
  │
  ├─ Decode JWT with SECRET_KEY (HS512)
  │   ├─ InvalidSignatureError → 401
  │   └─ ExpiredSignatureError → 401
  │
  ├─ Look up User by fs_uniquifier
  │   └─ User not found → 401
  │
  ├─ verify_hash(token.validation_check, user.password)
  │   └─ Hash mismatch (password changed) → 401
  │
  └─ ✓ Return authenticated user

docs.title: Authorization Header Parsing
docs.text:
Source: faraday/server/app.py — load_user_from_request() (line 218)

The Authorization header is parsed to determine the authentication method:

Authorization: <type> <credentials>
Type Handler Description Token verify_token() JWT validation (see above) Basic Username/password lookup HTTP Basic auth Agent Database lookup Agent persistent token 
# faraday/server/app.py, line 218
@app.login_manager.request_loader
def load_user_from_request(request):
    header = request.headers[config['SECURITY_TOKEN_AUTHENTICATION_HEADER']]
    (auth_type, token) = header.split(None, 1)
    auth_type = auth_type.lower()

    if auth_type == 'token':
        user = verify_token(token)
        ...
    elif auth_type == 'agent':
        return None  # handled in separate before_request
    elif auth_type == 'basic':
        username = request.authorization.get('username', '')
        password = request.authorization.get('password', '')
        user = User.query.filter_by(username=username).first()
        if user and user.verify_and_update_password(password):
            return user

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
The token lifetime is set by api_token_expiration in seconds:

Source Value Duration docker/server.ini 604800 7 days faraday/server/default.ini 43200 12 hours faraday/server/config.py (fallback) 86400 24 hours

Resolution order: 1. User's ~/.faraday/config/server.ini (if present) 2. faraday/server/default.ini (bundled defaults) 3. Python fallback in config.py

docs.title: Setting a Custom Expiration
docs.text:
Edit ~/.faraday/config/server.ini:

[faraday_server]
api_token_expiration = 604800

Restart the server for the change to take effect:

systemctl restart faraday-server

Note: This setting only affects newly issued tokens. Existing tokens retain their original expiration.

docs.title: Token Revocation Mechanisms
docs.text: Faraday uses implicit revocation through password hash validation rather than an explicit token blacklist.

docs.title: 1. Password Change (Immediate Revocation)
docs.text:
When a user's password changes, all previously issued tokens become invalid because the validation_check claim no longer matches the current password hash. This happens automatically — no revocation endpoint is needed.

Trigger: POST /_api/change or faraday-manage change-password

Scope: All JWT tokens for the affected user.

docs.title: 2. Token Expiration (Time-Based)
docs.text:
Each token has a hard exp claim. After expiration, the JWT library rejects the token with ExpiredSignatureError.

Scope: Only the expired token.

docs.title: 3. SECRET_KEY Rotation (Global Revocation)
docs.text:
Changing the Flask app's SECRET_KEY invalidates all tokens for all users because the HMAC signature can no longer be verified.

Scope: All tokens system-wide. Requires server restart.

docs.title: 4. Session Logout (Session Only)
docs.text: POST /_api/logout invalidates the session cookie only. It does not invalidate JWT tokens. A user who has obtained an API token can continue to use it after logging out of their session.

docs.title: Persistent Tokens (Commercial)
docs.text: The database includes a UserToken model (table: user_token) for integration-specific persistent tokens. These are distinct from JWT tokens.

docs.title: Schema
docs.text: Column Type Description id integer Primary key user_id integer FK → faraday_user.id token string Unique token string alias string Human-readable name expires_at datetime Optional expiration (null = never) scope enum gitlab, scheduler, service_desk, jira revoked boolean Explicit revocation flag hide boolean Hidden from UI listings

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
• expired — hybrid property: True if expires_at is set and in the past
  • Cascade-deleted when the parent user is deleted

docs.title: Key Differences from JWT Tokens
docs.text: Property JWT Token Persistent Token Storage Stateless (not stored) Database row Validation Signature + password hash Database lookup Scoping Full user access Integration-specific scope Revocation Password change revoked = True Expiration JWT exp claim expires_at column API exposure GET /_api/v3/token Commercial API only

docs.title: Registration Token (TOTP)
docs.text:
Source: faraday/server/api/modules/agent_auth_token.py

The agent registration token is a TOTP (Time-Based One-Time Password) generated with pyotp:

totp = pyotp.TOTP(
    faraday_server.agent_registration_secret,
    interval=int(faraday_server.agent_token_expiration)
)
  • Secret: Auto-generated on first server start, stored in server.ini
  • Interval: Default 60 seconds (configurable via agent_token_expiration)
  • Algorithm: SHA-1 (TOTP standard)

docs.title: Persistent Agent Token
docs.text:
Source: faraday/server/models.py — Agent model

Once an agent is registered, it receives a 64-character random string stored in the agent.token database column. This token is used for all subsequent agent-to-server communication.

Validation is a simple database lookup — no JWT or cryptographic verification:

agent = Agent.query.filter_by(token=token).first()
if not agent:
    abort(403)

docs.title: WebSocket Token
docs.text:
Source: faraday/server/api/modules/websocket_auth.py

WebSocket tokens are signed with itsdangerous.TimestampSigner:

  • User WS token: salt = "websocket", signs the workspace ID
  • Agent WS token: salt = "websocket_agent", signs the agent ID, max age = 60s

docs.title: Security Properties
docs.text: Property Status Notes Token algorithm HS512 (HMAC-SHA512) Strong symmetric signing Password hash in token Yes (validation_check) Enables implicit revocation Token blacklist No Not needed due to password-based revocation Token refresh endpoint No Must re-authenticate to get new token Audience/issuer claims No Single-server deployment assumed Token scoping No (community) All tokens grant full user access Replay protection Expiration only No nonce or jti claim Transport security HTTPS required Tokens in headers are visible over HTTP

docs.title: Vulnerabilities API
docs.text:
Base URL: https://<faraday-server>/_api API Version: v3 Authentication: Cookie-based session or API token (Authorization: Token <token>) Content-Type: application/json (unless noted otherwise)

The Vulnerabilities API is the largest endpoint group in Faraday, providing full lifecycle management for both standard infrastructure vulnerabilities and web application vulnerabilities. It exposes two scopes: workspaced (full CRUD within a workspace) and global (cross-workspace read-only access plus attachments and export).
