docs.title: Vulnerability Schema
docs.text:
The Vulnerability schema represents a standard infrastructure vulnerability finding.

Field Type Required Read-Only Write-Only Nullable Description id integer -- yes -- -- Unique vulnerability identifier _id integer -- yes -- -- Alias for id (legacy CouchDB compatibility) _rev string -- yes -- -- Revision string (legacy CouchDB compatibility) name string yes -- -- yes Vulnerability name (min 1 character) severity string yes -- -- -- One of: "critical", "high", "medium", "low", "informational", "unclassified" type string yes -- -- -- Must be "vulnerability" for standard vulns or "vulnerability_web" for web vulns status string -- -- -- -- One of: "open", "opened", "closed", "re-opened", "risk-accepted" confirmed boolean -- -- -- -- Whether the vulnerability has been confirmed desc string -- -- -- -- Detailed description (used for create/update) description string -- yes -- -- Description (read-only alias for desc in responses) data string -- -- -- yes Additional technical details or proof data resolution string -- -- -- yes Recommended remediation steps tool string -- -- -- -- Name of the tool/scanner that found this vulnerability command_id integer -- -- yes -- ID of the command that created this vulnerability service object -- yes -- -- Nested Service object (read-only in responses) target string -- yes -- -- Target host/IP date datetime -- yes -- -- Creation date update_user string -- yes -- -- Username that last modified this vulnerability run_date datetime -- -- -- -- Scanner run date easeofresolution string -- -- -- yes One of: "trivial", "simple", "moderate", "difficult", "infeasible", or null external_id string -- -- -- yes External reference ID (e.g., from a scanner) cve array[string] -- -- -- -- List of CVE identifiers (e.g., ["CVE-2021-44228"]) cwe array[string] -- -- -- -- List of CWE identifiers (e.g., ["CWE-89"]) cvss2 object -- -- -- -- CVSS v2 scoring object cvss3 object -- -- -- -- CVSS v3 scoring object vulnerability_duplicate_id integer -- yes -- yes ID of the duplicate vulnerability vulnerability_template_id integer -- yes -- yes ID of the vulnerability template duplicates_associated array -- yes -- -- Associated duplicate vulnerability references duplicates_main array -- yes -- -- Main duplicate vulnerability references refs array[Reference] -- -- -- -- External references tags array[string] -- -- -- -- User-assigned tags impact object -- -- -- -- Impact assessment object custom_fields object -- -- -- -- Custom field values (keys match custom field schema) policyviolations array[string] -- -- -- -- Policy violation identifiers owned boolean -- yes -- -- Whether the vulnerability has been exploited owner string -- yes -- -- Username of the creator issuetracker object -- -- -- -- Issue tracker integration data metadata object -- -- -- -- Additional metadata obj_id string -- yes -- -- Object identifier string risk string -- yes -- -- Calculated risk score host_os string -- yes -- -- Operating system of the affected host hostnames array -- yes -- -- Hostnames associated with the affected host reference_instances array -- yes -- -- Expanded reference objects _attachments object -- -- -- -- Attachment metadata

docs.title: Embedded Service Object
docs.text:
When a vulnerability is associated with a service, the service field in responses contains:

Field Type Description _id integer Service ID ports integer/array Port number(s) status string "open", "closed", or "filtered" protocol string Protocol (e.g., "tcp", "udp") name string Service name (e.g., "http", "ssh") version string Service version summary string Human-readable summary

docs.title: VulnerabilityWeb Schema
docs.text:
The VulnerabilityWeb schema extends the base Vulnerability schema with web-application-specific fields.

Required fields: name, severity, type (must be "vulnerability_web"), parent, parent_type

Field Type Required Description method string -- HTTP method (e.g., "GET", "POST") path string -- URL path where the vulnerability was found website string -- Target website/hostname request string -- Full HTTP request that triggered the vulnerability response string -- Full HTTP response received status_code integer -- HTTP response status code (nullable) params string -- Affected parameters pname string -- Primary parameter name query string -- Query string owasp string -- OWASP classification parent integer yes Parent host or service ID parent_type string yes Parent object type (e.g., "Host", "Service")

All fields from the base Vulnerability Schema are also present.

docs.title: Evidence Schema
docs.text:
Attachments (evidence files) associated with vulnerabilities use this schema.

Field Type Read-Only Description content_type string yes MIME type of the attachment data binary yes File content (returned on download) description string -- Description of the evidence file

docs.title: Reference Schema
docs.text:
External references attached to a vulnerability.

Field Type Description name string Reference identifier or URL (e.g., "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2021-44228") type string Reference type (e.g., "url", "cve", "other")

docs.title: Workspaced Endpoints
docs.text:
All workspaced endpoints require a {workspace_name} path parameter and operate within the scope of that workspace. There are 18 endpoints in total.

Base path: /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/vulns

docs.title: 1. List Vulnerabilities
docs.text:
Retrieve a paginated list of all vulnerabilities in a workspace.

Property Value Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/vulns Auth Required (session cookie or API token) Tags VulnerabilityGeneric

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description workspace_name string yes Workspace name

Query Parameters:

Parameter Type Default Description page integer 0 Page number (0-indexed) page_size integer 20 Items per page sort string -- Field to sort by (e.g., severity, name, date) sort_dir string asc Sort direction: asc or desc

Response: 200 OK

{
  "count": 42,
  "vulnerabilities": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "_id": 1,
      "name": "SQL Injection",
      "severity": "critical",
      "type": "vulnerability",
      "status": "open",
      "confirmed": true,
      "desc": "SQL injection in login form parameter 'username'",
      "description": "SQL injection in login form parameter 'username'",
      "resolution": "Use parameterized queries",
      "target": "192.168.1.10",
      "tool": "sqlmap",
      "date": "2026-02-20T14:30:00Z",
      "update_date": "2026-02-25T10:00:00Z",
      "cve": ["CVE-2024-12345"],
      "cwe": ["CWE-89"],
      "tags": ["sqli", "critical"],
      "refs": [
        {"name": "https://owasp.org/www-community/attacks/SQL_Injection", "type": "url"}
      ],
      "workspace_name": "pentest-2026",
      "owner": "admin",
      "service": {
        "_id": 5,
        "ports": 443,
        "status": "open",
        "protocol": "tcp",
        "name": "https",
        "version": "Apache 2.4.51",
        "summary": "443/tcp (https)"
      },
      "custom_fields": {},
      "_attachments": {},
      "_rev": ""
    }
  ]
}

Example:

# List all vulnerabilities in workspace "pentest-2026"
curl -s -b cookies.txt \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns?page=0&page_size=50&sort=severity&sort_dir=desc"

# With API token
curl -s -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns"

Error Codes:

Code Description 401 Unauthorized -- not authenticated 403 Forbidden -- insufficient permissions for this workspace 404 Workspace not found

docs.title: 2. Create Vulnerability
docs.text:
Create a new vulnerability (standard or web) in a workspace.

Property Value Method POST Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/vulns Auth Required Tags VulnerabilityGeneric Request Body application/json -- Vulnerability or VulnerabilityWeb

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description workspace_name string yes Workspace name

Request Body -- Standard Vulnerability:

{
  "name": "SQL Injection",
  "severity": "critical",
  "type": "vulnerability",
  "desc": "SQL injection vulnerability found in login form parameter 'username'. An attacker can extract database contents.",
  "resolution": "Use parameterized queries or prepared statements for all database queries.",
  "confirmed": true,
  "data": "sqlmap identified the following injection point:\n  Parameter: username (POST)\n  Type: boolean-based blind",
  "tags": ["sqli"],
  "cve": ["CVE-2024-12345"],
  "cwe": ["CWE-89"],
  "refs": [
    {"name": "https://owasp.org/www-community/attacks/SQL_Injection", "type": "url"}
  ],
  "easeofresolution": "simple",
  "impact": {
    "accountability": true,
    "availability": false,
    "confidentiality": true,
    "integrity": true
  },
  "policyviolations": [],
  "custom_fields": {}
}

Request Body -- Web Vulnerability:

{
  "name": "Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)",
  "severity": "high",
  "type": "vulnerability_web",
  "desc": "Reflected XSS in search parameter allows script injection.",
  "resolution": "Sanitize and encode all user-supplied output.",
  "confirmed": true,
  "method": "GET",
  "path": "/search",
  "website": "https://target.example.com",
  "request": "GET /search?q=<script>alert(1)</script> HTTP/1.1\nHost: target.example.com",
  "response": "HTTP/1.1 200 OK\n\n<html>...<script>alert(1)</script>...</html>",
  "status_code": 200,
  "params": "q",
  "pname": "q",
  "query": "q=<script>alert(1)</script>",
  "parent": 42,
  "parent_type": "Host",
  "cve": [],
  "cwe": ["CWE-79"],
  "owasp": "A7:2017-Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)",
  "tags": ["xss", "reflected"]
}

Response: 201 Created

Returns the created vulnerability object with all fields populated (including id, date, owner, etc.).

Example:

# Create a standard vulnerability
curl -s -b cookies.txt -X POST \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "SQL Injection",
    "severity": "critical",
    "type": "vulnerability",
    "desc": "SQL injection in login form",
    "resolution": "Use parameterized queries",
    "confirmed": true,
    "tags": ["sqli"]
  }'

# Create a web vulnerability
curl -s -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" -X POST \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "Reflected XSS",
    "severity": "high",
    "type": "vulnerability_web",
    "desc": "Reflected XSS in search parameter",
    "method": "GET",
    "path": "/search",
    "website": "https://target.example.com",
    "parent": 42,
    "parent_type": "Host"
  }'

Error Codes:

Code Description 400 Validation error (missing required fields, invalid severity, etc.) 401 Unauthorized 403 Forbidden 404 Workspace not found 409 Conflict (duplicate vulnerability)

docs.title: 3. Get Vulnerability
docs.text:
Retrieve a single vulnerability by its ID.

Property Value Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/vulns/{object_id} Auth Required Tags VulnerabilityGeneric

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description workspace_name string yes Workspace name object_id integer yes Vulnerability ID

Response: 200 OK

Returns the full vulnerability object (same structure as list items).

Example:

curl -s -b cookies.txt \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns/1"

Error Codes:

Code Description 401 Unauthorized 404 Vulnerability or workspace not found

docs.title: 4. Full Update Vulnerability
docs.text:
Note: The PUT method on this path is not declared in the published 5.10.0 OpenAPI spec (only GET, PATCH, and DELETE are defined). It may exist at runtime via the standard Flask mixin but should be used with caution. Prefer PATCH for updates.

Replace all fields of an existing vulnerability. All required fields must be provided.

Property Value Method PUT Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/vulns/{object_id} Auth Required Tags Vulnerability Request Body application/json -- full Vulnerability object

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description workspace_name string yes Workspace name object_id integer yes Vulnerability ID

Request Body:

All required fields (name, severity, type) must be included. Any omitted optional fields will be reset to their defaults.

{
  "name": "SQL Injection (Updated)",
  "severity": "high",
  "type": "vulnerability",
  "desc": "Updated description with additional findings.",
  "resolution": "Use parameterized queries. Apply input validation.",
  "status": "risk-accepted",
  "confirmed": true,
  "tags": ["sqli", "accepted-risk"]
}

Response: 200 OK

Returns the updated vulnerability object.

Example:

curl -s -b cookies.txt -X PUT \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns/1" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "SQL Injection (Updated)",
    "severity": "high",
    "type": "vulnerability",
    "desc": "Updated description.",
    "status": "risk-accepted"
  }'

Error Codes:

Code Description 400 Validation error 401 Unauthorized 404 Vulnerability or workspace not found

docs.title: 5. Partial Update Vulnerability
docs.text:
Update specific fields of an existing vulnerability without providing the full object.

Property Value Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/vulns/{object_id} Auth Required Tags VulnerabilityGeneric Request Body application/json -- partial Vulnerability or VulnerabilityWeb

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description workspace_name string yes Workspace name object_id integer yes Vulnerability ID

Request Body:

Only include the fields you want to change.

{
  "severity": "medium",
  "status": "closed",
  "resolution": "Patched in version 2.1.0"
}

Response: 200 OK

Returns the updated vulnerability object.

Example:

# Close a vulnerability
curl -s -b cookies.txt -X PATCH \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns/1" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"status": "closed"}'

# Update severity and add a tag
curl -s -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" -X PATCH \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns/1" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"severity": "medium", "tags": ["reviewed"]}'

Error Codes:

Code Description 400 Validation error 401 Unauthorized 404 Vulnerability or workspace not found 409 Conflict

docs.title: 6. Delete Vulnerability
docs.text:
Delete a single vulnerability by ID.

Property Value Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/vulns/{object_id} Auth Required Tags VulnerabilityGeneric

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description workspace_name string yes Workspace name object_id integer yes Vulnerability ID

Response: 204 No Content

No response body.

Example:

curl -s -b cookies.txt -X DELETE \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns/1"

Error Codes:

Code Description 401 Unauthorized 404 Vulnerability or workspace not found

docs.title: 7. Bulk Delete Vulnerabilities
docs.text:
Delete multiple vulnerabilities by their IDs in a single request.

Property Value Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/vulns Auth Required Tags VulnerabilityGeneric Request Body application/json

Request Body:

{
  "ids": [1, 2, 3, 15, 42]
}

Response: 204 No Content

Example:

curl -s -b cookies.txt -X DELETE \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"ids": [1, 2, 3, 15, 42]}'

Error Codes:

Code Description 400 Invalid request body 401 Unauthorized 404 Workspace not found

docs.title: 8. Bulk Update Vulnerabilities
docs.text:
Update one or more fields on multiple vulnerabilities in a single request.

Property Value Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/vulns Auth Required Tags VulnerabilityGeneric Request Body application/json

Request Body:

{
  "ids": [1, 2, 3],
  "update": {
    "severity": "high",
    "status": "closed"
  }
}

The update object supports any writable field from the vulnerability schema.

Response: 204 No Content

Example:

# Mark multiple vulnerabilities as closed
curl -s -b cookies.txt -X PATCH \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"ids": [1, 2, 3], "update": {"status": "closed"}}'

# Bulk change severity
curl -s -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" -X PATCH \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"ids": [10, 11, 12], "update": {"severity": "high", "confirmed": true}}'

Error Codes:

Code Description 400 Invalid request body or update fields 401 Unauthorized 404 Workspace not found

docs.title: 9. Count Vulnerabilities
docs.text:
Get a count of vulnerabilities, optionally grouped by a field.

Property Value Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/vulns/count Auth Required Tags Vulnerability

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description workspace_name string yes Workspace name

Query Parameters:

Parameter Type Description group_by string Field to group counts by (e.g., severity, status, confirmed)

Response: 200 OK

{
  "total_count": 142,
  "groups": [
    {"count": 5, "severity": "critical"},
    {"count": 23, "severity": "high"},
    {"count": 67, "severity": "medium"},
    {"count": 35, "severity": "low"},
    {"count": 12, "severity": "informational"}
  ]
}

Example:

# Total count
curl -s -b cookies.txt \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns/count"

# Grouped by severity
curl -s -b cookies.txt \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns/count?group_by=severity"

# Grouped by status
curl -s -b cookies.txt \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns/count?group_by=status"

Error Codes:

Code Description 401 Unauthorized 404 Workspace not found

docs.title: 10. Multi-Workspace Count
docs.text:
Count vulnerabilities across multiple workspaces.

Property Value Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/vulns/count_multi_workspace Auth Required Tags VulnerabilityGeneric

Response: 200 OK

Returns vulnerability counts broken down by workspace.

Example:

curl -s -b cookies.txt \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns/count_multi_workspace"

Error Codes:

Code Description 400 Bad request 401 Unauthorized

docs.title: 11. Filter Vulnerabilities
docs.text:
Retrieve vulnerabilities matching complex filter expressions.

Property Value Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/vulns/filter Auth Required Tags Filter, Vulnerability

Query Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description q string yes JSON-encoded filter expression

The filter expression uses the Faraday filter query language. See Faraday Filters documentation for full syntax.

Common filter operators:

Operator Description Example == Equals {"name": "severity", "op": "==", "val": "critical"} != Not equals {"name": "status", "op": "!=", "val": "closed"} has Contains (arrays) {"name": "tags", "op": "has", "val": "sqli"} like Pattern match {"name": "name", "op": "like", "val": "%injection%"} >, <, >=, <= Comparison {"name": "id", "op": ">", "val": 100}

Filter expression structure:

{
  "filters": [
    {"name": "severity", "op": "==", "val": "critical"}
  ]
}

Compound filter (AND):

{
  "filters": [
    {"name": "severity", "op": "==", "val": "critical"},
    {"name": "status", "op": "==", "val": "open"},
    {"name": "confirmed", "op": "==", "val": true}
  ]
}

Response: 200 OK

Returns a paginated list of matching vulnerabilities (same structure as List Vulnerabilities).

Example:

# Find all critical, open, confirmed vulnerabilities
curl -s -b cookies.txt \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns/filter?q=%7B%22filters%22%3A%5B%7B%22name%22%3A%22severity%22%2C%22op%22%3A%22%3D%3D%22%2C%22val%22%3A%22critical%22%7D%2C%7B%22name%22%3A%22status%22%2C%22op%22%3A%22%3D%3D%22%2C%22val%22%3A%22open%22%7D%5D%7D"

# Same query (URL-decoded for readability):
# ?q={"filters":[{"name":"severity","op":"==","val":"critical"},{"name":"status","op":"==","val":"open"}]}

Error Codes:

Code Description 400 Invalid filter expression 401 Unauthorized 404 Workspace not found

docs.title: 12. Upload Attachment
docs.text:
Upload a file attachment (evidence) to a vulnerability.

Property Value Method POST Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/vulns/{vuln_id}/attachment Auth Required Tags Vulnerability, File Content-Type multipart/form-data

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description workspace_name string yes Workspace name vuln_id integer yes Vulnerability ID

Form Fields:

Field Type Required Description file file yes The file to upload description string -- Description of the evidence

Response: 201 Created

Example:

# Upload a screenshot as evidence
curl -s -b cookies.txt -X POST \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns/1/attachment" \
  -F "file=@screenshot.png" \
  -F "description=SQL injection proof of concept"

# Upload a text file
curl -s -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" -X POST \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns/1/attachment" \
  -F "file=@exploit-output.txt"

Error Codes:

Code Description 400 No file provided or invalid upload 401 Unauthorized 403 Forbidden 404 Vulnerability or workspace not found

docs.title: 13. List Attachments
docs.text:
List all attachments for a specific vulnerability.

Property Value Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/vulns/{vuln_id}/attachment Auth Required Tags Vulnerability, File

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description workspace_name string yes Workspace name vuln_id integer yes Vulnerability ID

Response: 200 OK

Returns a list of attachment metadata objects.

[
  {
    "content_type": "image/png",
    "description": "SQL injection proof of concept",
    "filename": "screenshot.png"
  },
  {
    "content_type": "text/plain",
    "description": "",
    "filename": "exploit-output.txt"
  }
]

Example:

curl -s -b cookies.txt \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns/1/attachment"

Error Codes:

Code Description 401 Unauthorized 403 Forbidden 404 Vulnerability or workspace not found

docs.title: 14. Download Attachment
docs.text:
Download a specific attachment file by filename.

Property Value Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/vulns/{vuln_id}/attachment/{attachment_filename} Auth Required Tags Vulnerability, File

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description workspace_name string yes Workspace name vuln_id integer yes Vulnerability ID filename string yes Attachment filename (URL-encoded if it contains special characters)

Response: 200 OK

Returns the raw file content with the appropriate Content-Type header.

Example:

# Download an attachment
curl -s -b cookies.txt -o screenshot.png \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns/1/attachment/screenshot.png"

Error Codes:

Code Description 401 Unauthorized 404 Attachment, vulnerability, or workspace not found

docs.title: 15. Update Attachment Metadata
docs.text:
Note: This endpoint (PATCH on individual attachment) is not declared in the published 5.10.0 OpenAPI spec. Only GET and DELETE are defined for individual attachments.

Update the metadata (e.g., description) of an existing attachment.

Property Value Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/vulns/{vuln_id}/attachment/{attachment_filename} Auth Required Tags Vulnerability, File Request Body application/json

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description workspace_name string yes Workspace name vuln_id integer yes Vulnerability ID filename string yes Attachment filename

Request Body:

{
  "description": "Updated description for this evidence file"
}

Response: 200 OK

Example:

curl -s -b cookies.txt -X PATCH \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns/1/attachment/screenshot.png" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"description": "Proof of concept - SQL injection in login form"}'

Error Codes:

Code Description 400 Invalid request body 401 Unauthorized 404 Attachment, vulnerability, or workspace not found

docs.title: 16. Delete Attachment
docs.text:
Delete an attachment from a vulnerability.

Property Value Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/vulns/{vuln_id}/attachment/{attachment_filename} Auth Required Tags Vulnerability, File

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description workspace_name string yes Workspace name vuln_id integer yes Vulnerability ID filename string yes Attachment filename

Response: 200 OK

Example:

curl -s -b cookies.txt -X DELETE \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns/1/attachment/screenshot.png"

Error Codes:

Code Description 401 Unauthorized 404 Attachment, vulnerability, or workspace not found

docs.title: 17. Export CSV
docs.text:
Export all vulnerabilities in a workspace as a CSV file.

Property Value Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/vulns/export_csv Auth Required Tags Vulnerability, File

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description workspace_name string yes Workspace name

Response: 200 OK

Returns a CSV file download with Content-Type: text/csv.

Example:

curl -s -b cookies.txt -o vulns-export.csv \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns/export_csv"

Error Codes:

Code Description 401 Unauthorized 404 Workspace not found

docs.title: 18. Top Users
docs.text:
Get a ranking of users by the number of vulnerabilities they have created.

Property Value Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_name}/vulns/top_users Auth Required Tags Vulnerability

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Required Description workspace_name string yes Workspace name

Response: 200 OK

[
  {"username": "admin", "count": 85},
  {"username": "pentester1", "count": 42},
  {"username": "scanner_bot", "count": 15}
]

Example:

curl -s -b cookies.txt \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/pentest-2026/vulns/top_users"

Error Codes:

Code Description 401 Unauthorized 404 Workspace not found

docs.title: Global Endpoints
docs.text:
Important: These global vulnerability endpoints are not declared in the published 5.10.0 OpenAPI specification (faraday_swagger.json). They exist in the codebase spec for version 5.17.0+. The paths and behaviors described below are derived from the codebase and may not be available in all Faraday deployments.

Global endpoints provide cross-workspace access to vulnerabilities. They share the same request/response formats as their workspaced counterparts but operate without a workspace scope.

Base path: /_api/v3/vulns

docs.title: G1. List Vulnerabilities (Global)
docs.text:
Property Value Method GET Path /_api/v3/vulns Auth Required

Retrieve vulnerabilities across all workspaces the authenticated user has access to. Supports the same pagination and sort parameters as the workspaced list endpoint.

curl -s -b cookies.txt \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulns?page=0&page_size=100"

docs.title: G2. Get Vulnerability (Global)
docs.text:
Property Value Method GET Path /_api/v3/vulns/{object_id} Auth Required

Retrieve a single vulnerability by ID without specifying a workspace.

curl -s -b cookies.txt \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulns/1"

docs.title: G3. Bulk Delete (Global)
docs.text:
Property Value Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/vulns Auth Required Request Body application/json -- {"ids": [...]}

Delete multiple vulnerabilities by IDs across workspaces.

curl -s -b cookies.txt -X DELETE \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulns" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"ids": [1, 2, 3]}'

Response: 204 No Content

docs.title: G4. Bulk Update (Global)
docs.text:
Property Value Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/vulns Auth Required Request Body application/json -- {"ids": [...], "update": {...}}

Update fields on multiple vulnerabilities across workspaces.

curl -s -b cookies.txt -X PATCH \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulns" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"ids": [1, 2, 3], "update": {"severity": "high"}}'

Response: 204 No Content

docs.title: G5. Count (Global)
docs.text:
Property Value Method GET Path /_api/v3/vulns/count Auth Required

Count all vulnerabilities across all workspaces.

curl -s -b cookies.txt \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulns/count"

docs.title: G6. Multi-Workspace Count (Global)
docs.text:
Property Value Method GET Path /_api/v3/vulns/count_multi_workspace Auth Required

Count vulnerabilities grouped by workspace.

curl -s -b cookies.txt \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulns/count_multi_workspace"

docs.title: G7. Filter (Global)
docs.text:
Property Value Method GET Path /_api/v3/vulns/filter Auth Required

Filter vulnerabilities across all workspaces. Uses the same q query parameter and filter expression syntax as the workspaced filter endpoint.

curl -s -b cookies.txt \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulns/filter?q=%7B%22filters%22%3A%5B%7B%22name%22%3A%22severity%22%2C%22op%22%3A%22%3D%3D%22%2C%22val%22%3A%22critical%22%7D%5D%7D"

docs.title: G8. Upload Attachment (Global)
docs.text:
Property Value Method POST Path /_api/v3/vulns/{vuln_id}/attachment Auth Required Content-Type multipart/form-data

Upload an attachment to a vulnerability without specifying a workspace. See Upload Attachment for details.

curl -s -b cookies.txt -X POST \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulns/1/attachment" \
  -F "file=@evidence.png"

Response: 201 Created

docs.title: G9. List Attachments (Global)
docs.text:
Property Value Method GET Path /_api/v3/vulns/{vuln_id}/attachment Auth Required

List all attachments for a vulnerability. See List Attachments for response format.

curl -s -b cookies.txt \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulns/1/attachment"

docs.title: G10. Download Attachment (Global)
docs.text:
Property Value Method GET Path /_api/v3/vulns/{vuln_id}/attachment/{attachment_filename} Auth Required

Download a specific attachment file.

curl -s -b cookies.txt -o evidence.png \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulns/1/attachment/evidence.png"

docs.title: G11. Update Attachment Metadata (Global)
docs.text:
Property Value Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/vulns/{vuln_id}/attachment/{attachment_filename} Auth Required Request Body application/json

Update metadata on an attachment. See Update Attachment Metadata for details.

curl -s -b cookies.txt -X PATCH \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulns/1/attachment/evidence.png" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"description": "Updated description"}'

docs.title: G12. Delete Attachment (Global)
docs.text:
Property Value Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/vulns/{vuln_id}/attachment/{attachment_filename} Auth Required

Delete an attachment from a vulnerability.

curl -s -b cookies.txt -X DELETE \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulns/1/attachment/evidence.png"

docs.title: G13. Export CSV (Global)
docs.text:
Property Value Method GET Path /_api/v3/vulns/export_csv Auth Required

Export all vulnerabilities (across all accessible workspaces) as a CSV file.

curl -s -b cookies.txt -o all-vulns.csv \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulns/export_csv"

docs.title: G14. Top Users (Global)
docs.text:
Property Value Method GET Path /_api/v3/vulns/top_users Auth Required

Get a ranking of users by vulnerability creation count across all workspaces.

curl -s -b cookies.txt \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulns/top_users"

docs.title: Deprecated Endpoints
docs.text: Endpoint Status Replacement

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
All endpoints require authentication. Include one of the following with every request:

# Option 1: Cookie-based session (obtain via POST /_api/login)
curl -b cookies.txt ...

# Option 2: API token
curl -H "Authorization: Token <base64-token>" ...

docs.title: Pagination
docs.text:
All list endpoints support pagination:

?page=0&page_size=50&sort=severity&sort_dir=desc
Parameter Type Default Description page integer 0 Page number (0-indexed) page_size integer 20 Results per page sort string -- Sort field sort_dir string asc asc or desc

docs.title: Severity Values
docs.text:
Severity is a required field and must be one of:

Value Description critical Critical severity high High severity medium Medium severity low Low severity informational Informational finding unclassified Not yet classified

docs.title: Status Values
docs.text: Value Description open Vulnerability is open and unresolved closed Vulnerability has been resolved re-opened Vulnerability was closed but detected again risk-accepted Vulnerability acknowledged but accepted as a risk

docs.title: Ease of Resolution Values
docs.text: Value Description trivial Can be fixed in minutes with no risk simple Straightforward fix, low risk moderate Requires planning, moderate effort difficult Significant effort, may involve architectural changes infeasible Cannot be practically fixed

docs.title: Vulnerability Type Differences
docs.text: Field vulnerability vulnerability_web Required fields name, severity, type name, severity, type, parent, parent_type Web-specific fields N/A method, path, website, request, response, status_code, params, pname, query, owasp Use case Infrastructure, network, OS-level findings Web application vulnerabilities (XSS, SQLi, CSRF, etc.)

docs.title: Standard Error Response
docs.text:
All error responses follow this format:

{
  "messages": ["Error description here"]
}

docs.title: Standard HTTP Response Codes
docs.text: Code Meaning Used By 200 Success GET, PUT, PATCH (single), attachment operations 201 Created POST (create vuln, upload attachment) 204 No Content DELETE, PATCH (bulk operations) 400 Bad Request Validation errors, invalid filter expressions 401 Unauthorized Missing or invalid authentication 403 Forbidden Insufficient permissions 404 Not Found Workspace, vulnerability, or attachment not found 409 Conflict Duplicate vulnerability 500 Server Error Internal server error

docs.title: Workspaced (18 endpoints)
docs.text: # Method Path Description 1 GET /ws/{ws}/vulns List vulnerabilities 2 POST /ws/{ws}/vulns Create vulnerability 3 GET /ws/{ws}/vulns/{id} Get vulnerability 4 PUT /ws/{ws}/vulns/{id} Full update 5 PATCH /ws/{ws}/vulns/{id} Partial update 6 DELETE /ws/{ws}/vulns/{id} Delete vulnerability 7 DELETE /ws/{ws}/vulns Bulk delete 8 PATCH /ws/{ws}/vulns Bulk update 9 GET /ws/{ws}/vulns/count Count (with group_by) 10 GET /ws/{ws}/vulns/count_multi_workspace Multi-workspace count 11 GET /ws/{ws}/vulns/filter Filter vulnerabilities 12 POST /ws/{ws}/vulns/{id}/attachment Upload attachment 13 GET /ws/{ws}/vulns/{id}/attachment List attachments 14 GET /ws/{ws}/vulns/{id}/attachment/{file} Download attachment 15 PATCH /ws/{ws}/vulns/{id}/attachment/{file} Update attachment metadata 16 DELETE /ws/{ws}/vulns/{id}/attachment/{file} Delete attachment 17 GET /ws/{ws}/vulns/export_csv Export as CSV 18 GET /ws/{ws}/vulns/top_users Top users by vuln count

docs.title: Global (14 endpoints)
docs.text:
# Method Path Description G1 GET /vulns List vulnerabilities (all workspaces) G2 GET /vulns/{id} Get vulnerability G3 DELETE /vulns Bulk delete G4 PATCH /vulns Bulk update G5 GET /vulns/count Count all G6 GET /vulns/count_multi_workspace Multi-workspace count G7 GET /vulns/filter Filter all G8 POST /vulns/{id}/attachment Upload attachment G9 GET /vulns/{id}/attachment List attachments G10 GET /vulns/{id}/attachment/{file} Download attachment G11 PATCH /vulns/{id}/attachment/{file} Update attachment metadata G12 DELETE /vulns/{id}/attachment/{file} Delete attachment G13 GET /vulns/export_csv Export as CSV G14 GET /vulns/top_users Top users

All paths above are relative to /_api/v3. Full paths include the /_api/v3 prefix.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• [[README]] -- API Endpoint Reference index
  • [[hosts]] -- Hosts API (vulnerabilities are linked to hosts)
  • [[services]] -- Services API (vulnerabilities can be linked to services)
  • [[vulnerability-templates]] -- Vulnerability Templates API (reusable vulnerability definitions)
  • Faraday Filters documentation -- Filter query syntax
  • OpenAPI Spec Audit -- Full comparison of codebase vs published specs

docs.title: Vulnerability Templates API
docs.text:
Base URL: https://localhost:5985 API Version: v3 Authentication: Cookie-based session or API token (Authorization: Token <token>) Content-Type: application/json

Vulnerability Templates provide reusable definitions for common vulnerability findings. They allow security teams to maintain a library of standard vulnerability descriptions, resolutions, and metadata that can be applied when creating new vulnerability instances. Templates are global resources and are not scoped to a specific workspace.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• VulnerabilityTemplate Schema
  • Endpoints
  • List Templates
  • Create Template
  • Get Template
  • Full Update Template
  • Partial Update Template
  • Delete Template
  • Bulk Delete Templates
  • Bulk Update Templates
  • Bulk Create Templates
  • Filter Templates
  • Common Error Codes

docs.title: VulnerabilityTemplate Schema
docs.text:
Property Type Required Description id integer read-only Primary key. _id integer read-only Internal document ID. Same as id. _rev string read-only Document revision identifier. name string yes Template name (vulnerability title). exploitation string yes Exploitation difficulty. One of: "unclassified", "informational", "low", "med", "high", "critical". description string no Detailed description of the vulnerability. Also accessible as desc. desc string no Alias for description. resolution string no Recommended remediation steps. references array of strings no External reference URLs. Also accessible as refs. refs array of strings no Alias for references. cwe string no Associated CWE identifier (e.g., "CWE-79"). cve array of strings no Associated CVE identifiers (e.g., ["CVE-2024-1234"]). impact object no Impact assessment object with keys such as confidentiality, integrity, availability, accountability. easeofresolution string no Ease of resolution. One of: "trivial", "simple", "moderate", "difficult", "infeasible". policyviolations array of strings no Policy violations associated with this template. data string no Additional data or notes. external_id string no External identifier for integration with third-party systems. creator string read-only Username of the template creator. create_at datetime read-only ISO 8601 creation timestamp. creator_id integer read-only User ID of the template creator. customfields object no Key-value pairs for custom field values defined via the Custom Fields API. > Note: The cvss2, cvss3, and cvss4 fields are not present in the published VulnerabilityTemplate schema. CVSS scoring is only available on Vulnerability and VulnerabilityWeb objects.

Required fields for creation: name, exploitation

docs.title: Example VulnerabilityTemplate Object
docs.text:
{
  "id": 1,
  "_id": 1,
  "_rev": "",
  "name": "SQL Injection",
  "exploitation": "high",
  "description": "The application is vulnerable to SQL injection attacks through unsanitized user input in query parameters.",
  "resolution": "Use parameterized queries or prepared statements. Apply input validation and output encoding.",
  "references": [
    "https://owasp.org/www-community/attacks/SQL_Injection",
    "https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/89.html"
  ],
  "cwe": ["CWE-89"],
  "cve": [],
  "impact": {
    "confidentiality": true,
    "integrity": true,
    "availability": false,
    "accountability": false
  },
  "easeofresolution": "moderate",
  "policyviolations": ["PCI-DSS-6.5.1"],
  "data": "",
  "external_id": "",
  "creator": "admin",
  "create_at": "2026-02-27T10:00:00+00:00",
  "creator_id": 1,
  "customfields": {},
  "cvss2": {},
  "cvss3": {
    "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:N"
  },
  "cvss4": {}
}

docs.title: Endpoints
docs.text: All endpoints operate under the path prefix /_api/v3/vulnerability_template. Templates are global resources and do not require a workspace context.

docs.title: 1. List Templates
docs.text:
Retrieve a paginated list of all vulnerability templates.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/vulnerability_template Auth Required

Query Parameters:

Parameter Type Default Description page integer 0 Page number (zero-indexed). page_size integer 20 Number of results per page. sort string name Field to sort by. sort_dir string asc Sort direction (asc or desc).

Response: 200 OK

{
  "count": 85,
  "data": [
    { "...template object..." }
  ]
}

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulnerability_template?page=0&page_size=10" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication.

docs.title: 2. Create Template
docs.text:
Create a new vulnerability template.

Method POST Path /_api/v3/vulnerability_template Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "name": "Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)",
  "exploitation": "high",
  "description": "The application reflects user-supplied input without proper encoding, allowing injection of arbitrary JavaScript.",
  "resolution": "Encode all user-supplied output using context-appropriate encoding. Implement a Content Security Policy (CSP).",
  "references": [
    "https://owasp.org/www-community/attacks/xss/"
  ],
  "cwe": ["CWE-79"],
  "easeofresolution": "simple",
  "policyviolations": [],
  "customfields": {},
  "cvss3": {
    "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N"
  }
}

Required fields: name, exploitation

Response: 201 Created

Returns the newly created template object.

Example:

curl -s -X POST \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulnerability_template" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{
    "name": "Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)",
    "exploitation": "high",
    "description": "Reflected XSS vulnerability in search parameter.",
    "resolution": "Apply output encoding on all user-controlled input.",
    "cwe": ["CWE-79"],
    "easeofresolution": "simple"
  }'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Validation error (e.g., missing name or exploitation). 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 409 Template with this name already exists.

docs.title: 3. Get Template
docs.text:
Retrieve a single vulnerability template by its ID.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/vulnerability_template/{template_id} Auth Required

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Description template_id integer Template ID.

Response: 200 OK

Returns the full template object.

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulnerability_template/1" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 404 Template not found.

docs.title: 4. Full Update Template
docs.text:
Replace all mutable fields of a template. Fields not included in the request body are reset to their defaults.

Method PUT Path /_api/v3/vulnerability_template/{template_id} Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "name": "SQL Injection (Updated)",
  "exploitation": "critical",
  "description": "Updated description with additional attack vectors.",
  "resolution": "Use parameterized queries. Apply WAF rules as a defense-in-depth measure.",
  "references": [
    "https://owasp.org/www-community/attacks/SQL_Injection"
  ],
  "cwe": ["CWE-89"],
  "cve": ["CVE-2024-1234"],
  "easeofresolution": "moderate",
  "policyviolations": ["PCI-DSS-6.5.1"],
  "customfields": {}
}

Response: 200 OK

Returns the updated template object.

Example:

curl -s -X PUT \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulnerability_template/1" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{
    "name": "SQL Injection (Updated)",
    "exploitation": "critical",
    "description": "Updated description.",
    "resolution": "Use parameterized queries.",
    "cwe": ["CWE-89"],
    "easeofresolution": "moderate"
  }'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Validation error. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 404 Template not found. 409 Name conflict with another template.

docs.title: 5. Partial Update Template
docs.text:
Update only the specified fields of a template. Omitted fields remain unchanged.

Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/vulnerability_template/{template_id} Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "exploitation": "critical",
  "resolution": "Upgraded remediation guidance."
}

Response: 200 OK

Returns the updated template object.

Example:

curl -s -X PATCH \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulnerability_template/1" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{"exploitation": "critical"}'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Validation error. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 404 Template not found.

docs.title: 6. Delete Template
docs.text:
Delete a single vulnerability template.

Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/vulnerability_template/{template_id} Auth Required

Path Parameters:

Parameter Type Description template_id integer Template ID.

Response: 204 No Content

No response body.

Example:

curl -s -X DELETE \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulnerability_template/1" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Error Codes:

Code Reason 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 404 Template not found.

docs.title: 7. Bulk Delete Templates
docs.text:
Delete multiple vulnerability templates in a single request.

Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/vulnerability_template Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "ids": [1, 2, 3]
}

Response: 204 No Content

No response body.

Example:

curl -s -X DELETE \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulnerability_template" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{"ids": [1, 2, 3]}'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Invalid request body or empty ids array. 401 Missing or invalid authentication.

docs.title: 8. Bulk Update Templates
docs.text:
Update multiple vulnerability templates with the same set of field values.

Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/vulnerability_template Auth Required

Request Body:

{
  "ids": [1, 2, 3],
  "update": {
    "exploitation": "critical"
  }
}

Response: 200 OK

{
  "updated": 3
}

Example:

curl -s -X PATCH \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulnerability_template" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '{"ids": [1, 2, 3], "update": {"exploitation": "critical"}}'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Invalid request body, empty ids, or invalid fields in update. 401 Missing or invalid authentication.

docs.title: 9. Bulk Create Templates
docs.text:
Create multiple vulnerability templates in a single request.

Method POST Path /_api/v3/vulnerability_template/bulk_create Auth Required

Request Body:

[
  {
    "name": "SQL Injection",
    "exploitation": "high",
    "description": "SQL injection via unsanitized input.",
    "cwe": ["CWE-89"],
    "easeofresolution": "moderate"
  },
  {
    "name": "Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)",
    "exploitation": "high",
    "description": "Reflected XSS in search functionality.",
    "cwe": ["CWE-79"],
    "easeofresolution": "simple"
  }
]

Response: 201 Created

{
  "created": 2,
  "templates": [
    { "...template object..." },
    { "...template object..." }
  ]
}

Example:

curl -s -X POST \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulnerability_template/bulk_create" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -k \
  -d '[
    {"name": "SQL Injection", "exploitation": "high", "cwe": ["CWE-89"]},
    {"name": "XSS", "exploitation": "high", "cwe": ["CWE-79"]}
  ]'

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Validation error in one or more template objects. 401 Missing or invalid authentication. 409 One or more template names conflict with existing templates.

docs.title: 10. Filter Templates
docs.text:
Retrieve vulnerability templates matching a filter expression.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/vulnerability_template/filter Auth Required

Query Parameters:

Parameter Type Description q string (JSON) Filter expression. See Filter Syntax below. page integer Page number (zero-indexed). page_size integer Results per page.

Response: 200 OK

Same paginated format as List Templates.

Example:

curl -s -X GET \
  "https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/vulnerability_template/filter?q=%7B%22filters%22%3A%5B%7B%22name%22%3A%22exploitation%22%2C%22op%22%3A%22eq%22%2C%22val%22%3A%22critical%22%7D%5D%7D" \
  -H "Authorization: Token YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -k

Decoded q parameter:

{
  "filters": [
    {"name": "exploitation", "op": "eq", "val": "critical"}
  ]
}

Error Codes:

Code Reason 400 Malformed filter expression. 401 Missing or invalid authentication.

docs.title: Filter Syntax
docs.text:
Filters use a JSON structure with an array of filter objects:

{
  "filters": [
    {"name": "<field>", "op": "<operator>", "val": "<value>"}
  ],
  "conjunction": "AND"
}

Supported operators: eq, ne, lt, le, gt, ge, like, ilike, in, not_in, is_null, is_not_null

Filterable fields: name, exploitation, easeofresolution, creator, external_id, create_at

docs.title: Common Error Codes
docs.text: HTTP Status Meaning 200 Success. Response body contains the requested data. 201 Created. New template was successfully created. 204 No Content. Template was deleted successfully. 400 Bad Request. Malformed JSON, missing required fields, or invalid filter expression. 401 Unauthorized. Authentication token is missing, expired, or invalid. 404 Not Found. The template or endpoint does not exist. 409 Conflict. A template with the same name already exists. 500 Internal Server Error. Unexpected server-side failure.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• All curl examples include -k to skip TLS certificate verification for local development. Remove this flag in production environments with valid certificates.
  • Pagination is zero-indexed: page=0 returns the first page.
  • The exploitation field is the severity level and is required for all templates.
  • The desc and refs fields are aliases for description and references respectively. Either form is accepted in requests; responses return both forms.
  • Templates are global and not scoped to workspaces. Any authenticated user can access and manage templates.
  • Deleting a template does not affect existing vulnerabilities that were created from it.

docs.title: Workspaces API
docs.text:
Base URL: https://localhost:5985 API Version: v3 Authentication: All endpoints require session cookie or API token (see Authentication) Content-Type: application/json

Workspaces are the top-level organizational unit in Faraday. Every host, service, vulnerability, command, and credential belongs to a workspace. A workspace defines a scope (target network ranges) and tracks vulnerability statistics over time via histograms.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• Workspace Schema
  • Endpoints Overview
  • Standard CRUD Operations
  • List Workspaces
  • Create Workspace
  • Get Workspace
  • Full Update Workspace
  • Partial Update Workspace
  • Delete Workspace
  • Bulk Operations
  • Bulk Delete Workspaces
  • Special Actions
  • Filter Workspaces
  • Toggle Read-Only Mode
  • Error Reference

docs.title: Workspace Schema
docs.text:
The Workspace object represents a single workspace in Faraday.

Property Type Required Read-Only Nullable Description _id integer -- Yes No Internal database identifier id integer -- No No Workspace identifier name string Yes No No Unique workspace name. Used as the identifier in most API paths. Must not contain spaces or special characters description string -- No Yes Free-text description of the workspace purpose customer string -- No Yes Customer or project name associated with the workspace (max 250 characters) active boolean -- No No Whether the workspace is active. Inactive workspaces are hidden from default views readonly boolean -- No No When true, prevents modifications to workspace data (hosts, vulns, etc.) public boolean -- No No Whether the workspace is publicly accessible to all authenticated users scope array -- No No Array of CIDR ranges or hostnames defining the target scope (e.g., ["10.0.0.0/8", "192.168.1.0/24"]) duration object -- No No Duration metadata for the workspace engagement importance integer -- No No Priority/importance level of the workspace stats object -- No No Vulnerability statistics object (see Stats Object below) histogram array -- No No Array of historical vulnerability count snapshots (see Histogram Object below) users array of strings -- No No List of usernames with access to this workspace create_date string (date-time) -- Yes No ISO 8601 timestamp of workspace creation update_date string (date-time) -- Yes No ISO 8601 timestamp of last workspace modification last_run_agent_date string (date-time) -- Yes Yes ISO 8601 timestamp of the last agent execution in this workspace

docs.title: Stats Object
docs.text:
The stats object provides a summary of vulnerability counts by severity within the workspace.

{
  "total_vulns": 142,
  "info_vulns": 12,
  "low_vulns": 35,
  "med_vulns": 58,
  "high_vulns": 30,
  "critical_vulns": 7,
  "std_vulns": 120,
  "web_vulns": 22,
  "code_vulns": 0
}

docs.title: Histogram Object
docs.text:
Each histogram entry records vulnerability counts at a point in time.

Property Type Description date string Date of the snapshot medium integer Count of medium-severity vulnerabilities high integer Count of high-severity vulnerabilities critical integer Count of critical-severity vulnerabilities confirmed integer Count of confirmed vulnerabilities

docs.title: Endpoints Overview
docs.text: # Method Path Summary 1 GET /_api/v3/ws List all workspaces 2 POST /_api/v3/ws Create a new workspace 3 GET /_api/v3/ws/{object_id} Get a workspace by ID 4 PUT /_api/v3/ws/{object_id} Full update of a workspace 5 PATCH /_api/v3/ws/{object_id} Partial update of a workspace 6 DELETE /_api/v3/ws/{object_id} Delete a workspace 7 DELETE /_api/v3/ws Bulk delete workspaces 9 GET /_api/v3/ws/filter Filter workspaces

docs.title: 1. List Workspaces
docs.text:
Retrieve a paginated list of all workspaces accessible to the authenticated user.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws Authentication Required (session cookie or API token)

docs.title: Query Parameters

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
• Status: 200 OK
  • Body: Array of Workspace objects

docs.title: 2. Create Workspace
docs.text:
Create a new workspace. The workspace name must be unique.

Method POST Path /_api/v3/ws Authentication Required (session cookie or API token)

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: Field Type Required Description name string Yes Unique workspace name description string No Workspace description customer string No Customer/project name active boolean No Active status (default: true) public boolean No Public visibility (default: false) readonly boolean No Read-only mode (default: false) scope array No Array of CIDR ranges or hostnames importance integer No Priority level

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
• Status: 201 Created
  • Body: Created Workspace object

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \

    "scope": ["10.0.0.0/8", "webapp.acme.com"],

docs.title: 4. Full Update Workspace
docs.text:
Replace all fields of an existing workspace. All writable fields must be provided; omitted fields may be reset to defaults.

Method PUT Path /_api/v3/ws/{object_id} Authentication Required (session cookie or API token)

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description object_id integer Yes The workspace ID

docs.title: Request Body

Complete Workspace object with all writable fields.

Field Type Required Description name string Yes Unique workspace name description string No Workspace description customer string No Customer/project name active boolean No Active status public boolean No Public visibility readonly boolean No Read-only mode scope array No Array of CIDR ranges or hostnames importance integer No Priority level

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
• Status: 200 OK
  • Body: Updated Workspace object

  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \

docs.title: 5. Partial Update Workspace
docs.text:
Update one or more fields of an existing workspace without affecting other fields.

Method PATCH Path /_api/v3/ws/{object_id} Authentication Required (session cookie or API token)

docs.title: 6. Delete Workspace
docs.text:
Permanently delete a workspace and all of its associated data (hosts, services, vulnerabilities, commands, credentials, comments).

Warning: This action is irreversible. All data within the workspace will be permanently destroyed.

Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/ws/{object_id} Authentication Required (session cookie or API token)

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description object_id integer Yes The workspace ID

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
• Status: 204 No Content
  • Body: Empty

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -X DELETE \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/3

No response body is returned on success.

docs.title: Error Codes
docs.text: Status Description 401 Unauthorized -- missing or invalid authentication 403 Forbidden -- insufficient permissions to delete workspaces 404 Not found -- no workspace with the given ID

docs.title: 7. Bulk Delete Workspaces
docs.text:
Delete multiple workspaces in a single request by providing an array of workspace IDs.

Warning: This action is irreversible. All data within the specified workspaces will be permanently destroyed.

Method DELETE Path /_api/v3/ws Authentication Required (session cookie or API token)

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: Field Type Required Description ids array of integers Yes List of workspace IDs to delete

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
• Status: 204 No Content
  • Body: Empty

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
curl -s -X DELETE \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"ids": [4, 5, 6]}' \
  https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws

No response body is returned on success.

docs.title: Error Codes
docs.text: Status Description 400 Bad request -- invalid or empty ids array 401 Unauthorized -- missing or invalid authentication 403 Forbidden -- insufficient permissions

docs.title: 9. Filter Workspaces
docs.text:
Query workspaces using a structured filter expression. Supports complex filtering, sorting, and grouping through a JSON-encoded query parameter. Uses FlaskRestless-style filter syntax.

Method GET Path /_api/v3/ws/filter Authentication Required (session cookie or API token)

docs.title: Query Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description q string (JSON) Yes URL-encoded JSON filter expression

docs.title: Filter Syntax
docs.text:
The q parameter accepts a JSON object with the following structure:

{
  "filters": [
    {
      "name": "<field_name>",
      "op": "<operator>",
      "val": "<value>"
    }
  ],
  "order_by": [
    {
      "field": "<field_name>",
      "direction": "asc|desc"
    }
  ],
  "group_by": [
    {
      "field": "<field_name>"
    }
  ]
}

Supported Operators:

Operator Description Example == Equal to {"name": "active", "op": "==", "val": true} != Not equal to {"name": "customer", "op": "!=", "val": null} > Greater than {"name": "importance", "op": ">", "val": 2} < Less than {"name": "importance", "op": "<", "val": 5} >= Greater than or equal {"name": "importance", "op": ">=", "val": 3} <= Less than or equal {"name": "importance", "op": "<=", "val": 3} like SQL LIKE pattern {"name": "name", "op": "like", "val": "%pentest%"} in Value in list {"name": "id", "op": "in", "val": [1, 2, 3]} not_in Value not in list {"name": "id", "op": "not_in", "val": [4, 5]} is_null Field is null {"name": "customer", "op": "is_null", "val": ""}

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
• Status: 200 OK
  • Body: Filtered array of Workspace objects

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
# Find all active workspaces with "pentest" in their name
FILTER='{"filters":[{"name":"active","op":"==","val":true},{"name":"name","op":"like","val":"%pentest%"}],"order_by":[{"field":"create_date","direction":"desc"}]}'

curl -s -G \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  --data-urlencode "q=$FILTER" \
  https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws/filter

[
  {
    "_id": 1,
    "id": 1,
    "name": "pentest-2026-q1",
    "description": "Q1 2026 External Penetration Test",
    "customer": "Acme Corp",
    "active": true,
    "readonly": false,
    "public": false,
    "scope": ["10.0.0.0/8"],
    "duration": {},
    "importance": 3,
    "stats": {
      "total_vulns": 142,
      "info_vulns": 12,
      "low_vulns": 35,
      "med_vulns": 58,
      "high_vulns": 30,
      "critical_vulns": 7,
      "std_vulns": 120,
      "web_vulns": 22,
      "code_vulns": 0
    },
    "histogram": [],
    "create_date": "2026-01-10T08:30:00+00:00",
    "update_date": "2026-02-20T14:22:00+00:00",
    "last_run_agent_date": null
  }
]

docs.title: 12. Toggle Read-Only Mode
docs.text:
Note: This endpoint is not present in the published 5.10.0 OpenAPI specification. It exists in later versions of the codebase (5.17.0+).

Change the read-only status of a workspace. When a workspace is in read-only mode, no modifications can be made to its data (hosts, services, vulnerabilities, etc.). Calling this endpoint toggles the current readonly value.

Method PUT Path /_api/v3/ws/{workspace_id}/change_readonly/ Authentication Required (session cookie or API token)

docs.title: Path Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description workspace_id integer Yes The workspace ID to toggle read-only mode

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text: None required.

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
• Status: 200 OK
  • Body: Confirmation message

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
# Enable read-only mode on workspace 1
curl -s -X PUT \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \

{
  "message": "Workspace readonly status changed successfully"
}

# Call again to disable read-only mode (toggles the state)
curl -s -X PUT \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \

docs.title: Error Codes
docs.text: Status Description 401 Unauthorized -- missing or invalid authentication 403 Forbidden -- insufficient permissions 404 Not found -- no workspace with the given ID

docs.title: Error Reference
docs.text:
All error responses follow the standard Faraday API error format:

{
  "messages": ["Error description here"]
}

docs.title: Common Error Codes
docs.text: Status Code Name Description 400 Bad Request The request body is malformed, missing required fields, or contains invalid values 401 Unauthorized No valid authentication credentials were provided. Include a session cookie or Authorization: Token <token> header 403 Forbidden The authenticated user does not have permission to perform this action 404 Not Found The specified workspace ID does not exist 409 Conflict A workspace with the same name already exists (applies to POST, PUT, and PATCH operations) 500 Internal Server Error An unexpected server error occurred. Check server logs for details

docs.title: Validation Error Example
docs.text:
# Attempt to create a workspace without a name
curl -s -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"description": "Missing required name field"}' \
  https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws

{
  "messages": ["name: Missing data for required field."]
}

docs.title: Duplicate Name Error Example
docs.text:
# Attempt to create a workspace with a name that already exists
curl -s -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Token $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "pentest-2026-q1"}' \
  https://localhost:5985/_api/v3/ws

Response: 409 Conflict

{
  "messages": ["Duplicated key found"]
}

docs.title: Agent Executors
docs.text:
Faraday Agent Executors are scripts that integrate external security tools with the Faraday platform. Each executor runs a specific tool, collects results, and sends them to Faraday through the Agent Dispatcher middleware.

Dispatcher version: 3.9.1 Parameter types package: faraday_agent_parameters_types 1.9.1

docs.title: How Executors Work
docs.text:
1. The Faraday Server sends a RUN command via WebSocket to the Agent Dispatcher
  2. The Dispatcher spawns the executor as a subprocess, passing configuration through environment variables
  3. The executor runs the security tool (via CLI, API, or SDK), collects results, and outputs Faraday-compatible JSON to stdout
  4. The Dispatcher reads stdout and POSTs the data to Faraday's bulk create API (/_api/v3/ws/{workspace}/bulk_create)
  5. Vulnerabilities, hosts, and services appear in the selected workspace

docs.title: Environment Variable Conventions
docs.text:
Executors receive parameters through environment variables with specific prefixes:

Prefix Purpose Example EXECUTOR_CONFIG_ Per-execution arguments (targets, options) set at runtime EXECUTOR_CONFIG_TARGET AGENT_CONFIG_ Shared plugin arguments (tags, severity filters) sent by server AGENT_CONFIG_VULN_TAG (no prefix) Persistent credentials/secrets configured at setup time NESSUS_USERNAME

docs.title: Common Agent Parameters
docs.text:
All executors that call get_common_parameters() support these shared filtering and tagging options:

Environment Variable Type Default Description AGENT_CONFIG_IGNORE_INFO Boolean False Skip informational-severity vulnerabilities AGENT_CONFIG_RESOLVE_HOSTNAME Boolean True Resolve hostnames to IP addresses AGENT_CONFIG_MIN_SEVERITY String (none) Minimum severity filter (informational, low, medium, high, critical) AGENT_CONFIG_MAX_SEVERITY String (none) Maximum severity filter AGENT_CONFIG_VULN_TAG CSV (none) Comma-separated tags added to all vulnerabilities AGENT_CONFIG_SERVICE_TAG CSV (none) Comma-separated tags added to all services AGENT_CONFIG_HOSTNAME_TAG CSV (none) Comma-separated tags added to all hosts

docs.title: Network and Vulnerability Scanners
docs.text: Executor Tool Integration Plugin Nmap Nmap CLI NmapPlugin Nessus Tenable Nessus REST API NessusPlugin GVM/OpenVAS Greenbone GVM python-gvm SDK OpenvasPlugin OpenVAS Legacy OpenVAS (legacy) omp CLI OpenvasPlugin Nuclei ProjectDiscovery Nuclei CLI NucleiPlugin Tenable.io Tenable.io pyTenable SDK NessusPlugin Tenable.sc Tenable SecurityCenter pyTenable SDK NessusPlugin Cisco Cyber Vision Cisco Cyber Vision REST API Raw JSON HCL AppScan HCL AppScan on Cloud REST API AppScanPlugin Microsoft Defender MS Defender for Endpoint REST API (OAuth2) Raw JSON

docs.title: Web Application Scanners
docs.text: Executor Tool Integration Plugin Burp Suite Burp Suite Professional REST API BurpPlugin OWASP ZAP OWASP ZAP Python API ZapPlugin Arachni Arachni CLI ArachniPlugin Nikto Nikto CLI NiktoPlugin QualysGuard QualysGuard REST API QualysguardPlugin WPScan WPScan CLI (native) WPScanPlugin WPScan Legacy WPScan (Docker) CLI (Docker) WPScanPlugin w3af w3af CLI W3afPlugin

docs.title: Source Code Analysis
docs.text: Executor Tool Integration Plugin Dependabot GitHub Dependabot GitHub REST API Raw JSON CodeQL GitHub CodeQL GitHub REST API Raw JSON GitHub Secrets GitHub Secret Scanning GitHub REST API Raw JSON SonarQube SonarQube REST API SonarQubeAPIPlugin

docs.title: Pentesting

docs.title: Network Reconnaissance
