docs.title: Host and Application Scanners
docs.text: Executor Tool Integration Plugin InsightVM Rapid7 InsightVM / Nexpose REST API NexposeFullPlugin

docs.title: Generic
docs.text: Executor Tool Integration Plugin Report Processor Any tool File import Auto-detected

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Executors are configured in the Agent Dispatcher's YAML configuration file (typically ~/.faraday/config/dispatcher.yaml).

docs.title: Example: Adding an Nmap Executor
docs.text:
agent:
  agent_name: my_scanner
  executors:
    nmap_scan:
      repo_executor: nmap.py
      max_size: 65536
      varenvs: {}
      params:
        TARGET:
          mandatory: true
          type: list
          base: list
        PORT_LIST:
          mandatory: false
          type: list
          base: list
        OPTION_SV:
          mandatory: false
          type: boolean
          base: boolean
server:
  host: faraday.example.com
  ssl: true
  api_port: 5985
  websocket_port: 5985

docs.title: Using the Configuration Wizard
docs.text:
The recommended way to configure executors is the interactive wizard:

# Install the dispatcher
pip3 install faraday_agent_dispatcher

# Run the configuration wizard
faraday-dispatcher config-wizard

# Start the agent
faraday-dispatcher run --token <registration_token>

The wizard guides you through: 1. Server connection — Host, SSL, ports 2. Agent identity — Name, description 3. Executor selection — Choose from official executors or configure custom ones 4. Credentials — Set environment variables for the selected executor 5. Parameters — Review mandatory and optional execution arguments

docs.title: Custom Executors
docs.text:
You can create custom executors in any language. Requirements:

  • Output Faraday-compatible JSON to stdout
  • Log diagnostic messages to stderr
  • Signal end-of-output with a double newline (\n\n)
  • Read parameters from EXECUTOR_CONFIG_* environment variables
  • Exit with code 0 on success

See the [[plugin-development]] guide for the JSON format expected by Faraday's bulk create API.

docs.title: Dev/Example Executors (6)
docs.text:
These are included in the dispatcher for reference and testing:

Executor Purpose basic_example.py Minimal executor template heroku_discovery_agent.py Heroku app discovery prowlerSample.py AWS Prowler integration sample responder.py Responder tool integration rumble.py Rumble network discovery brainfuck.sh Shell-based test executor

docs.title: HCL AppScan Executor
docs.text: Category: Network and Vulnerability Scanners Script: appscan.py Integration: REST API (HCL AppScan on Cloud) Faraday Plugin: AppScanPlugin Website: https://www.hcltechsw.com/appscan

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Authenticates with the HCL AppScan on Cloud API (formerly IBM AppScan), creates or re-executes a DAST or SAST scan, waits for completion, generates an XML report, downloads it, and parses it into Faraday.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• HCL AppScan on Cloud account with API access
  • API Key ID and Secret (generated in AppScan Cloud settings)
  • Application ID for the target application

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: Variable Required Description HCL_KEY_ID Yes API Key ID for AppScan Cloud authentication HCL_KEY_SECRET Yes API Key Secret HCL_APP_ID Yes AppScan Application ID

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description HCL_SCAN_TYPE string Yes Scan type: DAST (Dynamic) or SAST (Static) HCL_SCAN_TARGET string Conditional Target URL (for DAST) or FileId (for SAST). Required if HCL_SCAN_ID is not provided HCL_SCAN_ID string No Existing scan ID to re-execute HCL_SCAN_NAME string No Scan display name (defaults to a timestamp-based name)

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  appscan_scan:
    repo_executor: appscan.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs:
      HCL_KEY_ID: "${APPSCAN_KEY_ID}"
      HCL_KEY_SECRET: "${APPSCAN_KEY_SECRET}"
      HCL_APP_ID: "${APPSCAN_APP_ID}"
    params:
      HCL_SCAN_TYPE:
        mandatory: true
        type: string
        base: string
      HCL_SCAN_TARGET:
        mandatory: false
        type: string
        base: string

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Authenticates with the AppScan Cloud API at https://cloud.appscan.com/api/v2/
  2. Either creates a new scan or re-executes an existing one by HCL_SCAN_ID
  3. For new DAST scans: creates a scan targeting the specified URL
  4. For new SAST scans: creates a scan for the specified FileId
  5. Polls scan status until completion
  6. Generates an XML report and downloads it
  7. Parses the XML through AppScanPlugin and outputs Faraday JSON

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Use HCL_SCAN_ID to re-execute an existing scan with its previous configuration
  • Without HCL_SCAN_ID, both HCL_SCAN_TYPE and HCL_SCAN_TARGET are required
  • The API endpoint is hardcoded to https://cloud.appscan.com/api/v2/

docs.title: Arachni Executor
docs.text: Category: Web Application Scanners Script: arachni.py Integration: CLI Faraday Plugin: ArachniPlugin Website: https://www.arachni-scanner.com/

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Runs the Arachni web application security scanner CLI against a target URL, then converts the binary .afr result file to XML using arachni_reporter, and parses the XML output into Faraday.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Arachni installed locally
  • Both arachni and arachni_reporter binaries available in the Arachni installation directory

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: Variable Required Description ARACHNI_PATH Yes Filesystem path to the Arachni installation directory (containing arachni and arachni_reporter)

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description NAME_URL url Yes Target URL to scan TIMEOUT string No Scan timeout in HH:MM:SS format

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  arachni_scan:
    repo_executor: arachni.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs:
      ARACHNI_PATH: /opt/arachni
    params:
      NAME_URL:
        mandatory: true
        type: url
        base: string
      TIMEOUT:
        mandatory: false
        type: string
        base: string

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Runs ./arachni <url> [--timeout <HH:MM:SS>] --report-save-path <output.afr> from within the Arachni installation directory
  2. Converts the .afr report to XML: ./arachni_reporter <output.afr> --reporter xml:outfile=<output.xml>
  3. Parses the XML through ArachniPlugin and outputs Faraday JSON

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Arachni development has been discontinued, but the scanner remains functional
  • The executor runs both binaries from ARACHNI_PATH, so the path must point to the directory containing arachni and arachni_reporter

docs.title: Burp Suite Executor
docs.text: Category: Web Application Scanners Script: burp.py Integration: REST API Faraday Plugin: BurpPlugin Website: https://portswigger.net/burp

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Connects to a running Burp Suite Professional instance via its REST API, initiates a scan against one or more target URLs, polls for completion, retrieves the discovered issues, generates an XML report, and parses it into Faraday.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Burp Suite Professional with the REST API enabled
  • REST API key generated (Burp > Settings > Suite > REST API)
  • Burp running as a service/daemon accessible from the dispatcher host

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: Variable Required Description BURP_HOST Yes Burp REST API endpoint (e.g., http://burp.local:1337) BURP_API_KEY Yes REST API authentication key BURP_API_PULL_INTERVAL No Polling interval in seconds (default: 30)

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description TARGET_URL list Yes JSON array of target URLs (e.g., ["https://example.com"]) NAMED_CONFIGURATION string No Burp named configuration (default: "Crawl strategy - fastest")

docs.title: Common Agent Parameters Supported
docs.text: AGENT_CONFIG_IGNORE_INFO, AGENT_CONFIG_RESOLVE_HOSTNAME, AGENT_CONFIG_VULN_TAG, AGENT_CONFIG_SERVICE_TAG, AGENT_CONFIG_HOSTNAME_TAG

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  burp_scan:
    repo_executor: burp.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs:
      BURP_HOST: "http://burp.local:1337"
      BURP_API_KEY: "${BURP_API_KEY}"
      BURP_API_PULL_INTERVAL: "30"
    params:
      TARGET_URL:
        mandatory: true
        type: list
        base: list
      NAMED_CONFIGURATION:
        mandatory: false
        type: string
        base: string

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Connects to the Burp REST API at the configured host
  2. Submits a scan request to /v0.1/scan with the target URLs and named configuration
  3. Polls /v0.1/scan/{task_id} at the configured interval until status is succeeded or failed
  4. Retrieves issue details and issue type definitions from the scan results
  5. Constructs an XML report matching Burp's native format
  6. Parses the XML through BurpPlugin and outputs Faraday JSON

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Multiple URLs can be scanned in a single execution by providing a JSON array
  • The default crawl strategy ("Crawl strategy - fastest") prioritizes speed; use "Crawl strategy - most complete" for thorough scans
  • The BURP_API_PULL_INTERVAL controls how frequently the executor checks scan status; lower values increase responsiveness but also API load

docs.title: GitHub CodeQL Executor
docs.text: Category: Source Code Analysis Script: codeql.py Integration: GitHub REST API Faraday Plugin: Raw JSON (no plugin — generates Faraday JSON directly) Website: https://codeql.github.com/

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Fetches all open code-scanning alerts (typically from CodeQL analysis) for a GitHub repository via the GitHub REST API. Each alert is enriched with description, file location, CWE identifiers, and references. Results are grouped by file path in Faraday.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• GitHub Personal Access Token with security_events scope
  • GitHub Advanced Security (GHAS) enabled on the repository
  • CodeQL analysis configured and running (typically via GitHub Actions)

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: Variable Required Description GITHUB_TOKEN Yes GitHub Personal Access Token GITHUB_OWNER Yes GitHub organization or user owning the repository

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description GITHUB_REPOSITORY string Yes Repository name to query for code scanning alerts

docs.title: Common Agent Parameters Supported
docs.text: AGENT_CONFIG_VULN_TAG, AGENT_CONFIG_HOSTNAME_TAG

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  codeql_scan:
    repo_executor: codeql.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs:
      GITHUB_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
      GITHUB_OWNER: my-org
    params:
      GITHUB_REPOSITORY:
        mandatory: true
        type: string
        base: string

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Fetches all open code-scanning alerts from https://api.github.com/repos/{owner}/{repo}/code-scanning/alerts (paginated)
  2. For each alert, retrieves detailed information including:
  3. Rule description and help text
  4. File path and line number of the finding
  5. CWE identifiers
  6. CVE identifiers (if applicable)
  7. Severity and external references
  8. Groups findings by file path (each file becomes a host as {owner}/{repo}/{path})
  9. Outputs the combined Faraday JSON

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Only open alerts are imported; dismissed or fixed alerts are excluded
  • This executor imports results from any code-scanning tool configured on the repository, not just CodeQL
  • The GitHub token must have access to the repository's security features
  • GitHub Advanced Security is required for private repositories; code scanning is free for public repositories

docs.title: CrackMapExec Executor
docs.text: Category: Pentesting Script: crackmapexec.py Integration: CLI Faraday Plugin: Raw JSON (no plugin — generates Faraday JSON directly) Website: byt3bl33d3r/CrackMapExec

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Runs CrackMapExec against a /24 network range using the SMB protocol. Parses stdout to extract host information, operating systems, SMB signing status, and credential validation results. Optionally supports Meterpreter injection via the met_inject module.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• CrackMapExec installed and available in $PATH
  • Verify: crackmapexec --version

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: (none required)

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description CRACKMAPEXEC_IP ip Yes Target IP address (the executor scans the entire /24 subnet) CRACKMAPEXEC_USER string No SMB username for authentication testing CRACKMAPEXEC_PASS password No SMB password for authentication testing CRACKMAPEXEC_LHOST string No Local handler host IP (for Meterpreter injection) CRACKMAPEXEC_LPORT integer No Local handler port (for Meterpreter injection)

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  cme_scan:
    repo_executor: crackmapexec.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs: {}
    params:
      CRACKMAPEXEC_IP:
        mandatory: true
        type: ip
        base: string
      CRACKMAPEXEC_USER:
        mandatory: false
        type: string
        base: string
      CRACKMAPEXEC_PASS:
        mandatory: false
        type: password
        base: string

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Runs crackmapexec smb <ip>/24 (with optional -u <user> -p <pass> for auth testing)
  2. If LHOST and LPORT are provided, adds --local-auth -M met_inject -o LHOST=<ip> LPORT=<port>
  3. Parses stdout line-by-line to extract:
  4. Host IPs and hostnames
  5. Operating system versions
  6. SMB signing status
  7. Credential validation results
  8. Constructs Faraday JSON with hosts, services (SMB/445), and credentials

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• The executor always scans the full /24 subnet of the provided IP
  • Meterpreter injection (met_inject) requires a running Metasploit handler
  • Use this executor only in authorized pentesting engagements

docs.title: Cisco Cyber Vision Executor
docs.text: Category: Network and Vulnerability Scanners Script: cisco_cybervision.py Integration: REST API Faraday Plugin: Raw JSON (no plugin — generates Faraday JSON directly)

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Connects to the Cisco Cyber Vision platform API to retrieve OT/IoT vulnerability data organized by presets (filter sets). Fetches vulnerability lists per preset with pagination, and constructs Faraday-compatible JSON with CVE and CVSS data for each affected asset.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Cisco Cyber Vision instance with API access enabled
  • API token (Admin > API > Token in the Cyber Vision web UI)

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: Variable Required Description CYBERVISION_TOKEN Yes API authentication token CYBERVISION_HTTPS_URL Yes Base HTTPS URL (e.g., https://192.168.1.100). Must start with https://

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description SPECIFIC_PRESETS list No JSON list of specific preset names to query PRESETS_CONTAINING string No Filter presets whose label contains this substring MY_PRESETS boolean No Only fetch presets in the "My preset" category REFRESH_PRESETS boolean No If true, triggers a data refresh on presets and exits without importing

docs.title: Common Agent Parameters Supported
docs.text: AGENT_CONFIG_VULN_TAG, AGENT_CONFIG_HOSTNAME_TAG

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  cybervision_scan:
    repo_executor: cisco_cybervision.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs:
      CYBERVISION_TOKEN: "${CYBERVISION_TOKEN}"
      CYBERVISION_HTTPS_URL: "https://cybervision.local"
    params:
      SPECIFIC_PRESETS:
        mandatory: false
        type: list
        base: list
      MY_PRESETS:
        mandatory: false
        type: boolean
        base: boolean

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Connects to the Cyber Vision API at the configured URL
  2. Fetches available presets from /api/3.0/presets
  3. Filters presets based on SPECIFIC_PRESETS, PRESETS_CONTAINING, or MY_PRESETS
  4. For each matching preset, retrieves the paginated vulnerability list from /api/3.0/presets/{id}/visualisations/vulnerability-list
  5. Optionally refreshes preset data via /api/3.0/presets/{id}/refreshData
  6. Constructs Faraday JSON with host entries containing CVE IDs, CVSS scores, and vulnerability details

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• The URL must use HTTPS; HTTP is not supported
  • If no preset filter is specified, all accessible presets are queried
  • Use REFRESH_PRESETS to trigger a data refresh before importing; this is useful for ensuring the latest vulnerability data

docs.title: GitHub Dependabot Executor
docs.text: Category: Source Code Analysis Script: dependabot.py Integration: GitHub REST API Faraday Plugin: Raw JSON (no plugin — generates Faraday JSON directly) Website: @dependabot

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Fetches all open Dependabot vulnerability alerts from a GitHub repository, constructs vulnerability records with CVE, CWE, and CVSS data, and outputs Faraday JSON. Vulnerabilities are grouped by manifest file path (each manifest becomes a separate host in Faraday).

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• GitHub Personal Access Token with security_events scope (or Dependabot alert read permissions)
  • Dependabot alerts enabled on the target repository

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: Variable Required Description GITHUB_TOKEN Yes GitHub Personal Access Token GITHUB_OWNER Yes GitHub organization or user owning the repository

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description GITHUB_REPOSITORY string Yes Repository name to query for Dependabot alerts

docs.title: Common Agent Parameters Supported
docs.text: AGENT_CONFIG_VULN_TAG, AGENT_CONFIG_HOSTNAME_TAG

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  dependabot_scan:
    repo_executor: dependabot.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs:
      GITHUB_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
      GITHUB_OWNER: my-org
    params:
      GITHUB_REPOSITORY:
        mandatory: true
        type: string
        base: string

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Fetches all Dependabot alerts from https://api.github.com/repos/{owner}/{repo}/dependabot/alerts (paginated)
  2. For each alert, extracts:
  3. CVE identifiers
  4. CWE identifiers
  5. CVSS vectors (v2 and/or v3)
  6. Affected package and version range
  7. Severity and advisory description
  8. Groups vulnerabilities by manifest file path (e.g., requirements.txt, package.json)
  9. Each manifest file becomes a separate host in Faraday
  10. Outputs the combined Faraday JSON

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Only open (active) alerts are imported; dismissed or fixed alerts are excluded
  • The GitHub token must have access to security alerts for the repository
  • Vulnerabilities include both CVSS v2 and v3 vectors when available from GitHub's advisory database

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The GitHub Secret Scanning Agent is an official Faraday agent that automatically imports exposed secrets detected by GitHub into your Faraday workspace. This allows security teams to centralize and track leaked credentials alongside the rest of their vulnerabilities, without requiring manual checks inside GitHub.

This agent does not perform the secret scan itself. Instead, it retrieves results from GitHub’s built-in Secret Scanning feature and converts them into Faraday-compatible vulnerabilities.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
When setting up the agent, you must provide the following parameters:

  • GITHUB_TOKEN: The authentication token
  • GITHUB_OWNER: Organization or user that owns the repository

When running the Agent, you must provide the following parameters:

  • GITHUB_REPOSITORY: Repository name to retrieve data from

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: The agent only imports open GitHub Secret Scanning alerts, since resolved or revoked findings no longer represent an active exposure. Because GitHub does not provide severity values, all imported secrets are assigned High severity. Each vulnerability is tagged with secret_detection, along with any additional tags defined by the user.

docs.title: GitHub Secret Scanning Executor
docs.text: Category: Source Code Analysis Script: github_secrets.py Integration: GitHub REST API Faraday Plugin: Raw JSON (no plugin — generates Faraday JSON directly)

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Fetches all open secret-scanning alerts from a GitHub repository and creates vulnerability entries for each detected secret. All secrets are assigned high severity since GitHub does not provide native severity ratings for secrets.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• GitHub Personal Access Token with security_events scope
  • GitHub Advanced Security (GHAS) enabled on the repository
  • Secret scanning enabled in the repository settings

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: Variable Required Description GITHUB_TOKEN Yes GitHub Personal Access Token GITHUB_OWNER Yes GitHub organization or user owning the repository

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description GITHUB_REPOSITORY string Yes Repository name to query for secret scanning alerts

docs.title: Common Agent Parameters Supported
docs.text: AGENT_CONFIG_VULN_TAG, AGENT_CONFIG_HOSTNAME_TAG

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  github_secrets_scan:
    repo_executor: github_secrets.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs:
      GITHUB_TOKEN: "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"
      GITHUB_OWNER: my-org
    params:
      GITHUB_REPOSITORY:
        mandatory: true
        type: string
        base: string

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Fetches all open secret-scanning alerts from https://api.github.com/repos/{owner}/{repo}/secret-scanning/alerts (paginated)
  2. For each alert, creates a vulnerability entry with:
  3. Secret type and provider
  4. High severity (default for all secrets)
  5. Auto-tag: secret_detection
  6. Host is set to github.com/{owner}/{repo}
  7. Outputs the combined Faraday JSON

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Only open alerts are imported; resolved alerts are excluded
  • All secrets are assigned high severity since GitHub provides no native severity for secrets
  • Results are automatically tagged with secret_detection plus any user-defined tags
  • This executor does not perform independent scanning — it imports results from GitHub's native Secret Scanning
  • Secret scanning is free for public repositories; GitHub Advanced Security is required for private repositories

docs.title: GVM/OpenVAS Executor
docs.text: Category: Network and Vulnerability Scanners Script: gvm_openvas.py Integration: python-gvm SDK Faraday Plugin: OpenvasPlugin Website: https://www.greenbone.net/

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Connects to a Greenbone Vulnerability Management (GVM) / OpenVAS instance via the python-gvm library, creates a scan target and task, executes the scan, waits for completion, exports the XML report, and parses it into Faraday.

This is the modern GVM executor. For legacy OpenVAS installations using the omp CLI, use the OpenVAS Legacy executor.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• GVM/OpenVAS installed and running (Greenbone Community Edition or Enterprise)
  • Python package: python-gvm
  • One of: Unix socket access, SSH access, or TLS access to the GVM daemon

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: Variable Required Description GVM_USER Yes GVM username GVM_PASSW Yes GVM password HOST Yes GVM host address PORT Yes GVM port number

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description SCAN_TARGET string Yes Target host or IP to scan CONNECTION_TYPE string Yes Connection method: socket, ssh, or tls SCAN_ID string No GVM scan configuration UUID (default: daba56c8-73ec-11df-a475-002264764cea — Full and Fast) PORT_LIST_ID string No Port list UUID (default: 33d0cd82-57c6-11e1-8ed1-406186ea4fc5 — All IANA assigned TCP) SOCKET_PATH string No Unix socket path (default: /var/run/gvmd/gvmd.sock). Required when CONNECTION_TYPE is socket SSH_USER string Conditional SSH username. Required when CONNECTION_TYPE is ssh SSH_PASSW password Conditional SSH password. Required when CONNECTION_TYPE is ssh TLS_CERTFILE_PATH string No TLS client certificate file path TLS_CAFILE_PATH string No TLS CA certificate file path TLS_KEYFILE_PATH string No TLS client key file path TLS_PKEY_PASSW password No TLS private key password

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  openvas_scan:
    repo_executor: gvm_openvas.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs:
      GVM_USER: admin
      GVM_PASSW: "${GVM_PASSWORD}"
      HOST: gvm.local
      PORT: "9390"
    params:
      SCAN_TARGET:
        mandatory: true
        type: string
        base: string
      CONNECTION_TYPE:
        mandatory: true
        type: string
        base: string

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Establishes a connection to GVM using the selected connection type (socket, SSH, or TLS)
  2. Authenticates with the GVM Management Protocol (GMP)
  3. Creates a target definition for the scan host
  4. Creates a scan task using the specified scan configuration and port list
  5. Starts the scan and polls for completion
  6. Exports the finished report in XML format
  7. Parses the XML through OpenvasPlugin and outputs Faraday JSON

docs.title: Common Scan Configuration UUIDs
docs.text: UUID Name daba56c8-73ec-11df-a475-002264764cea Full and Fast (default) 698f691e-7489-11df-9d8c-002264764cea Full and Fast Ultimate 708f25c4-7489-11df-8094-002264764cea Full and Very Deep 74db13d6-7489-11df-91b9-002264764cea Full and Very Deep Ultimate

docs.title: Exposing the gvmd Socket from Docker
docs.text: To allow the executor to communicate with gvmd, the gvmd.sock socket must be exposed from the Docker container to the host.

docs.title: 1. Stop the Current Deployment
docs.text:
First, stop and remove the running containers and volumes: 

docker compose -f docker-compose.yml down -v

This ensures the environment is recreated with the correct configuration.

docs.title: 2. Create the Socket Directory on the Host
docs.text:
Create a directory that will be used to bind the socket from the container to the host:

sudo mkdir -p /opt/gvm/run
sudo chown 1000:1000 /opt/gvm/run
The ownership must match the user inside the container (UID 1000) so the socket can be created and accessed properly.

docs.title: 3. Modify the docker-compose.yml
docs.text:
Open docker-compose.yml and locate the volumes section at the bottom of the file. You will see something similar to:

volumes:
  ...
  gvmd_socket_vol:
Replace it with the following configuration:

volumes:
  ...
  gvmd_socket_vol:
    driver: local
    driver_opts:
      type: none
      o: bind
      device: /opt/gvm/run
This binds the container socket directory to /opt/gvm/run on the host.

docs.title: 4. Start the Environment Again
docs.text:
After updating the configuration, start the stack: 

docker compose -f docker-compose.yml up -d
Wait until all containers are fully started.

docs.title: 5. Configure the Executor
docs.text:
Once the containers are running, the gvmd socket will be available on the host at: 

/opt/gvm/run/gvmd.sock
Provide this path to the executor so it can connect to gvmd.

docs.title: 6. Python Dependency
docs.text:
Make sure the Python client library is up to date: 

pip install -U python-gvm
The executor was tested with: 
python-gvm==26.9.0

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• The socket connection type is fastest and recommended when the dispatcher runs on the same host as GVM
  • For remote connections, ssh or tls are required
  • TLS parameters are only used when CONNECTION_TYPE is tls

docs.title: InsightVM / Nexpose Executor
docs.text: Category: Host and Application Scanners Script: insightvm.py Integration: REST API (InsightVM API v3) Faraday Plugin: NexposeFullPlugin Website: https://www.rapid7.com/products/insightvm/

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Connects to a Rapid7 InsightVM (Nexpose) instance via its REST API. Either launches a new scan on a site and waits for completion, or downloads an existing executive report. Generates an XML Export v2 report and parses it into Faraday.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Rapid7 InsightVM or Nexpose installed and accessible
  • Valid credentials with scan and report permissions

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: Variable Required Description INSIGHTVM_HOST Yes InsightVM server URL (e.g., https://insightvm.local:3780) INSIGHTVM_USR Yes InsightVM username INSIGHTVM_PASSWD Yes InsightVM password

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description SITE_ID integer Conditional Site ID to launch a scan on. Provide this OR EXECUTIVE_REPORT_ID EXECUTIVE_REPORT_ID string Conditional Existing executive report ID to download

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  insightvm_scan:
    repo_executor: insightvm.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs:
      INSIGHTVM_HOST: "https://insightvm.local:3780"
      INSIGHTVM_USR: "${INSIGHTVM_USER}"
      INSIGHTVM_PASSWD: "${INSIGHTVM_PASS}"
    params:
      SITE_ID:
        mandatory: false
        type: integer
        base: integer
      EXECUTIVE_REPORT_ID:
        mandatory: false
        type: string
        base: string

docs.title: Scan Mode (using SITE_ID)
docs.text:
1. Launches a new scan on the specified site via POST /api/3/sites/{id}/scans
  2. Polls scan status via GET /api/3/scans/{id} until completion
  3. Generates an XML Export v2 report for the scan results
  4. Downloads and parses the report through NexposeFullPlugin

docs.title: Report Mode (using EXECUTIVE_REPORT_ID)
docs.text:
1. Downloads the specified executive report via the InsightVM API
  2. Parses the report through NexposeFullPlugin

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• At least one of SITE_ID or EXECUTIVE_REPORT_ID must be provided
  • When using SITE_ID, a new scan is initiated each time the executor runs
  • Sites and their scan configurations must be pre-configured in the InsightVM console

docs.title: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Executor
docs.text: Category: Network and Vulnerability Scanners Script: microsoft_defender.py Integration: REST API (OAuth2 Client Credentials) Faraday Plugin: Raw JSON (no plugin — generates Faraday JSON directly) Website: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/business/endpoint-security/microsoft-defender-endpoint

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Authenticates with Microsoft Defender for Endpoint via OAuth2 (client credentials flow), retrieves all managed machines, filters by a "last seen" time threshold, fetches vulnerabilities for each machine, and constructs Faraday JSON with CVE and CVSS v3 data.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Microsoft Defender for Endpoint license (E5 or standalone)
  • Azure AD application registration with the following API permissions:
  • Machine.Read.All — Read machine data
  • Vulnerability.Read.All — Read vulnerability data
  • Azure AD Tenant ID, Client ID, and Client Secret

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: Variable Required Description TENANT_ID Yes Azure Active Directory Tenant ID CLIENT_ID Yes Azure AD Application (Client) ID CLIENT_SECRET Yes Azure AD Application Client Secret

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description DAYS_OLD string Yes Only include machines seen within this many days (integer > 0)

docs.title: Common Agent Parameters Supported
docs.text: AGENT_CONFIG_VULN_TAG, AGENT_CONFIG_HOSTNAME_TAG

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  defender_scan:
    repo_executor: microsoft_defender.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs:
      TENANT_ID: "${AZURE_TENANT_ID}"
      CLIENT_ID: "${AZURE_CLIENT_ID}"
      CLIENT_SECRET: "${AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET}"
    params:
      DAYS_OLD:
        mandatory: true
        type: string
        base: string

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Authenticates via OAuth2 client credentials at https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant_id}/oauth2/v2.0/token
  2. Retrieves all machines from https://api.security.microsoft.com/api/machines
  3. Filters machines by lastSeen date, keeping only those seen within the DAYS_OLD threshold
  4. For each qualifying machine, fetches vulnerabilities from https://api.security.microsoft.com/api/machines/{id}/vulnerabilities
  5. Constructs Faraday JSON with host details (computer name, OS, IP) and vulnerability data (CVE ID, CVSS v3 score, description)

docs.title: Setting Up Azure AD Permissions
docs.text:
1. Go to Azure Portal > App registrations > New registration
  2. Create an application and note the Client ID and Tenant ID
  3. Under Certificates & secrets, create a new client secret
  4. Under API permissions, add:
  5. Microsoft Threat Protection > Application > Machine.Read.All
  6. Microsoft Threat Protection > Application > Vulnerability.Read.All
  7. Click Grant admin consent for your organization

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• The executor uses rate limiting (40 requests/minute) via requests_ratelimiter to comply with Microsoft API limits
  • DAYS_OLD controls the recency filter — set to 7 for weekly imports, 30 for monthly
  • Only machines with a lastSeen timestamp within the threshold are processed

docs.title: Nessus Executor
docs.text: Category: Network and Vulnerability Scanners Script: nessus.py Integration: REST API Faraday Plugin: NessusPlugin Website: https://www.tenable.com/products/nessus

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Connects to a Tenable Nessus Professional instance via its REST API, creates or locates an existing scan by name, launches it, waits for completion, exports the results in .nessus format, and parses them into Faraday.

This executor is designed for on-premise Nessus Professional installations. For Tenable's cloud platform, use the TenableIO executor instead.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Tenable Nessus Professional installed and accessible over the network
  • Valid Nessus user credentials
  • Network access to the Nessus port (default: 8834)

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: Variable Required Description NESSUS_USERNAME Yes Nessus account username NESSUS_PASSWORD Yes Nessus account password NESSUS_URL Yes Nessus server URL (e.g., https://nessus.local:8834)

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description NESSUS_SCAN_TARGET string Yes Target IPs, domains, or ranges (e.g., 192.168.1.0/24) NESSUS_SCAN_NAME string No Scan display name. If a scan with this name already exists, it will be relaunched NESSUS_SCAN_TEMPLATE string No Nessus scan template name (default: "basic") NESSUS_URL url No Override the setup URL for this execution

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  nessus_scan:
    repo_executor: nessus.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs:
      NESSUS_USERNAME: admin
      NESSUS_PASSWORD: "${NESSUS_PASS}"
      NESSUS_URL: "https://nessus.local:8834"
    params:
      NESSUS_SCAN_TARGET:
        mandatory: true
        type: string
        base: string
      NESSUS_SCAN_NAME:
        mandatory: false
        type: string
        base: string
      NESSUS_SCAN_TEMPLATE:
        mandatory: false
        type: string
        base: string

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Authenticates with the Nessus API using username/password to obtain a session token
  2. Extracts the X-API-Token from the Nessus JavaScript bundle (nessus6.js)
  3. Searches for an existing scan matching NESSUS_SCAN_NAME
  4. If no matching scan is found, creates a new scan with the specified target and template
  5. Launches the scan and polls for completion
  6. Exports results in .nessus (XML) format
  7. Parses the export through NessusPlugin and outputs Faraday JSON

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
• Self-signed SSL certificates: Nessus uses self-signed certs by default. The executor disables SSL verification (urllib3.disable_warnings)
  • Scan templates: Template names must match exactly as they appear in the Nessus UI (e.g., "basic", "advanced", "discovery")
  • Existing scans: If NESSUS_SCAN_NAME matches an existing scan, that scan is relaunched with its original configuration

docs.title: Nikto Executor
docs.text: Category: Web Application Scanners Script: nikto2.py Integration: CLI Faraday Plugin: NiktoPlugin Website: https://cirt.net/Nikto2

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Runs the Nikto web server scanner against a target URL, producing XML output that is parsed into Faraday. Nikto checks for dangerous files, outdated server software, version-specific vulnerabilities, and server configuration issues.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Nikto installed and available in $PATH
  • Verify: nikto -Version

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: (none required)

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description TARGET_URL url Yes Target URL to scan (e.g., https://example.com)

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  nikto_scan:
    repo_executor: nikto2.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs: {}
    params:
      TARGET_URL:
        mandatory: true
        type: url
        base: string

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Runs nikto -h <url> -o <output.xml> to scan the target
  2. Parses the XML output through NiktoPlugin and outputs Faraday JSON

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Nikto performs comprehensive web server checks but can be noisy; consider running during authorized testing windows
  • The executor has minimal configuration — only the target URL is needed

docs.title: Nmap Executor
docs.text: Category: Network and Vulnerability Scanners Script: nmap.py Integration: CLI Faraday Plugin: NmapPlugin Website: https://nmap.org/

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Runs an Nmap network scan against one or more targets with configurable port ranges, scripts, and timing options. Results are output in XML format and parsed by the Faraday Nmap plugin.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Nmap installed and available in $PATH
  • Verify: nmap --version

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: (none required)

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description TARGET list Yes JSON array of target IPs, domains, or CIDR ranges (e.g., ["192.168.1.0/24", "example.com"]) PORT_LIST list No JSON array of ports (e.g., ["80", "443", "8080"]) TOP_PORTS string No Number of top ports to scan (e.g., "100") OPTION_SC boolean No Enable -sC (default NSE scripts) OPTION_SV boolean No Enable -sV (service version detection) OPTION_PN boolean No Enable -Pn (skip host discovery, treat all hosts as online) SCRIPT_TIMEOUT integer No Timeout for NSE scripts (seconds) HOST_TIMEOUT integer No Timeout per host (seconds) SCRIPT_CMD string No Custom --script argument (e.g., "vuln,exploit")

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  nmap_scan:
    repo_executor: nmap.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs: {}
    params:
      TARGET:
        mandatory: true
        type: list
        base: list
      PORT_LIST:
        mandatory: false
        type: list
        base: list
      OPTION_SV:
        mandatory: false
        type: boolean
        base: boolean
      OPTION_SC:
        mandatory: false
        type: boolean
        base: boolean

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Reads the TARGET list and builds an nmap command line
  2. Appends optional flags (-sC, -sV, -Pn) if enabled
  3. Adds port specifications from PORT_LIST or TOP_PORTS
  4. Runs nmap [options] -oX - -- <targets> (XML output to stdout)
  5. Passes XML output through NmapPlugin to generate Faraday entities

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• PORT_LIST and TOP_PORTS are mutually exclusive; if both are provided, PORT_LIST takes precedence
  • Targets are passed after -- to prevent them from being interpreted as Nmap options
  • Requires sufficient OS privileges for certain scan types (e.g., SYN scan requires root)

docs.title: Nuclei Executor
docs.text: Category: Network and Vulnerability Scanners Script: nuclei.py Integration: CLI Faraday Plugin: NucleiPlugin Website: https://nuclei.projectdiscovery.io/

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Runs ProjectDiscovery's Nuclei template-based vulnerability scanner against one or more targets. Nuclei uses YAML templates to detect security issues across web applications, APIs, networks, and cloud infrastructure.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Nuclei installed and available in $PATH
  • Nuclei templates directory available locally
  • Verify: nuclei --version

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: Variable Required Description NUCLEI_TEMPLATES Yes Path to the Nuclei templates directory (e.g., /root/nuclei-templates)

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description NUCLEI_TARGET list Yes Comma-separated list of target URLs or IPs NUCLEI_EXCLUDE list No Comma-separated list of template paths to exclude (relative to the templates directory)

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  nuclei_scan:
    repo_executor: nuclei.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs:
      NUCLEI_TEMPLATES: /root/nuclei-templates
    params:
      NUCLEI_TARGET:
        mandatory: true
        type: list
        base: list
      NUCLEI_EXCLUDE:
        mandatory: false
        type: list
        base: list

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Reads the target list from NUCLEI_TARGET
  2. For a single target, runs: nuclei -target <url> -t <templates_path> -j -o <output.json>
  3. For multiple targets, writes them to a file and runs: nuclei -l <urls_file> -t <templates_path> -j -o <output.json>
  4. Applies template exclusions if NUCLEI_EXCLUDE is specified
  5. Parses the JSON output through NucleiPlugin and outputs Faraday JSON

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• The -j flag enables JSON output, which is required for the Faraday plugin to parse results
  • Template exclusions are relative paths within the templates directory (e.g., cves/2020/)
  • Keep templates updated with nuclei -update-templates for best coverage

docs.title: OpenVAS Legacy Executor
docs.text: Category: Network and Vulnerability Scanners Script: openvas_legacy.py Integration: omp CLI Faraday Plugin: OpenvasPlugin

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Uses the legacy omp (OpenVAS Management Protocol) command-line tool to create a scan target and task, execute the scan, wait for completion, and retrieve the XML report. This executor is intended for older OpenVAS installations that do not support the modern GVM API.

For modern GVM installations, use the GVM/OpenVAS executor instead.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• OpenVAS installed with the omp CLI tool available in $PATH
  • Valid OpenVAS credentials

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: (none — credentials are passed as execution arguments)

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description OPENVAS_USER string Yes OpenVAS username OPENVAS_PASSW password Yes OpenVAS password OPENVAS_HOST ip Yes OpenVAS server IP address OPENVAS_PORT integer Yes OpenVAS port (typically 9390) OPENVAS_SCAN_URL url Yes Target host or IP to scan OPENVAS_SCAN_ID string No Scan configuration UUID (default: daba56c8-73ec-11df-a475-002264764cea — Full and Fast)

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  openvas_legacy_scan:
    repo_executor: openvas_legacy.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs: {}
    params:
      OPENVAS_USER:
        mandatory: true
        type: string
        base: string
      OPENVAS_PASSW:
        mandatory: true
        type: password
        base: string
      OPENVAS_HOST:
        mandatory: true
        type: ip
        base: string
      OPENVAS_PORT:
        mandatory: true
        type: integer
        base: integer
      OPENVAS_SCAN_URL:
        mandatory: true
        type: url
        base: string

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Runs omp commands to create a target definition
  2. Creates a scan task referencing the target and scan configuration
  3. Starts the scan via omp
  4. Polls scan status until completion
  5. Retrieves the report in XML format
  6. Parses the XML through OpenvasPlugin and outputs Faraday JSON

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• The omp CLI is deprecated in modern Greenbone releases; migrate to the GVM executor when possible
  • All parameters (including credentials) are passed as execution arguments, not environment variables

docs.title: QualysGuard Executor
docs.text: Category: Web Application Scanners Script: qualys.py Integration: REST API Faraday Plugin: QualysguardPlugin Website: https://www.qualys.com/

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Authenticates with the QualysGuard API, registers the target IP if it does not already exist, launches a vulnerability scan using a specified option profile, waits for completion, downloads the scan report, and parses it into Faraday.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• QualysGuard account with scan permissions
  • A pre-configured scan option profile in QualysGuard

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: Variable Required Description QUALYS_USERNAME Yes QualysGuard username QUALYS_PASSWORD Yes QualysGuard password

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description TARGET_IP string Yes Target IP address to scan OPTION_PROFILE string Yes Scan option profile name or numeric ID PULL_INTERVAL integer No Polling interval in seconds (default: 180)

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  qualys_scan:
    repo_executor: qualys.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs:
      QUALYS_USERNAME: "${QUALYS_USER}"
      QUALYS_PASSWORD: "${QUALYS_PASS}"
    params:
      TARGET_IP:
        mandatory: true
        type: string
        base: string
      OPTION_PROFILE:
        mandatory: true
        type: string
        base: string
      PULL_INTERVAL:
        mandatory: false
        type: integer
        base: integer

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Authenticates with the QualysGuard API at https://qualysguard.qg4.apps.qualys.com
  2. Checks if the target IP exists in the asset inventory; adds it if not
  3. Launches a vulnerability scan using the specified option profile
  4. Polls scan status at the configured interval until completion
  5. Downloads the scan report
  6. Parses the report through QualysguardPlugin and outputs Faraday JSON

docs.title: Creating a Scan Option Profile
docs.text:
1. In QualysGuard VMDR, navigate to Scans > Option Profiles
  2. Click New > Option Profile
  3. Configure the scan settings (ports, authentication, performance)
  4. Note the profile name or numeric ID for use with this executor

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• The API base URL is hardcoded to https://qualysguard.qg4.apps.qualys.com; if your Qualys platform uses a different URL (e.g., qg2, qg3), the executor source must be modified
  • The PULL_INTERVAL default of 180 seconds (3 minutes) is conservative; reduce for faster feedback on shorter scans
  • The executor targets a single IP per execution; for multiple targets, run multiple executor instances

docs.title: Report Processor Executor
docs.text: Category: Generic Script: report_processor.py Integration: File import Faraday Plugin: Auto-detected (via PluginsManager / ReportAnalyzer)

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
A generic executor that imports pre-existing security scan report files into Faraday. It reads a report file from a specified directory, auto-detects the tool that generated it (or accepts a manual tool specification), and uses the appropriate Faraday plugin to parse it.

This executor is useful for importing reports from tools that don't have a dedicated executor, or for processing reports generated outside of the agent workflow.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• The report file must already exist in the specified reports directory
  • The faraday_plugins package must support the report format

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: Variable Required Description REPORTS_PATH Yes Directory path containing report files

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description REPORT_NAME string Yes Filename of the report to import CONFIG_TOOL string No Tool name to force a specific parser. If not set, the tool is auto-detected

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  report_import:
    repo_executor: report_processor.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs:
      REPORTS_PATH: /opt/scan-reports
    params:
      REPORT_NAME:
        mandatory: true
        type: string
        base: string
      CONFIG_TOOL:
        mandatory: false
        type: string
        base: string

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Reads the report file from {REPORTS_PATH}/{REPORT_NAME}
  2. If CONFIG_TOOL is specified, loads the corresponding Faraday plugin by name
  3. If CONFIG_TOOL is not specified, uses ReportAnalyzer to auto-detect the tool from the file content
  4. Parses the report using the selected plugin
  5. Outputs Faraday JSON

docs.title: Supported Report Formats
docs.text:
Any format supported by faraday_plugins can be imported, including:

  • Nessus (.nessus), Nmap (.xml), Burp (.xml), ZAP (.xml)
  • Nuclei (.json), WPScan (.json), Nikto (.xml)
  • OpenVAS (.xml), Qualys (.xml), Nexpose (.xml)
  • And many more (see the [[plugin-list]] for the full list of supported tools)

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Auto-detection works by analyzing file content, not file extensions
  • Use CONFIG_TOOL when auto-detection fails or when you want to force a specific parser
  • This executor is commonly used with Docker deployments where the dispatcher mounts a reports volume

docs.title: Shodan Executor
docs.text: Category: Network Reconnaissance Script: shodan2.py Integration: CLI (shodan command) Faraday Plugin: ShodanPlugin Website: https://www.shodan.io/

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Runs the Shodan CLI to download search results as a compressed JSON file, decompresses it, and parses the results into Faraday. Shodan provides information about internet-connected devices, open ports, services, and vulnerabilities.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Shodan CLI installed (pip install shodan)
  • Shodan API key configured (shodan init <api-key>)
  • Verify: shodan info

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: (none required — Shodan uses its own API key configuration)

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description SHODAN_QUERY string Yes Shodan search query (e.g., "org:\"My Company\"", "port:443 country:US")

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  shodan_scan:
    repo_executor: shodan2.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs: {}
    params:
      SHODAN_QUERY:
        mandatory: true
        type: string
        base: string

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Runs shodan download <output.json.gz> "<query>"
  2. Decompresses the gzipped JSON file
  3. Parses the JSON results through ShodanPlugin and outputs Faraday JSON

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• The Shodan API key must be configured separately via shodan init <api-key> before using this executor
  • Shodan query syntax supports filters like port:, country:, org:, hostname:, os:, etc.
  • Results depend on Shodan's existing scan data — this executor does not perform active scanning
  • API credit usage depends on the query scope; large queries may consume significant credits

docs.title: SonarQube Executor
docs.text: Category: Source Code Analysis Script: sonarqube.py Integration: REST API Faraday Plugin: SonarQubeAPIPlugin Website: https://www.sonarsource.com/products/sonarqube/

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Connects to a SonarQube instance via its REST API to fetch all security-related issues for a project. Optionally retrieves security hotspots. Results are parsed through the Faraday SonarQube API plugin.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• SonarQube server running and accessible
  • Authentication token (generated in SonarQube > Account > Security > Generate Tokens)
  • At least one analyzed project in SonarQube

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: Variable Required Description SONAR_URL Yes SonarQube server URL (e.g., http://sonarqube.local:9000)

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description TOKEN password Yes SonarQube authentication token COMPONENT_KEY string No Project/component key to filter results (e.g., my-project) GET_HOTSPOT boolean No If true, also fetches security hotspots (default: false)

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  sonarqube_scan:
    repo_executor: sonarqube.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs:
      SONAR_URL: "http://sonarqube.local:9000"
    params:
      TOKEN:
        mandatory: true
        type: password
        base: string
      COMPONENT_KEY:
        mandatory: false
        type: string
        base: string
      GET_HOTSPOT:
        mandatory: false
        type: boolean
        base: boolean

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Connects to the SonarQube API at the configured URL
  2. Fetches security issues from /api/issues/search (paginated, filtered by SECURITY impact)
  3. If GET_HOTSPOT is enabled, also fetches security hotspots from /api/hotspots/search and enriches each with details from /api/hotspots/show
  4. Parses all results through SonarQubeAPIPlugin and outputs Faraday JSON

docs.title: Setting Up SonarQube
docs.text:
1. Start SonarQube (e.g., docker run -p 9000:9000 sonarqube:latest)
  2. Log in with default credentials (admin/admin) and change the password
  3. Create a project and note the project key
  4. Generate an authentication token under Account > Security > Generate Tokens
  5. Run your first analysis using sonar-scanner: 
    docker run --rm \
  -v "$(pwd):/usr/src" \
  sonarsource/sonar-scanner-cli \
  -Dsonar.projectKey=my-project \
  -Dsonar.sources=. \
  -Dsonar.host.url=http://host.docker.internal:9000 \
  -Dsonar.token=<your-token>

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• The executor imports results from existing SonarQube analyses — it does not trigger new scans
  • Security hotspots require an additional API call per hotspot for full details, which can be slow for large projects
  • The COMPONENT_KEY parameter filters to a specific project; without it, all accessible projects' issues are returned

docs.title: Sublist3r Executor
docs.text: Category: Network Reconnaissance Script: sublist3r.sh (Shell script) Integration: CLI (bash) Faraday Plugin: Raw JSON (no plugin — generates Faraday JSON via jq)

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Runs Sublist3r to enumerate subdomains of a target domain, resolves each discovered subdomain to its IP address using getent hosts, and outputs Faraday JSON with the discovered hosts.

This is the only shell-script executor in the official collection.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Sublist3r installed (sublist3r.py available in $PATH or /usr/bin)
  • jq installed for JSON construction
  • getent available (standard on Linux)

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: (none required)

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description DOMAIN string Yes Target domain for subdomain enumeration (e.g., example.com)

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  subdomain_enum:
    repo_executor: sublist3r.sh
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs: {}
    params:
      DOMAIN:
        mandatory: true
        type: string
        base: string

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Runs sublist3r.py -d <domain> -o <output_file>
  2. Reads each discovered subdomain from the output file
  3. For each subdomain, runs getent hosts <subdomain> to resolve the IP address
  4. Uses jq to construct Faraday JSON with host entries (IP + hostname)
  5. Outputs the combined JSON

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Sublist3r uses passive sources (search engines, DNS databases) — no active scanning of targets
  • getent hosts may fail for subdomains that don't resolve; these are skipped
  • The jq dependency is essential for JSON output construction

docs.title: Tenable.io Executor
docs.text: Category: Network and Vulnerability Scanners Script: tenableio.py Integration: pyTenable SDK (tenable.io) Faraday Plugin: NessusPlugin Website: https://www.tenable.com/products/tenable-io

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Connects to the Tenable.io cloud platform via the pyTenable Python SDK to create, launch, or re-run vulnerability scans. Supports both built-in Tenable templates and user-defined scan policies. Exports results in Nessus format and parses them into Faraday.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Tenable.io account with API access
  • API access key and secret key (generated in Tenable.io > Settings > My Account > API Keys)
  • Python package: tenable

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: Variable Required Description TENABLE_ACCESS_KEY Yes Tenable.io API access key TENABLE_SECRET_KEY Yes Tenable.io API secret key TENABLE_PULL_INTERVAL No Polling interval in seconds (default: 30)

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description SCAN_NAME string No Scan display name (default: "faraday-scan") SCAN_ID string No Existing scan ID to relaunch or export results from SCAN_TARGETS list Conditional JSON array of target IPs or domains. Required for new scans with user-defined templates TEMPLATE_NAME string No Built-in template name (default: "agent_basic") RELAUNCH_SCAN boolean No If true, relaunches the scan specified by SCAN_ID (default: false) USE_USER_DEFINED_TEMPLATE boolean No If true, uses a user-defined policy template instead of built-in (default: false) AGENT_GROUP_NAME string Conditional Agent group name (required for agent-based scans)

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  tenableio_scan:
    repo_executor: tenableio.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs:
      TENABLE_ACCESS_KEY: "${TENABLE_ACCESS_KEY}"
      TENABLE_SECRET_KEY: "${TENABLE_SECRET_KEY}"
      TENABLE_PULL_INTERVAL: "30"
    params:
      SCAN_NAME:
        mandatory: false
        type: string
        base: string
      SCAN_TARGETS:
        mandatory: false
        type: list
        base: list
      TEMPLATE_NAME:
        mandatory: false
        type: string
        base: string

docs.title: 1. Create a New Scan with a Built-in Template
docs.text:
Set TEMPLATE_NAME to one of Tenable's 50+ built-in templates and provide SCAN_TARGETS:

Template Name Description agent_basic Basic agent scan (default) agent_advanced Advanced agent scan basic Basic network scan advanced Advanced network scan webapp Web application tests pci PCI quarterly external scan log4shell Log4Shell detection discovery Host discovery malware Malware scan

docs.title: 2. Create a Scan with a User-Defined Template
docs.text: Set USE_USER_DEFINED_TEMPLATE to true, provide TEMPLATE_NAME (the user-defined policy name), and SCAN_TARGETS.

docs.title: 3. Relaunch an Existing Scan
docs.text: Set SCAN_ID to the existing scan ID and RELAUNCH_SCAN to true. The executor will relaunch the scan and wait for completion.

docs.title: 4. Export Results from a Completed Scan
docs.text: Set SCAN_ID to the scan ID. Without RELAUNCH_SCAN, the executor exports the most recent results.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Authenticates with Tenable.io using the access/secret key pair
  2. Based on parameters, either creates a new scan, relaunches an existing one, or fetches results
  3. Polls the scan status at the configured interval until completion
  4. Exports results in Nessus format (.nessus)
  5. Parses the export through NessusPlugin and outputs Faraday JSON

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• API keys are generated per-user in Tenable.io under Settings > My Account > API Keys
  • The agent_basic and agent_advanced templates require Tenable Nessus Agents installed on targets
  • When using RELAUNCH_SCAN, the executor waits for the new scan to complete before exporting

docs.title: Tenable.sc Executor
docs.text: Category: Network and Vulnerability Scanners Script: tenablesc.py Integration: pyTenable SDK (tenable.sc) Faraday Plugin: NessusPlugin Website: https://www.tenable.com/products/tenable-sc

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Connects to an on-premise Tenable SecurityCenter (Tenable.sc) instance via the pyTenable SDK, retrieves completed scan results, exports them in Nessus format, and parses them into Faraday. Supports fetching specific scan IDs or all completed scans.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Tenable SecurityCenter installed and accessible
  • API access key and secret key (generated in Tenable.sc > Users > Select user > API Keys)
  • Python package: tenable

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: Variable Required Description TENABLE_HOST Yes Tenable.sc server hostname or IP TENABLE_ACCESS_KEY Yes API access key TENABLE_SECRET_KEY Yes API secret key

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description TENABLE_SCAN_ID list Conditional JSON array of scan instance IDs to export COMPLETED_SCANS boolean No If set, fetch all completed scan instances

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  tenablesc_import:
    repo_executor: tenablesc.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs:
      TENABLE_HOST: "sc.internal.local"
      TENABLE_ACCESS_KEY: "${TENABLE_ACCESS_KEY}"
      TENABLE_SECRET_KEY: "${TENABLE_SECRET_KEY}"
    params:
      TENABLE_SCAN_ID:
        mandatory: false
        type: list
        base: list
      COMPLETED_SCANS:
        mandatory: false
        type: boolean
        base: boolean

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Authenticates with Tenable.sc using API keys
  2. If TENABLE_SCAN_ID is provided, retrieves those specific scan instances
  3. If COMPLETED_SCANS is set, retrieves all completed scan instances
  4. Exports each scan result in Nessus format (ZIP archive containing .nessus files)
  5. Extracts and parses each .nessus file through NessusPlugin
  6. Outputs combined Faraday JSON

docs.title: Finding Scan IDs
docs.text: In Tenable.sc, navigate to Scans > Scan Results and view the scan details. The scan instance ID is visible in the URL or detail view.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• At least one of TENABLE_SCAN_ID or COMPLETED_SCANS must be provided
  • The executor does not launch new scans — it imports results from existing completed scans
  • API keys are generated per-user in the Tenable.sc administration interface

docs.title: w3af Executor
docs.text: Category: Web Application Scanners Script: w3af.py Integration: CLI (w3af_console) Faraday Plugin: W3afPlugin Website: http://w3af.org/

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Generates a w3af configuration script that enables all crawl, grep, audit, and bruteforce plugins, runs w3af_console in scripted mode against a target URL, and parses the XML output into Faraday.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• w3af installed locally
  • Python 2.7 (required by w3af itself)
  • w3af_console available in the w3af installation directory

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: Variable Required Description W3AF_PATH Yes Path to the w3af installation directory (containing w3af_console)

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description W3AF_TARGET_URL url Yes Target URL to scan

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  w3af_scan:
    repo_executor: w3af.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs:
      W3AF_PATH: /opt/w3af
    params:
      W3AF_TARGET_URL:
        mandatory: true
        type: url
        base: string

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Generates a comprehensive w3af configuration script enabling:
  2. All crawl plugins (except bing_spider, google_spider, spider_man)
  3. All grep plugins
  4. All audit plugins
  5. All bruteforce plugins
  6. Sets the target URL and XML output path in the script
  7. Runs python2.7 ./w3af_console -s <config_script> from within W3AF_PATH
  8. Parses the XML output through W3afPlugin and outputs Faraday JSON

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• w3af requires Python 2.7, which is end-of-life; consider using alternative scanners for new deployments
  • The scan profile is aggressive (all plugins enabled) and can be slow on large targets
  • The executor runs from within the W3AF_PATH directory, so the path must point to the w3af installation root

docs.title: WPScan Legacy Executor (Docker)
docs.text: Category: Web Application Scanners Script: wpscan_legacy.py Integration: CLI (Docker container) Faraday Plugin: WPScanPlugin

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Runs WPScan inside the official Docker container (wpscanteam/wpscan:latest) against a WordPress site with JSON output. This is the legacy version that uses Docker instead of a native WPScan installation.

For native WPScan execution with more options, use the WPScan executor.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Docker installed and running
  • Network access to Docker Hub (to pull wpscanteam/wpscan:latest)

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: (none required)

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description WPSCAN_TARGET_URL url Yes WordPress site URL to scan

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  wpscan_docker:
    repo_executor: wpscan_legacy.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs: {}
    params:
      WPSCAN_TARGET_URL:
        mandatory: true
        type: url
        base: string

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Creates a temporary directory for output
  2. Runs: docker run --rm --mount type=bind,source=<tmpdir>,target=/output wpscanteam/wpscan:latest -o /output/wpscan-output.json --url <target> -f json
  3. Reads the JSON output from the mounted volume
  4. Parses it through WPScanPlugin and outputs Faraday JSON

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• This executor does not support an API token; use the native WPScan executor for API token support
  • Docker must have network access to both Docker Hub (for image pull) and the target WordPress site
  • The Docker image is pulled at each execution unless already cached locally

docs.title: WPScan Executor
docs.text: Category: Web Application Scanners Script: wpscan.py Integration: CLI (native installation) Faraday Plugin: WPScanPlugin Website: https://wpscan.com/

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Runs the WPScan command-line tool natively against a WordPress site, producing JSON output that is parsed into Faraday. WPScan detects known vulnerabilities in WordPress core, plugins, and themes.

For Docker-based WPScan execution, use the WPScan Legacy executor.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• WPScan installed (Ruby gem: gem install wpscan)
  • Verify: wpscan --version

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: (none required)

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description WPSCAN_TARGET_URL url Yes WordPress site URL to scan WPSCAN_API_TOKEN password No WPScan API token for vulnerability data enrichment (from wpscan.com) WPSCAN_RANDOM_USER_AGENT boolean No If set, uses a random user agent string

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  wpscan_scan:
    repo_executor: wpscan.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs: {}
    params:
      WPSCAN_TARGET_URL:
        mandatory: true
        type: url
        base: string
      WPSCAN_API_TOKEN:
        mandatory: false
        type: password
        base: string
      WPSCAN_RANDOM_USER_AGENT:
        mandatory: false
        type: boolean
        base: boolean

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Runs wpscan -o <output.json> --url <target> -f json [--api-token <token>] [--random_user_agent]
  2. Parses the JSON output through WPScanPlugin and outputs Faraday JSON

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• The WPSCAN_API_TOKEN significantly improves results by adding vulnerability details from the WPScan.com database
  • Without an API token, WPScan still detects plugins, themes, and basic issues but without CVE cross-references
  • Free WPScan API tokens allow a limited number of daily requests

docs.title: OWASP ZAP Executor
docs.text: Category: Web Application Scanners Script: zap.py Integration: Python API (python-owasp-zap-v2.4) Faraday Plugin: ZapPlugin Website: https://www.zaproxy.org/

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Connects to a running OWASP ZAP instance via its Python API, initiates a spider scan against a target URL, waits for completion, generates an XML report, and parses it into Faraday.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• OWASP ZAP installed and running as a daemon or service
  • ZAP API key generated (ZAP > Tools > Options > API)
  • Python package: python-owasp-zap-v2.4
  • Python package: psutil

docs.title: Environment Variables (Setup)
docs.text: Variable Required Description ZAP_API_KEY Yes ZAP API authentication key

docs.title: Execution Arguments
docs.text: Parameter Type Mandatory Description TARGET_URL url Yes Target URL to scan (e.g., https://example.com)

docs.title: YAML Configuration Example
docs.text:
executors:
  zap_scan:
    repo_executor: zap.py
    max_size: 65536
    varenvs:
      ZAP_API_KEY: "${ZAP_API_KEY}"
    params:
      TARGET_URL:
        mandatory: true
        type: url
        base: string

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Connects to the ZAP API using zapv2.ZAPv2 with the provided API key
  2. Initiates a spider scan against the target URL
  3. Polls the spider status until scan progress reaches 100%
  4. Generates an XML report from the scan results
  5. Parses the XML through ZapPlugin and outputs Faraday JSON

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• ZAP must be running before the executor starts; the executor does not launch ZAP
  • The executor runs a spider scan (crawling + passive scanning); active scanning must be triggered separately in ZAP
  • ZAP typically listens on localhost:8080 by default
